text: file gives AI coding assistants fast orientation repository. Human contributors read vignettes/create-model-library.Rmd full conventions README project overview.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: nlmixr2lib model library nlmixr2. ships curated pharmacometric models (PK, PD, endogenous, therapeutic-area-specific) users can load name combine modify.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: inst/modeldb/ — every distributed model, organized category (specificDrugs/, endogenous/, pharmacokinetics/, pharmacodynamics/, therapeuticArea/). function name inside .R file must match filename. R/modeldb.R — registry-building machinery (buildModelDb(), readModelDb(), modellib()). vignettes/ — validation vignettes, one per published model. vignettes/create-model-library.Rmd — canonical naming conventions document.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: Repo-scoped skills live .claude/skills/: extract-literature-model — guided workflow extracting pharmacometric model scientific source (journal article, supplement, poster, regulatory document) package. Use user provides paper asks add model. See .claude/skills/extract-literature-model/SKILL.md. skill’s references/ folder contains templates standards enforces. authoritative covariate-column register inst/references/covariate-columns.md — consult introducing new covariate column. register installed package checkModelConventions() can parse runtime; update file (R code) ratifying new canonical covariate.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: full details see vignettes/create-model-library.Rmd .claude/skills/extract-literature-model/references/naming-conventions.md. Compartments: depot, central, peripheral1, peripheral2, effect. Observation: Cc. PK parameters (log-scale): lka, lcl, lvc, lvp, lq, lfdepot; derived ka, cl, vc, vp, q, kel, k12, etc. IIV: eta + transformed name (etalcl, etalvc). Use etalcl even paper used etacl. Residual error: propSd, addSd; multi-output prefixes output (CcpropSd). Covariate columns: canonical names inst/references/covariate-columns.md. Standardized choices include SEXF (1 = female), ADA_POS (1 = ADA-positive), RACE_<GROUP> prefix race indicators.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: Never push directly main. Always branch open PR. regenerating data/modeldb.rda / inst/modeldb.qs2, sync origin/main upstream additions aren’t clobbered. nlmixr2lib::buildModelDb() regenerates registry; commit regenerated artifacts alongside model file vignette.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: devtools::check() must pass. Model files validated via buildModelDb() (filename ↔︎ function-name match, parseable ini() / model()). Vignettes must build cleanly; use PKNCA (Suggests) NCA validation.

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical mechanistic DAR PK model (Bender 2014)
text: vignette exercises mechanistic model (Bender 2014 Table II), tracks drug--antibody-ratio moiety DAR0, DAR1, …, DAR7 individually. moiety three-compartment distribution shares central volume V1, peripheral volumes V2/V3, distributional clearances CLd2/CLd3 every DAR species. first-order deconjugation chain carries DAR_n toward DAR_(n-1) rates k_7->6 = k_6->5 = k_5->4 = k_4->3 = k_3->2 — single shared upper-chain rate estimated species (k_7->3 model file); k_2->1 k_1->0 — two separate first-order rate constants. See companion Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_reduced model / vignette simpler two-species (T-DM1 + DAR0) lumping (Bender 2014 Table III).

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical mechanistic DAR PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Cynomolgus monkey estimates populate default ini() block. rat parameter set documented model’s population$species_parameters metadata model-file comments.
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_mechanistic")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' str(mod$meta$population$species_parameters, max.level = 2) #> List of 2 #>  $ cynomolgus:List of 12 #>   ..$ source  : chr "Bender 2014 Table II, cynomolgus columns" #>   ..$ CL_TT   : chr "17.4 mL/day (IIV CV 24.8%)" #>   ..$ k_plasma: chr "0.0939 /day" #>   ..$ V1      : chr "148 mL (IIV CV 11.7%)" #>   ..$ CLd2    : chr "25.5 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V2      : chr "57.2 mL (IIV CV 46.8%)" #>   ..$ CLd3    : chr "81.2 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V3      : chr "127 mL (no IIV)" #>   ..$ k_7_to_3: chr "0.341 /day (no IIV)  [shared k_7->6 = k_6->5 = k_5->4 = k_4->3 = k_3->2]" #>   ..$ k_2_to_1: chr "0.255 /day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ k_1_to_0: chr "0.0939 /day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ res_err : chr "15.3% proportional" #>  $ rat       :List of 13 #>   ..$ source  : chr "Bender 2014 Table II, rat columns" #>   ..$ CL_TT   : chr "2.42 mL/day (IIV CV 24.0%)" #>   ..$ k_plasma: chr "0.156 /day" #>   ..$ V1      : chr "11.0 mL (IIV CV 18.5%)" #>   ..$ CLd2    : chr "49.0 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V2      : chr "3.44 mL (IIV CV 49.4%)" #>   ..$ CLd3    : chr "12.0 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V3      : chr "16.7 mL (IIV CV 16.8%)" #>   ..$ k_7_to_3: chr "0.543 /day (IIV CV 21.8%)" #>   ..$ k_2_to_1: chr "0.388 /day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ k_1_to_0: chr "0.114 /day (IIV CV 15.1%)" #>   ..$ res_err : chr "11.1% proportional" #>   ..$ notes   : chr "Rat fit has additional IIV terms on V3, k_7->3, and k_1->0 not active in the cynomolgus default. To switch to r"| __truncated__ str(mod$meta$population$dose_products, max.level = 2) #> List of 3 #>  $ note        : chr "Paper reports two preclinical dose products. The DAR-specific fractions below are seeded into the DAR0_central."| __truncated__ #>  $ T_DM1_DAR3_1:List of 8 #>   ..$ DAR7: num 0.02 #>   ..$ DAR6: num 0.05 #>   ..$ DAR5: num 0.1 #>   ..$ DAR4: num 0.19 #>   ..$ DAR3: num 0.26 #>   ..$ DAR2: num 0.23 #>   ..$ DAR1: num 0.13 #>   ..$ DAR0: num 0.02 #>  $ T_DM1_DAR1_5:List of 8 #>   ..$ DAR7: num 0 #>   ..$ DAR6: num 0 #>   ..$ DAR5: num 0.01 #>   ..$ DAR4: num 0.04 #>   ..$ DAR3: num 0.13 #>   ..$ DAR2: num 0.26 #>   ..$ DAR1: num 0.35 #>   ..$ DAR0: num 0.21

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical mechanistic DAR PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Every ini() value inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_mechanistic.R comes Bender 2014 (doi:10.1208/s12248-014-9618-3) Table II:

what: Cynomolgus 30 mg/kg IV T-DM1 DAR 3.1 replication
title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical mechanistic DAR PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Bender 2014 Figure 4 (cynomolgus panel) shows TT per-DAR concentration profiles single 30 mg/kg IV T-DM1 DAR 3.1 dose. Simulating mechanistic model requires seeding dar*_central compartment DAR-3.1 dose-product fraction multiplied total dose amount.   per-DAR profiles reproduce expected qualitative features paper: DAR7 DAR6 decline monotonically (higher species feed ); DAR3–DAR5 peak early decay; DAR1 DAR0 build time fed deconjugation higher moieties.
code: dose_mg <- 120  # 30 mg/kg * 4 kg body weight dar_fracs <- list(   dar7_central = 0.02, dar6_central = 0.05,   dar5_central = 0.10, dar4_central = 0.19,   dar3_central = 0.26, dar2_central = 0.23,   dar1_central = 0.13, dar0_central = 0.02 )  events <- rxode2::et() for (cmt in names(dar_fracs)) {   events <- rxode2::et(events,                        amt = dose_mg * dar_fracs[[cmt]],                        cmt = cmt, time = 0) } events <- rxode2::et(events, seq(0.05, 42, length.out = 150), cmt = "Cc")  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = events) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' sim_long <- as.data.frame(sim) |>   pivot_longer(c(Cc, Ctt), names_to = "analyte", values_to = "conc") |>   mutate(analyte = recode(analyte,                           Cc  = "T-DM1 (DAR1-7)",                           Ctt = "Total trastuzumab"))  ggplot(sim_long, aes(time, conc, colour = analyte)) +   geom_line(size = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.5, NA)) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Concentration (ug/mL)",        colour = NULL,        title  = "Cynomolgus 30 mg/kg IV T-DM1 DAR 3.1 (Bender 2014 mechanistic)",        caption = "Replicates the composite analytes in Bender 2014 Figure 4.") #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated. dar_cols <- paste0("Cdar", 0:7) sim_dar <- as.data.frame(sim) |>   select(time, all_of(dar_cols)) |>   pivot_longer(-time, names_to = "moiety", values_to = "conc") |>   mutate(moiety = factor(moiety, levels = dar_cols, labels = paste0("DAR", 0:7)))  ggplot(sim_dar, aes(time, conc, colour = moiety)) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.01, NA)) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Concentration (ug/mL)",        colour = "Moiety",        title  = "Per-DAR concentration profiles (cynomolgus 30 mg/kg DAR 3.1)",        caption = "Replicates the per-moiety panels in Bender 2014 Figure 4.") #> Warning: Removed 18 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`).

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical mechanistic DAR PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Cynomolgus body weight fixed 4 kg convert paper’s mg/kg doses absolute mg; model treats volumes absolute (mL paper, L ini() block). Residual error applied Cc (T-DM1) Ctt (TT) ; per-DAR concentrations exposed deterministic derived outputs (Cdar0 … Cdar7) VPC-style comparison. model’s k_plasma parameter retained future -vitro plasma stability simulations, even though -vivo ODE sum CL_in_vivo/V1 + k_plasma simplifies CL_TT/V1.

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Bender et al. (2014) developed two preclinical PK models trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1), antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) trastuzumab maytansine derivative DM1 linked via SMCC. dataset combined rat (n = 34) cynomolgus monkey (n = 18) PK, plus vitro plasma stability. vignette exercises reduced model (Bender 2014 Table III), collapses conjugated species single lumped “T-DM1” compartment tracks naked trastuzumab (DAR0) separately. T-DM1 deconjugates DAR0 via single first-order deconjugation clearance CL_DEC. forms share central / two peripheral volumes distributional clearances. See companion Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_mechanistic model / vignette full DAR0–DAR7 catenary chain (Bender 2014 Table II).

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: rat cynomolgus monkey parameter sets documented model’s population$species_parameters metadata. ini() block populated cynomolgus estimates (Table III, cyno column). rat alternates noted -file comments can applied updating fixed effects (converting published mL/day values L/day via /1000).
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_reduced")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' str(mod$meta$population$species_parameters, max.level = 2) #> List of 2 #>  $ cynomolgus:List of 9 #>   ..$ source : chr "Bender 2014 Table III, cynomolgus columns" #>   ..$ CL_TT  : chr "19.9 mL/day (IIV CV 19.8%)" #>   ..$ V1     : chr "154 mL (IIV CV 7.65%)" #>   ..$ CLd2   : chr "56.8 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V2     : chr "50.0 mL (no IIV)" #>   ..$ CLd3   : chr "60.4 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V3     : chr "84.7 mL (IIV CV 27.3%)" #>   ..$ CL_DEC : chr "22.0 mL/day (IIV CV 11.9%)" #>   ..$ res_err: chr "9.64% proportional" #>  $ rat       :List of 10 #>   ..$ source : chr "Bender 2014 Table III, rat columns" #>   ..$ CL_TT  : chr "2.37 mL/day (IIV CV 24.6%)" #>   ..$ V1     : chr "10.7 mL (IIV CV 19.8%)" #>   ..$ CLd2   : chr "59.7 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V2     : chr "2.52 mL (IIV CV 68.0%)" #>   ..$ CLd3   : chr "13.9 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V3     : chr "15.5 mL (IIV CV 16.5%)" #>   ..$ CL_DEC : chr "2.24 mL/day (IIV CV 15.3%)" #>   ..$ res_err: chr "10.9% proportional" #>   ..$ notes  : chr "Rat fit adds IIV on V2; to switch from the cynomolgus default to the rat parameter set, call ini() on the resul"| __truncated__

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Every ini() value inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_reduced.R comes Bender 2014 (doi:10.1208/s12248-014-9618-3): Values ini() block expressed L L/day (mL mL/day divided 1000) dose mg volume L give concentrations mg/L = ug/mL directly.

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Bender 2014 Figure 3 (bottom panel) displays total trastuzumab (TT) T-DM1 concentration–time profiles single 30 mg/kg IV T-DM1 DAR 3.1 dose four cynomolgus monkeys. Reference body weight cynomolgus monkeys ~4 kg, giving nominal 120 mg absolute dose.
code: dose_mg <- 120  # 30 mg/kg * 4 kg body weight events  <- rxode2::et(amt = dose_mg, cmt = "central", time = 0) |>   rxode2::et(seq(0.1, 42, length.out = 120), cmt = "Cc")  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = events) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp2', 'etalcldec' sim_long <- as.data.frame(sim) |>   pivot_longer(c(Cc, Ctt), names_to = "analyte", values_to = "conc") |>   mutate(analyte = recode(analyte,                           Cc  = "T-DM1",                           Ctt = "Total trastuzumab"))  ggplot(sim_long, aes(time, conc, colour = analyte)) +   geom_line(size = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.5, NA)) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Concentration (ug/mL)",        colour = NULL,        title  = "Cynomolgus monkey 30 mg/kg IV T-DM1 (Bender 2014 reduced)",        caption = "Typical-value replication of Bender 2014 Figure 3 (cynomolgus panel).") #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated.

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Switching ini() fixed effects Table III rat estimates two-step update: convert published mL mL/day L L/day (divide 1000), pass ini() model object. IIV V2 reported rat cyno default ini etalvp term, illustrate typical-value simulation .
code: mod_rat <- mod |>   ini(     lcl    = log(0.00237),     lvc    = log(0.0107),     lqd2   = log(0.0597),     lvp    = log(0.00252),     lqd3   = log(0.0139),     lvp2   = log(0.0155),     lcldec = log(0.00224),     CcpropSd  = 0.109,     CttpropSd = 0.109   ) #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lcl` to `-6.0448653238351` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lvc` to `-4.53751153751428` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lqd2` to `-2.81842325858358` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lvp` to `-5.98349637745881` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lqd3` to `-4.27586643884549` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lvp2` to `-4.16691525505694` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lcldec` to `-6.10127941311519` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `CcpropSd` to `0.109` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `CttpropSd` to `0.109`  rat_dose <- 3  # 10 mg/kg * 0.3 kg typical rat BW events_rat <- rxode2::et(amt = rat_dose, cmt = "central", time = 0) |>   rxode2::et(seq(0.05, 28, length.out = 120), cmt = "Cc")  sim_rat <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_rat |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = events_rat) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp2', 'etalcldec' sim_rat_long <- as.data.frame(sim_rat) |>   pivot_longer(c(Cc, Ctt), names_to = "analyte", values_to = "conc") |>   mutate(analyte = recode(analyte,                           Cc  = "T-DM1",                           Ctt = "Total trastuzumab"))  ggplot(sim_rat_long, aes(time, conc, colour = analyte)) +   geom_line(size = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.5, NA)) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Concentration (ug/mL)",        colour = NULL,        title  = "Rat 10 mg/kg IV T-DM1 (Bender 2014 reduced, rat parameter set)",        caption = "Typical-value replication of Bender 2014 Figure 2 (rat panel).")

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Bender 2014 Table III reports derived terminal half-lives total trastuzumab T-DM1 species. Computing simulated log-linear terminal phase provides end--end sanity check published values.
code: late <- as.data.frame(sim) |>   filter(time >= 25)  t12 <- function(t, c) log(2) / -coef(lm(log(c) ~ t))[2] data.frame(   analyte = c("T-DM1", "Total trastuzumab"),   simulated_t12_days = c(t12(late$time, late$Cc),                          t12(late$time, late$Ctt)),   paper_t12_days = c(5.21, 10.5) ) #>             analyte simulated_t12_days paper_t12_days #> 1             T-DM1           5.207294           5.21 #> 2 Total trastuzumab          10.465739          10.50

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Cynomolgus body weight fixed 4 kg rat body weight fixed 0.3 kg convert paper mg/kg doses absolute mg inputs; model scale body weight, matching paper’s absolute-volume parameterization. Residual error treated pure proportional linear space; Bender 2014 reports residual error additive log-scale, equivalent proportional nlmixr2’s linear space. BSV zeroed rxode2::zeroRe() typical-value replication; Table III IIV estimates remain available ini() stochastic simulation.

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Simulate tislelizumab (anti-PD-1 IgG4) concentration-time profiles using final three-compartment population PK model Budha et al. (2023). pooled analysis included 2596 patients advanced tumors 12 studies (14,473 serum concentration observations). structural model three-compartment IV model linear elimination. Final covariates: body weight, albumin, baseline tumor size, ADA status, tumor-type indicators classical Hodgkin lymphoma gastric cancer CL; body weight, age, sex Vc. Tislelizumab PK linear across 0.5–10 mg/kg dose range, approved dose 200 mg IV every 3 weeks.

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: See inst/references/covariate-columns.md canonical register.

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Approximate Budha 2023 Table 2 distributions pooled 2596-patient cohort (medians ranges — paper publish individual covariates covariate correlations).
code: set.seed(2023) n_subj <- 500  # Weight and age: log-normal / normal fits to reported medians and ranges WT  <- pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(65), 0.20), 31.9), 130) AGE <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 60, 12), 18), 90)  # Albumin (g/L): normal around median, clipped to observed range ALB <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 41, 5), 17), 61.3)  # Tumor size (mm): log-normal around median 63.3, clipped to observed range TUMSZ <- pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(63), 0.75), 10), 408)  # Sex: 26.0% female SEXF <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.26)  # ADA: 16.6% positive (of the 98.9% non-missing; treat missing as negative here) ADA_POS <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.166)  # Tumor type: sample from Table 2 proportions; only cHL and GC get indicators tumtp_p <- c(   cHL   = 0.027,   CRC   = 0.031,   EC    = 0.144,   GC    = 0.039,   HCC   = 0.122,   NPC   = 0.008,   NSCLC = 0.443,   OC    = 0.020,   UC    = 0.058,   Other = 0.108 ) TUMTP <- sample(names(tumtp_p), n_subj, replace = TRUE, prob = tumtp_p) TUMTP_CHL <- as.integer(TUMTP == "cHL") TUMTP_GC  <- as.integer(TUMTP == "GC")  pop <- data.frame(   ID        = seq_len(n_subj),   WT, AGE, ALB, TUMSZ,   SEXF, ADA_POS,   TUMTP, TUMTP_CHL, TUMTP_GC )

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Approved flat regimen: 200 mg IV q3w. Simulate 10 cycles (≈ 30 weeks) followed observation tail capture terminal disposition.
code: cycle_days <- 21 n_cycles   <- 10 dose_times <- seq(0, (n_cycles - 1) * cycle_days, by = cycle_days)  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 21, by = 0.5),                     # intensive profile through cycle 1   seq(21, n_cycles * cycle_days, by = 3),   # pre/post dose through multi-dose   seq(n_cycles * cycle_days, 365, by = 7)   # washout tail )))  d_dose <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(     AMT  = 200,          # flat 200 mg     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "central",     DUR  = 30 / (60 * 24), # 30-minute IV infusion in days     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(     AMT  = NA_real_,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DUR  = NA_real_,     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>   as.data.frame()

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Budha_2023_tislelizumab") set.seed(20230331) sim <- rxSolve(mod, d_sim, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Tislelizumab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Simulated tislelizumab PK, 200 mg IV q3w x 10 cycles",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500 virtual patients)",     caption = "Model: Budha et al. (2023) CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Effect of ADA status on PK
title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
code: sim_ada <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(ADA_label = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, ADA_label) |>   dplyr::summarise(median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  ggplot(sim_ada, aes(x = time / 7, y = median, color = ADA_label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("ADA-negative" = "steelblue", "ADA-positive" = "tomato")) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Tislelizumab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Tislelizumab median concentrations by ADA status",     subtitle = "ADA-positive patients have ~11.7% higher CL (exp(0.111) - 1)",     color = "Immunogenicity",     caption = "Model: Budha et al. (2023)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Run PKNCA first dosing interval (day 0–21) pseudo-steady-state interval (cycle 10, days 189–210), stratified ADA status. Terminal half-life expected approximate paper’s reported ≈ 23.8 days (Budha 2023 Methods) ADA-negative arm. Cycle 1 NCA summary (days 0-21), stratified ADA status Cycle 10 NCA summary (days 189-210, pseudo-steady state), stratified ADA status
code: # Per-subject treatment grouping for PKNCA pop$treatment <- ifelse(pop$ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")  # Cycle 1 interval conc1 <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= 0, time <= 21, Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::rename(ID = id) |>   dplyr::left_join(dplyr::select(pop, ID, treatment), by = "ID") |>   dplyr::transmute(ID, time_rel = time, Cc, treatment, interval = "Cycle 1")  dose1 <- pop |> knitr::kable(   summary(nca_results),   digits  = 2,   caption = "Cycle 1 NCA summary (days 0-21), stratified by ADA status" ) # Steady-state-ish interval at cycle 10 (day 189-210) conc_ss <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= 189, time <= 210, Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::rename(ID = id) |>   dplyr::left_join(dplyr::select(pop, ID, treatment), by = "ID") |>   dplyr::transmute(ID, time_rel = time - 189, Cc, treatment)  dose_ss <- pop |>   dplyr::transmute(ID, time_rel = 0, AMT = 200, treatment)  conc_obj_ss <- PKNCAconc(conc_ss, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj_ss <- PKNCAdose(dose_ss, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) nca_data_ss <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj_ss, dose_obj_ss,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 21,     cmax      = TRUE,     cmin      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE   ) ) nca_results_ss <- pk.nca(nca_data_ss) knitr::kable(   summary(nca_results_ss),   digits  = 2,   caption = "Cycle 10 NCA summary (days 189-210, pseudo-steady state), stratified by ADA status" )

what: Volume of distribution at steady state
title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: paper reports Vss = 6.42 L (Budha 2023 Results). nlmixr2 model, Vss simply sum central peripheral volumes typical covariates:
code: vss <- 3.05 + 1.27 + 2.10 cat(sprintf("Typical-subject Vss = %.2f L (Budha 2023 reports 6.42 L)\n", vss)) #> Typical-subject Vss = 6.42 L (Budha 2023 reports 6.42 L)

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Budha 2023 publish individual PK covariate tables, virtual population approximates Table 2 medians ranges: Weight: log-normal, median 65 kg, clipped 31.9–130 kg. Age: normal, median 60 y, clipped 18–90 y. Albumin: normal, median 41 g/L, clipped 17–61.3 g/L. paper uses g/L (SI), g/dL. Tumor size: log-normal, median 63.3 mm (matched Budha 2023 Table 2 median 63.3 mm), clipped 10–408 mm. Sex: 26% female (Budha 2023 Table 2). ADA status: 16.6% positive (Budha 2023 Table 2; 1.1% missing re-coded negative simulation). Tumor-type mix: sampled independently Table 2 proportions; cHL (2.7%) GC (3.9%) get indicator columns — types reference. covariate correlations imposed (paper report ). Dose regimen uses approved 200 mg q3w flat dose (Budha 2023 Methods label); model also valid 0.5–10 mg/kg per PopPK dataset. Infusion duration set 30 min (clinical administration); final PopPK model treats infusions bolus deriving concentration-time profiles study EDC.

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Structure: three-compartment IV linear elimination. Reference covariates: WT 65 kg, AGE 60 y, ALB 41 g/L, TUMSZ 63 mm, male, ADA-negative, non-cHL / non-GC tumor. Strongest covariates CL (Budha 2023 Figures 6–7, clinically meaningful): ±20% range WT (0.565 exponent) ALB (-0.457 exponent). cHL patients ~19% lower CL (exp(-0.216) = 0.806) reference tumor mix. Shrinkage V2 V3 IIV high (55.8%, 44.4%). paper retains etas; implementation follows paper.

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Budha N, Wu CY, Tang Z, et al. Model-based population pharmacokinetic analysis tislelizumab patients advanced tumors. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2023;12(1):95-109. doi:10.1002/psp4.12880

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Citation: Carlsson Petri KC, Hale PM, Hesse D, Rathor N, Mastrandrea LD. Liraglutide pharmacokinetics exposure-response adolescents obesity. Pediatric Obesity. 2021;16(10):e12799. doi:10.1111/ijpo.12799 Description: Liraglutide PK model adolescents (Carlsson Petri 2021) Article: Pediatric Obesity 2021;16(10):e12799 Open-access PMC copy: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8519033/

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: published analysis pooled 176 subjects across four Novo Nordisk trials liraglutide (Saxenda, 3.0 mg maintenance -daily subcutaneous): 121 adolescents phase 3a trial NN2211-4180 (primary population), 13 adolescents phase 1 trial NN2211-3967, 13 children NN2211-4181, 29 adults NN2211-3630 (Carlsson Petri 2021 Methods). adolescent phase 3a population (Table 1) 55.4% female (67/121), 84.3% White / 10.7% Black / 1.7% Asian / 3.3% , mean body weight 99.4 kg (SD 19.7, range 62.1-178.2 kg) mean age 14.6 years (SD 1.6, range 12-17). 107/121 adolescents reached 3.0 mg maintenance dose weekly escalation (0.6-1.2-1.8-2.4-3.0 mg). Age cutoffs used define CHILD / ADOLESCENT indicators children 7-11 y, adolescents 12-17 y, adults >=18 y. information available programmatically via readModelDb("CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide")$population.

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide.R. table collects review.

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Carlsson Petri 2021 Table 1 adolescent phase 3a (trial NN2211-4180) demographics: 55.4% female, body weight ~ Normal(99.4, 19.7) truncated reported 62.1-178.2 kg range, CHILD = 0 ADOLESCENT = 1. subjects receive 3.0 mg -daily SC maintenance dose (escalation, match 107/121 “maintenance” regimen). Liraglutide molar mass 3751.2 g/mol (C172H265N43O51); doses entered nmol simulated Cc = central / Vc directly nmol/L (matching paper’s reported concentration units). event table -daily SC dosing four weeks (sampling every hour last dosing interval capture steady-state shape) constructed .
code: set.seed(20210609) # publication date reference n_subj <- 400  # Liraglutide molar mass (g/mol). 3 mg = 3e-3 / 3751.2 mol = 7.997e-7 mol = 799.7 nmol. lira_mw <- 3751.2 dose_mg <- 3.0 dose_nmol <- dose_mg * 1e6 / lira_mw # convert mg to nmol: 1 mg = (1 / MW) * 1e6 nmol  cohort <- tibble(   id          = seq_len(n_subj),   SEXF        = as.integer(runif(n_subj) < 0.554),   WT          = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 99.4, sd = 19.7), 62.1), 178.2),   CHILD       = 0L,   ADOLESCENT  = 1L,   treatment   = factor("3 mg QD (adolescent)") ) sim_days <- 28 tau <- 24 # dosing interval (h) n_doses <- sim_days # one dose per day dose_times <- seq(0, by = tau, length.out = n_doses)  # Dense sampling on the final dosing interval (for Cmax / Tmax / AUCtau), # plus coarse sampling earlier (for visual steady-state approach). final_dose_time <- dose_times[n_doses] obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, final_dose_time, by = 6), # every 6 h across the run-in   final_dose_time + c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24) )))  dose_rows <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_nmol, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)  obs_rows <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, SEXF, WT, CHILD, ADOLESCENT, treatment) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod, events = events,   keep = c("SEXF", "WT", "CHILD", "ADOLESCENT", "treatment") )

what: Typical steady-state concentration profile over the last 24 h
title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Carlsson Petri 2021 publish concentration-time figure 3.0 mg adolescent dose, report exposure metric Cavg = dose / (CL/F * 24 h) steady state. deterministic (“typical”) profile zeros random effects median trajectory reference 100 kg female adolescent visible.
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cohort <- tibble(   id = 1,   SEXF = 1L, WT = 100, CHILD = 0L, ADOLESCENT = 1L,   treatment = factor("Typical adolescent, 100 kg female, 3 mg QD") )  typical_doses <- typical_cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_nmol, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)  typical_obs <- typical_cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = seq(0, final_dose_time + tau, by = 0.5)) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  typical_events <- dplyr::bind_rows(typical_doses, typical_obs) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, SEXF, WT, CHILD, ADOLESCENT, treatment) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = typical_events,   keep = c("SEXF", "WT", "CHILD", "ADOLESCENT", "treatment") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc'  sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   ggplot(aes(time / 24, Cc)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Liraglutide concentration (nmol/L)",     title = "Typical adolescent: 3 mg QD SC, 100 kg female",     caption = "Deterministic typical-value prediction.") +   theme_minimal()

what: VPC-style median and 5-95 percentiles over the 28-day run-in
title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: stochastic cohort summary (median 5th 95th percentiles across 400 simulated adolescents) replicates type summary shown paper’s Figure 3 (exposure metrics vs. body weight / age group).
code: sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time / 24, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Liraglutide concentration (nmol/L)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles across adolescent cohort (3 mg QD SC)",     caption = "Cohort covariates approximate Carlsson Petri 2021 Table 1 (trial NN2211-4180).")

what: Exposure vs. body weight at steady state
title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Carlsson Petri 2021 Figure 3A shows individual steady-state average concentration decreases increasing body weight (consistent positive allometric exponents CL V, greater effect CL). replicate relationship computing simulated subject’s Cavg final dosing interval.
code: cavg_by_id <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= final_dose_time, time <= final_dose_time + tau,     !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(id, WT, SEXF) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Cavg_nmol_L = mean(Cc),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   dplyr::mutate(Sex = ifelse(SEXF == 1L, "Female", "Male"))  ggplot(cavg_by_id, aes(WT, Cavg_nmol_L, colour = Sex)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +   labs(x = "Body weight (kg)", y = "C_avg,ss (nmol/L)",     title = "Steady-state Cavg vs. body weight",     caption = "Replicates the shape of Carlsson Petri 2021 Figure 3A (adolescents, 3 mg QD).") +   theme_minimal() #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Compute Cmax, Tmax, C_tau, C_avg, AUClast using PKNCA final dosing interval steady state (recipe 3). treatment grouping variable (treatment) placed id formula per project convention. Steady-state NCA final 24-h dosing interval (3 mg QD SC adolescent cohort).
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= final_dose_time, time <= final_dose_time + tau) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_rel = time - final_dose_time) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_rel, Cc, treatment)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1, time == final_dose_time) |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = tau,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) knitr::kable(summary(nca_res),   caption = "Steady-state NCA at the final 24-h dosing interval (3 mg QD SC adolescent cohort).")

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Carlsson Petri 2021 reports steady-state Cavg = dose / (CL/F * 24) individual (Figure 3A), tabulated population mean; however, reference female adult (CL/F typical = 1.01 L/h), predicted Cavg 3.0 mg dose : Cavg_ref = 3 mg / (1.01 L/h * 24 h) = 0.1238 mg/L = 33.0 nmol/L (liraglutide MW = 3751.2 g/mol) typical 100 kg female adolescent, model adds 1.06x age factor CL (adolescent/adult), typical CL = 1.01 * (100/100)^0.762 * 1.12^0 * 1.06 = 1.0706 L/h, giving predicted typical Cavg 3 / (1.0706 * 24) * 1e6 / 3751.2 = 31.1 nmol/L. Predicted steady-state Cavg 3 mg QD adolescent cohort.
code: typical_cl <- 1.01 * (100 / 100)^0.762 * 1.12^(1 - 1) * 1.06^1 # female (SEXF=1), 100 kg, ADOLESCENT typical_cavg_mg_L <- dose_mg / (typical_cl * 24) typical_cavg_nmol_L <- typical_cavg_mg_L * 1e6 / lira_mw  # Median simulated Cavg from the stochastic cohort sim_cavg <- cavg_by_id |>   dplyr::summarise(median_Cavg_nmol_L = median(Cavg_nmol_L),     q05 = quantile(Cavg_nmol_L, 0.05),     q95 = quantile(Cavg_nmol_L, 0.95))  compare_tbl <- tibble::tibble(   Source = c("Typical-value closed form",     "Simulated cohort (median)",     "Simulated cohort (90% range)"),   Cavg_nmol_L = c(sprintf("%.1f", typical_cavg_nmol_L),     sprintf("%.1f", sim_cavg$median_Cavg_nmol_L),     sprintf("%.1f - %.1f", sim_cavg$q05, sim_cavg$q95)) )  knitr::kable(compare_tbl,   caption = "Predicted steady-state Cavg for the 3 mg QD adolescent cohort.")

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: published analysis covariate SEXM (1 = male, 0 = female) translated canonical SEXF (1 = female). sex effect CL encoded e_sex_cl^(1 - SEXF) males retain 1.12x factor Table 3 females reference (factor = 1). previous implementation (1 - SEXF)^e_sex_cl bug: females (SEXF = 1), 0^1.12 = 0 zeroed CL. 100, correct transformation isomega^2 = log(1 + CV^2). fixed model file; see -file comment onetalcl/etalvc`. age-effect encoding previously CHILD^e_age_child_cl *   ADOLESCENT^e_age_adolescent_cl, evaluates 0^1.11 * 0^1.06 = 0 adults (indicators 0). corrected form e_age_child_cl^CHILD * e_age_adolescent_cl^ADOLESCENT, correctly evaluates 1 adults, 1.06 adolescents, 1.11 children. simulated cohort uses NN2211-4180 adolescent phase 3a demographics (weight, sex). Race/ethnicity model covariate therefore reproduced. simulated subjects “adolescents 3.0 mg maintenance” match dominant 107/121 regimen; weekly dose escalation reproduced paper’s steady-state exposure metric concerns maintenance dose. Liraglutide molar mass (3751.2 g/mol) used convert mg dose input nmol amount needed Cc nmol/L. source paper’s LLOQ 0.03 nmol/L. deterministic typical-value check agrees closed-form Cavg = dose / (CL/F * 24) within rounding; stochastic cohort median tracks typical-value prediction within ~10% across sampled weight distribution.

what: Mirikizumab population PK in Crohn’s disease (VIVID-1 phase 3)
title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: Replicate VIVID-1 final population PK model reported Chua et al. (2025) patients moderately--severely active Crohn’s disease. Mirikizumab humanized IgG4 monoclonal antibody binds p19 subunit IL-23. induction regimen 900 mg IV every 4 weeks three doses (weeks 0, 4, 8); maintenance regimen 300 mg SC every 4 weeks week 52. Citation: Chua L, Otani Y, Lin Z, Friedrich S, Durand F, Zhang XC. Mirikizumab Pharmacokinetics Exposure-Response Patients Moderately--Severely Active Crohn’s Disease: Results Two Randomized Studies. Clin Transl Sci. 2025;18(8):e70320. doi:10.1111/cts.70320 Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/cts.70320 paper reports two independent final popPK models — SERENITY (phase 2, n=186) VIVID-1 (phase 3, n=711) — different covariate structures. vignette replicates VIVID-1 , supports approved Crohn’s disease dose regimen model underlying published simulations (Figure S6). SERENITY reproduced package.

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: VIVID-1 (NCT03926130) global phase 3 treat-study (July 2019 October 2023) mirikizumab versus placebo ustekinumab moderately-- severely active Crohn’s disease. popPK dataset comprised 5,318 observations 711 patients (628 mirikizumab-treated induction plus 83 placebo non-responders crossed Week 12). Baseline demographics (Chua 2025 Table 1): mean weight 68.1 kg (range 21.1-154.0), mean age 36.3 years (range 18-74), 56.5% male, 70.7% White / 25.0% Asian / 4.2% ; mean baseline SES-CD 12.9 mean baseline CDAI 319. 53.4% previous biologic therapy 47.8% failed previous biologic therapy. demographics available programmatically via model’s metadata (accessible meta$population list model evaluated).

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Chua_2025_mirikizumab.R. rate parameters converted h^-1 (published) day^-1 (nlmixr2lib time convention) multiplying 24.

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: CRP BMI canonical entries inst/references/covariate-columns.md. hsCRP column (high-sensitivity assay used e.g. Thakre 2022) separate canonical column; conflate.

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: Original subject-level data VIVID-1 publicly available. virtual cohort uses covariate distributions approximating published Table 1 demographics. Covariates sampled independently (paper publish joint distributions) marginals centered scaled give simulated individual parameters bracket reference values.
code: set.seed(20250819) n_subj <- 300  cohort <- tibble(   ID  = seq_len(n_subj),   # Weight: mean 68.1 kg, range 21.1-154; lognormal covers the skew.   WT  = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(66), 0.27), 25), 150),   # Albumin: reference 44.57 g/L; VIVID-1 CD population often mildly low.   ALB = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 41, 4), 25), 52),   # CRP in CD skews right; reference 7.41 mg/L; log-normal shape.   CRP = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(6), 1.1), 0.3), 150),   # BMI: reference 24.75 kg/m^2; typical adult IBD distribution.   BMI = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 24.5, 4.5), 14), 45) )

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: Approved VIVID-1 regimen: 900 mg IV every 4 weeks 3 doses (weeks 0, 4, 8), 300 mg SC every 4 weeks week 12 week 52.
code: week <- 7 # days per week iv_days <- c(0, 4, 8) * week          # 0, 28, 56 sc_days <- seq(12, 48, by = 4) * week # 84, 112, ..., 336  # Observation grid: dense early, coarser in maintenance, plus steady-state cycle obs_days <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 84, by = 1),           # induction phase, daily   seq(85, 336, by = 3.5),       # maintenance, every half-week   seq(336, 364, by = 0.5)       # last SC interval, dense for Cmax/trough )))  iv_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = iv_days) |>   mutate(AMT = 900, EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_,          phase = "induction_IV")  sc_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = sc_days) |>   mutate(AMT = 300, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot",   DV = NA_real_,          phase = "maintenance_SC")  obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_days) |>   mutate(AMT = 0, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_,          phase = NA_character_)  events <- bind_rows(iv_dose, sc_dose, obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, CRP, BMI, phase)

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Chua_2025_mirikizumab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / week, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 52, by = 8)) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = expression("Mirikizumab concentration ("*mu*"g/mL)"),     title = "VIVID-1 regimen: 900 mg IV Q4W x 3, then 300 mg SC Q4W",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 300 virtual CD patients)",     caption = "Model: Chua et al. (2025) VIVID-1 final popPK."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: paper reports steady-state exposures phase regimen (Chua 2025 Section 3.1.1 Figure S6): Compute PKNCA NCA parameters steady-state IV interval (dose 3: week 8 week 12) steady-state SC interval (dose week 48 week 52). Simulated steady-state NCA regimen phase (median across N = 300).
code: tau <- 4 * week # 28 days  iv_ss_start <- 8 * week sc_ss_start <- 48 * week  # Concentrations over the two SS intervals, keyed by treatment group iv_conc <- sim |>   filter(time >= iv_ss_start, time <= iv_ss_start + tau, !is.na(Cc)) |>   transmute(ID = id, time_rel = time - iv_ss_start, Cc,             treatment = "IV_900mg_Q4W_ss")  sc_conc <- sim |>   filter(time >= sc_ss_start, time <= sc_ss_start + tau, !is.na(Cc)) |>   transmute(ID = id, time_rel = time - sc_ss_start, Cc,             treatment = "SC_300mg_Q4W_ss")  nca_conc <- bind_rows(iv_conc, sc_conc)  nca_dose <- bind_rows(   tibble(ID = cohort$ID, time_rel = 0, AMT = 900,          treatment = "IV_900mg_Q4W_ss"),   tibble(ID = cohort$ID, time_rel = 0, AMT = 300,          treatment = "SC_300mg_Q4W_ss") ) conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID)  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = tau,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■                      36% |  ETA:  3s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■   99% |  ETA:  0s  knitr::kable(   summary(nca_res),   digits  = 3,   caption = "Simulated steady-state NCA by regimen phase (median across N = 300)." )

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: Simulated vs published steady-state exposures (Chua 2025 Section 3.1.1). four metrics expected match within 20%. Typical-value checks (using rxode2::zeroRe() zero -subject variability) model development matched paper within ~10% IV Cmax,ss (336 vs 332) IV AUC_tau,ss (1902 vs 1820), within ~10-12% SC Cmax,ss (15.0 vs 13.6) SC AUC_tau,ss (246 vs 220). Terminal half-life typical 900 mg single IV dose 9.26 days (paper: 9.3 days, < 1% difference).
code: nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result)  simulated_sbs <- nca_tbl |>   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "auclast")) |>   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) |>   summarise(sim_median = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = sim_median) |>   rename(Cmax_sim = cmax, AUCtau_sim = auclast)  published <- tibble::tibble(   treatment  = c("IV_900mg_Q4W_ss", "SC_300mg_Q4W_ss"),   Cmax_pub   = c(332, 13.6),   AUCtau_pub = c(1820, 220) )  comparison <- published |>   left_join(simulated_sbs, by = "treatment") |>   mutate(     Cmax_pct_diff   = 100 * (Cmax_sim   - Cmax_pub)   / Cmax_pub,     AUCtau_pct_diff = 100 * (AUCtau_sim - AUCtau_pub) / AUCtau_pub   )  knitr::kable(   comparison,   digits  = 1,   caption = "Simulated vs published steady-state exposures (Chua 2025 Section 3.1.1)." )

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: SERENITY replicated. paper reports separate phase 2 popPK analysis (SERENITY) different covariates (SES-CD power-ALB CL, linear BMI effect F linear scale). analysis reproduced package. Users needing SERENITY estimates refer Chua 2025 Table 2 directly. BMI centering logit(F). Table 2 footnote e states F parameterized logit scale linear BMI slope, report BMI centering value used VIVID-1 fit. SERENITY analysis uses centering 24.75 kg/m^2 (also Table 2 footnote e); value adopted reasonable default. affect typical-value F = 38.8% BMI = 24.75. Residual error. Chua 2025 tabulate residual error Table 2; estimates shown supplementary model-code figure (Figure S13), available structured data. combined proportional 20% additive 0.10 ug/mL error assumed reasonable default IgG mAb popPK model. Users fitting model data re-estimate. Covariate distributions. WT, ALB, CRP, BMI marginals sampled independently; paper publish joint distributions. Marginals chosen approximate VIVID-1 demographics Table 1. Time-varying albumin simplification. ALB treated time-varying source model held constant baseline draw simulation. paper’s covariate-effect magnitude modest (CL shifts ~6% per +3 g/L ALB around 44.57 g/L), minor approximation steady-state exposures shown . Unit convention. published rate parameters reported h^-1 Chua 2025 Table 2; converted day^-1 nlmixr2lib (time unit = day).

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Citation: Cirincione B, Mager DE. Population pharmacokinetics exenatide. British Journal Clinical Pharmacology. 2017;83(3):517-526. doi:10.1111/bcp.13135 Description: Population PK model exenatide immediate-release (Cirincione 2017): two-compartment, parallel linear Michaelis-Menten elimination, sequential zero-order saturable first-order absorption SC dosing. Article: Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2017;83(3):517-526

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: published analysis pooled 195 subjects 8 clinical trials, including healthy volunteers patients type 2 diabetes mellitus. Baseline demographics Cirincione 2017 Table 1: 22% female, 67% Caucasian; age 18-76 years (mean 48, SD 12.9); body weight 52.6-162 kg (mean 86, SD 16.2); MDRD eGFR ranged 4.1 mL/min/1.73 m^2 (ESRD) >= 90 (normal), pooled mean 88.7 (SD 31.4). Renal-function distribution: 129 normal, 47 mild impairment, 10 moderate, 1 severe, 8 ESRD. Single repeated SC doses 2.5-20 ug. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Cirincione_2017_exenatide")$population.

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Cirincione_2017_exenatide.R. table collects review.

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Table 1 demographics: body weight truncated normal centered 86 kg (SD 16.2), CRCL distribution matching reported renal-function bands. non-Study-1 / non-Study-5 (pooled “”) residual group simulated NCA comparison uses largest share original cohort. fixed 10 ug single SC dose used match paper’s Figure 5 reference dose.
code: set.seed(20260418) n_subj <- 500  renal_bands <- tibble::tibble(   band       = c("normal", "mild", "moderate", "severe", "ESRD"),   n_in_paper = c(129, 47, 10, 1, 8),   egfr_min   = c(90, 60, 30, 15, 4.1),   egfr_max   = c(140, 89, 59, 29, 14) ) renal_bands$frac <- renal_bands$n_in_paper / sum(renal_bands$n_in_paper)  sample_band <- sample(renal_bands$band, n_subj, replace = TRUE, prob = renal_bands$frac) egfr_draws <- vapply(sample_band, function(b) {   row <- renal_bands[renal_bands$band == b, ]   runif(1, row$egfr_min, row$egfr_max) }, numeric(1))  cohort <- tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 86, sd = 16.2), 52.6), 162),   CRCL = egfr_draws,   STUDY1 = 0L,   STUDY5 = 0L,   treatment = factor(sample_band, levels = c("normal", "mild", "moderate", "severe", "ESRD")) )  events <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 10, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L, time = 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT, CRCL, STUDY1, STUDY5, treatment) |>   dplyr::bind_rows(     cohort |>       tidyr::crossing(time = c(seq(0, 4, 0.1), seq(4.2, 12, 0.2))) |>       dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L) |>       dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT, CRCL, STUDY1, STUDY5, treatment)   ) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: deterministic replication (typical-value predictions without -subject variability, matching paper’s Figure 5):
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Cirincione_2017_exenatide")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = c("WT", "CRCL", "treatment")) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> Warning: No sigma parameters in the model  typical_scenarios <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1:6,   scenario = c("75 kg, CRCL 90", "100 kg, CRCL 90", "125 kg, CRCL 90",     "75 kg, CRCL 75", "75 kg, CRCL 45", "median (84.8 kg, CRCL 80)"),   WT = c(75, 100, 125, 75, 75, 84.8),   CRCL = c(90, 90, 90, 75, 45, 80),   STUDY1 = 0L,   STUDY5 = 0L )  ev_typical <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(typical_scenarios)), function(i) {   ev_i <- rxode2::et(amt = 10, cmt = "depot") |>     rxode2::et(seq(0, 12, 0.05)) |>     as.data.frame()   ev_i$id <- typical_scenarios$id[i]   ev_i }))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical,   params = typical_scenarios[, c("id", "WT", "CRCL", "STUDY1", "STUDY5")],   returnType = "tibble" ) |>   dplyr::left_join(typical_scenarios[, c("id", "scenario")], by = "id") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalkm' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure 5 — typical concentration by body weight and CRCL
title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Cirincione 2017 Figure 5 shows simulated typical concentration-time profiles 10 ug SC dose across body weight CRCL scenarios. Lower body weight lower CRCL increase exposure (lower central volume lower linear clearance, respectively).
code: sim_typical |>   ggplot(aes(time, Cc, colour = scenario)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "Exenatide concentration (pg/mL)",     colour = NULL,     title = "Typical concentration profiles (replicates Figure 5)",     caption = "Replicates Figure 5 of Cirincione 2017: 10 ug SC, typical values.") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: VPC-style summary simulated cohort, stratified renal-function band.
code: sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~treatment) +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "Exenatide concentration (pg/mL)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles by renal-function band",     caption = "10 ug SC; cohort covariate distributions approximate Table 1.") #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Compute Cmax, Tmax, AUC0-last, terminal half-life using PKNCA. Groups renal-function band (treatment stratifier) summary can compared paper’s typical values.
