title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: paper reports typical Cmax 75 kg approximately 287, 273, 321 pg/mL CRCL 90, 75, 45 mL/min/1.73 m^2, respectively (Figure 5), typical terminal half-life ~2.4 h. deterministic (“typical”) check simulated cohort: Simulated typical values (cmax, tmax, half.life) vs. Cirincione 2017 Figure 5 / typical half-life.
code: nca_typical <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(   PKNCA::PKNCAconc(     sim_typical |>       dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>       dplyr::mutate(treatment = scenario) |>       dplyr::distinct(id, time, treatment, .keep_all = TRUE) |>       dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment),     Cc ~ time | treatment + id   ),   PKNCA::PKNCAdose(     typical_scenarios |>       dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 10, treatment = scenario) |>       dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment),     amt ~ time | treatment + id   ),   intervals = data.frame(start = 0, end = 12, cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE, half.life = TRUE) ))  nca_typical_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_typical$result) |>   dplyr::filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "half.life")) |>   dplyr::select(treatment, PPTESTCD, PPORRES) |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES)  published <- tibble::tribble(   ~treatment, ~cmax_pub, ~thalf_pub,   "75 kg, CRCL 90", 287, 2.4,   "100 kg, CRCL 90", 237, 2.4,   "125 kg, CRCL 90", 189, 2.4,   "75 kg, CRCL 75", 273, 2.4,   "75 kg, CRCL 45", 321, 2.4,   "median (84.8 kg, CRCL 80)", NA, 2.4 )  dplyr::left_join(published, nca_typical_tbl, by = "treatment") |>   knitr::kable(digits = 2,     caption = "Simulated typical values (cmax, tmax, half.life) vs. Cirincione 2017 Figure 5 / typical half-life.")

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: source column DVID (character-valued: "study1" / "study5" / "otherStudy") split two binary indicators (STUDY1, STUDY5) per nlmixr2lib covariate-columns.md register. STUDY1 = STUDY5 = 0 selects pooled-residual error. Km reported paper 567 pg/mL. model encoded ng/mL (0.567) remain unit-consistent Cc = central / vc (ug/L = ng/mL); observation subsequently multiplied 1000 report pg/mL. virtual cohort approximates Table 1 demographics. Race distribution used model therefore reproduced. CRCL sampled uniformly within renal-function band using paper’s per-band counts. IIV variance encoded log(1 + CV^2) (log-normal variance CV) rather shorthand log(1 + CV) prior version file used. matches standard NONMEM → nlmixr2 interpretation paper reports CV% log-normal random effects. Typical-value Cmax agrees Figure 5 within ~10% across weight CRCL scenarios; simulated terminal half-life ~2.4 h matches published value. Differences corrected tuning parameters.

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Replicate Figures 4, 5, 6 Clegg et al. (2024): Figure 4: Prediction-corrected VPC final model, stratified trial RSV season (Phase 2b, MELODY, MEDLEY Season 1, MEDLEY Season 2). Figure 5: Prediction-corrected VPC MELODY safety cohort. Figure 6: Predicted exposure (AUC₀₋₃₆₅, C_max, Day-151 concentration) versus body weight gestational age (GA) group weight-banded dosing. original study data publicly available, figures show simulated prediction intervals virtual infant populations whose covariate distributions approximate published trial demographics (Table 1, Clegg 2024). Body weight trajectories generated using weight--age growth standards (combined sex, 0–24 months).

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: weight--age LMS parameters combined sex, 0–24 months (Multicentre Growth Reference Study Group, 2006). Formula: weight (kg) = M × (1 + L × S × z)^(1/L)

what: Helper: build a virtual cohort dataset
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Builds dose + observation records virtual cohort n infants. ga_range: gestational age range weeks. pna0_range: postnatal age dosing range months. max_day: follow-duration days. season2: TRUE Season 2 (200 mg flat dose, Season 2 CL effect applied). obs_days: observation time points days.
code: make_cohort <- function(n, ga_range, pna0_range, max_day,                         season2 = FALSE, obs_days = seq(0, max_day, by = 7)) {   GA <- runif(n, ga_range[1], ga_range[2]) # weeks   PNA_0 <- runif(n, pna0_range[1], pna0_range[2]) # months at dosing   wt_z <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n, 0, 1)))   WT_0 <- who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   AMT <- if (season2) rep(200, n) else ifelse(WT_0 < 5, 50, 100)    pop <- data.frame(     ID = seq_len(n), GA, PNA_0, wt_z, WT_0, AMT,     SEASON2 = as.integer(season2),     ADA_POS = 0L,     # All White/Nat.Haw reference race for simplicity     RACE_BLACK_OTH = 0L,     RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI = 0L   )    # Dose records   d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(       TIME = 0, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot", DV = NA,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA_0,       WT = WT_0     )    # Observation records with time-varying WT and PAGE   d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(obs_days)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(obs_days, times = n),       EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA, AMT = 0,       PNA = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA,       WT = who_weight(pmax(0, PNA), wt_z)     )    bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>     select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PAGE,       RACE_BLACK_OTH, RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI, SEASON2, ADA_POS, WT_0) }

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Clegg_2024_nirsevimab")

what: Figure 4 — Prediction-corrected VPC by trial and season
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Four panels matching published trials: vignette shows simulated prediction intervals (observed data; original data publicly available).
code:
set.seed(8897) # MEDI8897 = nirsevimab development code  d_p2b <- make_cohort(200, c(29, 35), c(0, 3), 400) |> mutate(trial = "Phase 2b
(healthy infants, 29 to <35 wGA)") d_melody <- make_cohort(200, c(35, 42), c(0, 3), 500) |> mutate(trial = "MELODY
(healthy infants, ≥35 wGA)") d_med_s1 <- make_cohort(200, c(24, 35), c(0, 3), 400) |>   mutate(trial = "MEDLEY Season 1
(infants ≤35 wGA, CHD/CLD)") d_med_s2 <- make_cohort(100, c(35, 42), c(12, 24), 400,   season2 = TRUE) |> mutate(trial = "MEDLEY Season 2
(children with CHD/CLD)")  sim_f4 <- bind_rows(d_p2b, d_melody, d_med_s1, d_med_s2)  out_f4 <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = sim_f4) |>   as.data.frame() |>   left_join(     sim_f4 |> select(ID, trial) |> distinct(),     by = c("id" = "ID")   ) #> Warning in left_join(as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = sim_f4)), : Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`. #> ℹ Row 1 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`. #> ℹ Row 1 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`. #> ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship = #>   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning. d_f4 <- out_f4 |>   group_by(trial, time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(trial = factor(trial, levels = c(     "Phase 2b
(healthy infants, 29 to <35 wGA)",     "MELODY
(healthy infants, ≥35 wGA)",     "MEDLEY Season 1
(infants ≤35 wGA, CHD/CLD)",     "MEDLEY Season 2
(children with CHD/CLD)"   ))) ggplot(d_f4, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "#4682b4", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~trial, scales = "free_x", nrow = 2) +   scale_y_log10(     limits = c(0.1, NA),     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 500, by = 100)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = "Nirsevimab serum concentration (μg/mL)
Prediction corrected",     title = "Figure 4 — Prediction-corrected VPC by trial and season",     caption = paste0(       "Simulated median (line) and 90% prediction interval (shaded).
",       "Original observed data not available; reference race = White, ADA negative."     )   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 9)) #> Warning in scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.1, NA), labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)): log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values.

what: Figure 5 — Prediction-corrected VPC for the MELODY safety cohort
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Healthy term late-preterm infants (≥35 weeks GA), Season 1, followed 360 days. paper uses 10th/50th/90th percentiles (rather 5th/95th used Figure 4).
code: set.seed(3979) # NCT03979313 = MELODY trial d_f5 <- make_cohort(500, c(35, 42), c(0, 3), 360,   obs_days = seq(0, 360, by = 7))  out_f5 <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_f5) |> as.data.frame() d_f5_plot <- out_f5 |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q10 = quantile(Cc, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q90 = quantile(Cc, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) ggplot(d_f5_plot, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q10, ymax = Q90), fill = "#4682b4", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q10), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q90), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +   scale_y_log10(     limits = c(0.5, NA),     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 360, by = 30)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = "Nirsevimab serum concentration (\u03bcg/mL)\nPrediction corrected",     title = "Figure 5 \u2014 Prediction-corrected VPC: MELODY safety cohort",     caption = paste0(       "Simulated 10th/50th/90th percentiles (N = 500 virtual infants, \u226535 wGA).\n",       "Original observed data not available."     )   ) +   theme_bw() #> Warning in scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.5, NA), labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)): log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values.

what: Figure 6 — Predicted exposure versus body weight by GA group
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Simulate 3 GA groups (GA <29 weeks, ≥29 <35 weeks, ≥35 weeks), dosing birth, weight-banded dosing (50 mg ≤5 kg, 100 mg >5 kg). Compute AUC₀₋₃₆₅ (trapezoidal), C_max, Day-151 concentration. Use daily time steps accurate NCA. Adult reference lines paper (3000 mg IV dose): AUC₀₋₃₆₅ = 57.0 day·mg/mL; C_max = 1140 μg/mL. EC₉₀ (preclinical) = 6.8 μg/mL (Day-151 concentration panel).
code: set.seed(2024) n_per_ga <- 200 obs_daily <- seq(0, 365, by = 1)  ga_groups <- list(   list(label = "GA < 29 weeks", ga = c(24, 29)),   list(label = "GA \u226529 to <35 weeks", ga = c(29, 35)),   list(label = "GA \u226535 weeks", ga = c(35, 42)) )  sim_f6_list <- lapply(ga_groups, function(g) {   GA <- runif(n_per_ga, g$ga[1], g$ga[2])   PNA_0 <- rep(0, n_per_ga) # dose at birth   wt_z <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n_per_ga, 0, 1)))   WT_0 <- who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   AMT <- ifelse(WT_0 <= 5, 50, 100)    pop <- data.frame(     ID = seq_len(n_per_ga), GA, PNA_0, wt_z, WT_0, AMT,     SEASON2 = 0L, ADA_POS = 0L,     RACE_BLACK_OTH = 0L, RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI = 0L   )    d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(TIME = 0, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot", DV = NA,       PAGE = GA / 4.35, WT = WT_0)    d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_per_ga), each = length(obs_daily)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(obs_daily, times = n_per_ga),       EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA, AMT = 0,       PNA = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA,       WT = who_weight(pmax(0, PNA), wt_z)     )    d_events <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>     select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PAGE,       RACE_BLACK_OTH, RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI, SEASON2, ADA_POS, WT_0)    out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_events) |>     as.data.frame()    # NCA by individual   nca <- out |>     group_by(id) |>     summarise(       AUC0_365 = {         t <- time         c <- Cc         sum(diff(t) * (c[-length(c)] + c[-1]) / 2) # trapezoid       },       Cmax = max(Cc),       C_day151 = Cc[which.min(abs(time - 151))],       WT_base = WT_0[1],       .groups = "drop"     ) |>     mutate(ga_group = g$label) })  d_f6_nca <- bind_rows(sim_f6_list) |>   mutate(ga_group = factor(ga_group, levels = sapply(ga_groups, `[[`, "label"))) # 90% prediction intervals (5th-95th) by baseline weight bin wt_bins <- seq(1, 11, by = 0.5)  d_f6_long <- d_f6_nca |>   pivot_longer(c(AUC0_365, Cmax, C_day151), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") |>   mutate(wt_bin = cut(WT_base, breaks = wt_bins, right = FALSE))  d_f6_pi <- d_f6_long |>   group_by(ga_group, metric, wt_bin) |>   summarise(     wt_mid = mean(WT_base),     Q05 = quantile(value, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(value, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(value, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   filter(!is.na(wt_bin))  metric_labels <- c(   AUC0_365 = "AUC[0-365]~(day%.%mg/mL)",   Cmax     = "C[max]~(mu*g/mL)",   C_day151 = "C[day~151]~(mu*g/mL)" ) # Adult reference values (3000 mg IV) adult_refs <- data.frame(   metric = c("AUC0_365", "Cmax"),   yref   = c(57.0, 1140) ) ec90_val <- 6.8 # preclinical EC90, μg/mL  ggplot(d_f6_pi, aes(x = wt_mid)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "#87ceeb", alpha = 0.6) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q50), linewidth = 0.8) +   # Individual posthoc predictions   geom_point(     data = d_f6_nca |>       pivot_longer(c(AUC0_365, Cmax, C_day151), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value"),     aes(x = WT_base, y = value),     colour = "grey50", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.4   ) +   # Adult reference lines (AUC and Cmax panels only)   geom_hline(     data = adult_refs,     aes(yintercept = yref),     linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.7   ) +   # EC90 line (Day-151 panel only)   geom_hline(     data = data.frame(metric = "C_day151", yref = ec90_val),     aes(yintercept = yref),     linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7   ) +   facet_grid(     metric ~ ga_group,     scales = "free_y",     labeller = labeller(metric = as_labeller(metric_labels, label_parsed))   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 11, by = 2)) +   labs(     x = "Baseline weight (kg)",     y = NULL,     title = "Figure 6 \u2014 Predicted exposure vs body weight by GA group",     caption = paste0(       "Blue band: 90% prediction interval (5th\u201395th percentile). ",       "Black line: median. Grey dots: individual predictions.\n",       "Dotted line: adult reference (3000 mg IV). ",       "Dashed line: preclinical EC\u2090 = 6.8 \u03bcg/mL (Day-151 panel).\n",       "White race, ADA negative, Season 1 assumed."     )   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(     strip.text.x = element_text(size = 9),     strip.text.y = element_text(size = 8)   ) #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Model: 2-compartment first-order IM absorption; published parameters 70 kg adult reference, scaled allometrically infant weights. Virtual populations: Covariate distributions match published trial demographics (Table 1). Race set White/Native Hawaiian reference ADA negative simulated subjects isolate weight maturation effects. Weight trajectories: weight--age LMS growth standards (combined sex, 0–24 months), infant’s growth percentile (z-score) held constant. IIV: Simulated using published omega² values: CL (26% CV), V2 (43% CV), Ka (44% CV; high shrinkage 83%), correlation r = 0.785 CL V2. Q V3 IIV final model. Residual error: Proportional 21%. Figure 6 NCA: AUC₀₋₃₆₅ computed trapezoidal rule daily simulated concentrations; C_max Day-151 concentration derived densely sampled time course.

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Clegg L, Freshwater E, Leach , Villafana T, Wahlby Hamren U. Population Pharmacokinetics Nirsevimab Preterm Term Infants. J Clin Pharmacol. 2024;64(5):555-567. doi:10.1002/jcph.2401

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Citation: Farrell C, Schweizer C, Wustner J, Weil S, Namiki M, Nakano T, Nakai K, Phillips MD. Population pharmacokinetics farletuzumab, humanized monoclonal antibody folate receptor alpha, epithelial ovarian cancer. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 2012;70(5):727-734. doi:10.1007/s00280-012-1959-y Description: Two-compartment population PK model farletuzumab (humanized IgG1 anti-folate-receptor-alpha monoclonal antibody) first-order linear elimination IV infusion women advanced epithelial ovarian cancer (Farrell 2012). Article: Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 2012;70(5):727-734

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: analysis pooled 79 women advanced epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, primary peritoneal cancer two clinical trials (Farrell 2012 Table 1): Phase study MORAb-003-001 (n = 25, weekly IV farletuzumab ascending 12.5–400 mg/m² 4 weeks relapse standard chemotherapy) Phase II study MORAb-003-002 (n = 54, relapsed platinum-sensitive disease; 37.5, 62.5, 100 mg/m² weekly, majority 100 mg/m² combination chemotherapy arms later transitioned back monotherapy). Patients 31–81 years old (median 61); body weight ranged 44.5–118.2 kg (median 66.2); reported race predominantly Caucasian (~77%) smaller Asian, Hispanic, Black, groups. Farletuzumab given 1 mg/min infusion advanced 5 mg/min tolerated. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Farrell_2012_farletuzumab")$population.

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Farrell_2012_farletuzumab.R. table collects review.

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Table 1 overall demographics: body weights 44.5–118.2 kg range drawn truncated distribution around 66.2 kg median, every patient assigned PHASE2 = 1 (Phase II residual error — larger study one combined additive + proportional error). Dosing 100 mg/m² weekly IV infusion 1 hour 8 weeks, matching primary Phase II dosing schedule.
code: set.seed(20260421) n_subj <- 100  # Truncated approximation of the Table 1 overall weight distribution: # median 66.2, range 44.5–118.2. Use a log-normal-like sample then clip. wt_draws <- pmin(pmax(exp(rnorm(n_subj, mean = log(66.2), sd = 0.18)), 44.5), 118.2) # BSA derived by DuBois from weight assuming 163 cm mean height (clipped 1.37–2.24 # to match Table 1): BSA = 0.007184 * (wt^0.425) * (height_cm^0.725). bsa_draws <- pmin(pmax(0.007184 * wt_draws^0.425 * 163^0.725, 1.37), 2.24)  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = wt_draws,   BSA = bsa_draws,   PHASE2 = 1L,   dose_mgm2 = 100,   dose_mg = 100 * bsa_draws  # mg/m^2 x m^2 = mg ) cohort$treatment <- "100 mg/m2 weekly" # Build event table: 8 weekly 1-hour IV infusions + dense sampling for NCA. tau <- 24 * 7  # one week in hours n_doses <- 8 dose_times <- seq(0, by = tau, length.out = n_doses)  doses <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(     amt = dose_mg,     rate = dose_mg,      # 1-hour infusion => rate = dose (mg/h)     cmt = "central",     evid = 1L   ) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, rate, cmt, evid, WT, PHASE2, treatment)  # Observation grid: dense during the first week, then per-week troughs, and # one long post-last-dose tail for terminal-phase characterization. obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 24, by = 1),                  # first day, hourly   seq(28, 168, by = 12),               # rest of week 1, 12-hourly   seq(170, 8 * tau - 1, by = 24),      # dose interval troughs   seq(8 * tau, 8 * tau + 60 * 24, by = 24)  # 60-day terminal tail )))  obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, rate = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, rate, cmt, evid, WT, PHASE2, treatment)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(doses, obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Typical-value replication (zeroing -subject variability) useful “central” prediction curve carry wide Vp IIV:
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Farrell_2012_farletuzumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod,   events = events,   keep = c("WT", "PHASE2", "treatment") ) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> Warning: No sigma parameters in the model  typical_cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1L, WT = 66.2, PHASE2 = 1L, treatment = "100 mg/m2 weekly (typical)" )  typical_doses <- typical_cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 170, rate = 170, cmt = "central", evid = 1L) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, rate, cmt, evid, WT, PHASE2, treatment)  typical_obs <- typical_cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, rate = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, rate, cmt, evid, WT, PHASE2, treatment)  typical_events <- dplyr::bind_rows(typical_doses, typical_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = typical_events, keep = c("WT", "PHASE2", "treatment") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp'

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Farrell 2012 Figure 2 visual predictive check final model observed data. five-fifty-ninety-five percentile summary simulated cohort weekly dosing interval post-last-dose terminal phase captures structural features (peak near end infusion, slow biexponential decline).
code: sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(day = time / 24) |>   dplyr::group_by(day) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(day, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (days)", y = "Farletuzumab concentration (µg/mL)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles — 100 mg/m² weekly × 8",     caption = "Replicates the structure of Figure 2 (VPC) of Farrell 2012."   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Figure 3 — weekly vs. 3-weekly dosing at matched mg/kg/week
title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Farrell 2012 Figure 3 shows steady-state exposure similar weekly vs. 3-weekly dosing weekly-equivalent mg/kg dose maintained. block reproduces comparison using typical-value predictions.
code: wt_ref <- 66.2 bsa_ref <- 0.007184 * wt_ref^0.425 * 163^0.725  # ~1.70 m^2  weekly_mg  <- 100 * bsa_ref                      # 100 mg/m^2 weekly q3w_mg     <- 3 * weekly_mg                      # same mg/kg/week, given every 3 weeks  tau_w  <- 24 * 7 tau_q3 <- 24 * 21  make_regimen <- function(amt, tau, n_doses, label) {   dt <- seq(0, by = tau, length.out = n_doses)   obs_t <- sort(unique(c(     seq(0, max(dt) + tau, by = 6),     seq(max(dt) + tau, max(dt) + tau + 60 * 24, by = 24)   )))   dplyr::bind_rows(     tibble::tibble(id = 1L, time = dt,   amt = amt, rate = amt,       cmt = "central", evid = 1L, WT = wt_ref, PHASE2 = 1L, treatment = label),     tibble::tibble(id = 1L, time = obs_t, amt = 0, rate = 0,       cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L, WT = wt_ref, PHASE2 = 1L, treatment = label)   ) |> dplyr::arrange(time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  ev_weekly <- make_regimen(weekly_mg, tau_w,  12, "100 mg/m² weekly") ev_q3w    <- make_regimen(q3w_mg,    tau_q3,  4, "300 mg/m² every 3 weeks")  regimen_events <- dplyr::bind_rows(   ev_weekly,   ev_q3w |> dplyr::mutate(id = 2L) )  sim_regimens <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = regimen_events, keep = c("WT", "PHASE2", "treatment") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'  sim_regimens |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(day = time / 24) |>   ggplot(aes(day, Cc, colour = treatment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)", y = "Farletuzumab concentration (µg/mL)",     colour = NULL,     title = "Weekly vs. every-3-week dosing at matched mg/m²/week (typical)",     caption = "Replicates Figure 3 of Farrell 2012."   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Compute Cmax, Tmax, AUC(0-τ) (one-week interval first dose), terminal half-life using PKNCA. terminal half-life estimated 60-day post-last-dose tail, β-phase dominates. Simulated NCA summaries 100 mg/m² weekly Phase II cohort.
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  # After-first-dose NCA over tau = 1 week, plus terminal-phase half-life over # the 60-day post-last-dose tail. start_last <- max(dose_df$time)  # last dose time intervals <- data.frame(   start      = c(0,   start_last + 1,    start_last + 1),   end        = c(tau, start_last + tau,  start_last + 60 * 24),   cmax       = c(TRUE,  TRUE,  FALSE),   tmax       = c(TRUE,  TRUE,  FALSE),   auclast    = c(TRUE,  TRUE,  FALSE),   ctrough    = c(TRUE,  TRUE,  FALSE),   cmin       = c(FALSE, TRUE,  FALSE),   half.life  = c(FALSE, FALSE, TRUE) )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #> Warning: Requesting an what: Comparison against the analytical terminal half-life
title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Farrell 2012 tabulate NCA-derived Cmax/AUC. dominant biexponential rate constant (β) can computed analytically Table 3 parameters direct numerical check. Analytical two-compartment half-lives Table 3 point estimates. terminal β half-life computed Table 3 consistent published IgG-class monoclonal antibody half-lives (multi-week).
code: CL <- 0.00784; Vc <- 3.00; Q <- 0.0203; Vp <- 7.50 k10 <- CL / Vc k12 <- Q  / Vc k21 <- Q  / Vp a <- k10 + k12 + k21 b <- k10 * k21 alpha <- (a + sqrt(a^2 - 4 * b)) / 2 beta  <- (a - sqrt(a^2 - 4 * b)) / 2 thalf_alpha_h <- log(2) / alpha thalf_beta_h  <- log(2) / beta  tibble::tibble(   quantity = c("Distribution half-life (α-phase)",                "Terminal half-life (β-phase)"),   value_h    = round(c(thalf_alpha_h, thalf_beta_h), 1),   value_days = round(c(thalf_alpha_h, thalf_beta_h) / 24, 1) ) |>   knitr::kable(caption = "Analytical two-compartment half-lives from Table 3 point estimates.")

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: IIV correlation. paper’s Methods section states “Initial model building used diagonal covariance matrix inter-individual variability correlation clearance volume central compartment included starting model.” Table 3, however, reports diagonal ω² values CL, Vc, Vp without covariance entry. model implemented diagonal IIV match Table 3 tabulates; narrative hint starting CL–Vc correlation carried final covariance value published. Per-study residual error. paper reports separate residual-error estimates Phase Phase II studies (Table 3). new binary PHASE2 indicator (0 = Phase , 1 = Phase II) selects model, following pattern used Cirincione_2017_exenatide.R STUDY1 / STUDY5. virtual cohort sets PHASE2 = 1 Phase II study larger cohort (1,750 2,472 samples) drives weekly weekly / Q3W comparison explored Figure 3. Reference weight. paper specifies COV_ST median covariate value study population. Table 1 overall median weight 66.2 kg used; within-final-dataset median (excluding 169 low-dose samples) separately reported. BSA dose scaling. Doses administered mg/m². virtual cohort computes BSA DuBois subject’s weight assuming 163 cm mean height, clipped Table 1 BSA range (1.37–2.24 m²). paper publish per-subject heights. BSA used convert mg/m² doses mg amounts; covariate model (weight effect CL Vc relevant PK scaling). Dose exclusion. final model built 2,303 samples excluding 169 samples 12.5 25 mg/m² due apparent low-dose nonlinearity. packaged model reproduces final-model parameters therefore used simulate exposure two lowest doses; implemented structure strictly linear. Residual-error interpretation. Table 3’s column heading “σ² proportional (% CV)” reports variance SD% last column’s % CV SD. model uses SDs (20.5% / 34.9% / 7.94 µg/mL) nlmixr2 propSd addSd magnitudes.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Simulate infliximab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Fasanmade et al. (2009) patients ulcerative colitis ACT 1 ACT 2 phase III trials (N = 482). model 2-compartment IV infusion model linear elimination. Covariates final model serum albumin, anti-drug antibody (ATI / ADA) status, sex clearance, body weight sex central volume. Concomitant immunomodulators (azathioprine, 6-mercaptopurine) evaluated statistically significant effect CL.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: set.seed(2009) n_subj <- 500  pop <- data.frame(   ID      = seq_len(n_subj),   WT      = rlnorm(n_subj, log(77), 0.22),   # Median 77 kg, range ~40-177   ALB     = rnorm(n_subj, 4.1, 0.4),         # Mean 4.1, SD 0.4 g/dL   SEXF    = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.392),        # 39.2% female   ADA_POS = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.068)         # 6.8% ADA-positive ) pop$ALB <- pmax(2.0, pmin(pop$ALB, 5.5))

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Standard UC induction: 5 mg/kg IV weeks 0, 2, 6, maintenance every 8 weeks week 46 (8 doses total). Infusion 2 hours.
code: dose_weeks <- c(0, 2, 6, 14, 22, 30, 38, 46) dose_times <- dose_weeks * 7  # convert to days  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 7, by = 0.5),     # Dense first week   seq(7, 364, by = 3.5)    # Twice weekly thereafter )))  d_dose <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = round(5 * WT, 1),  # 5 mg/kg     EVID = 1,     CMT  = 1,     DUR  = 2 / 24,            # 2-hour infusion in days     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 1, DUR = NA_real_, DV = NA_real_)  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Fasanmade_2009_infliximab") sim <- rxSolve(mod, d_sim, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: sim_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkred") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "darkred", linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Infliximab concentration (ug/mL)",     title = "Simulated infliximab PK (5 mg/kg IV, induction + q8w maintenance)",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500 virtual UC patients)",     caption = "Model: Fasanmade et al. (2009) Eur J Clin Pharmacol"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Effect of anti-drug antibodies on PK
title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: sim_ada <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   mutate(ADA_label = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")) %>%   group_by(time, ADA_label) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_ada, aes(x = time / 7, y = median, color = ADA_label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("ADA-negative" = "steelblue", "ADA-positive" = "tomato")) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Infliximab concentration (ug/mL)",     title = "Infliximab median concentrations by ADA status",     subtitle = "ADA-positive patients have 47% higher clearance",     color = "Immunogenicity",     caption = "Model: Fasanmade et al. (2009)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Run PKNCA maintenance dosing interval (5th dose, weeks 22–30). Stratify ADA status treatment group NCA summary, required skill’s PKNCA recipe. NCA summary (maintenance interval, weeks 22-30), stratified ADA status
code: nca_conc <- sim %>%   filter(time >= 154, time <= 210, Cc > 0) %>%   mutate(     time_rel = time - 154,     treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")   ) %>%   rename(ID = id) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- pop %>%   mutate(     time_rel  = 0,     AMT       = 5 * WT,     treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")   ) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, AMT, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 56,     cmax      = TRUE,     tmax      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE,     half.life = TRUE   ) ) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■■■                           20% |  ETA:  8s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                 54% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      88% |  ETA:  1s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = "NCA summary (maintenance interval, weeks 22-30), stratified by ADA status" )

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Fasanmade 2009 publish individual PK per-subject covariate values, virtual population approximates reported distributions rather reproducing : Weight: sampled log-normal around 77 kg median SD 0.22 log scale (≈ 40–177 kg), matching reported range. Albumin: sampled normal around mean 4.1 g/dL, SD 0.4 g/dL, clipped [2.0, 5.5] g/dL. Sex ADA status: sampled independently 39.2% female 6.8% ADA-positive, respectively. pooled ACT 1 / ACT 2 data fully independent, joint distribution reported. correlation WT ALB imposed; paper report one. Dosing regimen uses 5 mg/kg induction + q8w maintenance schedule. Fasanmade 2009 pooled 10 mg/kg arm; 5 mg/kg schedule approved label dose relevant typical use. Race included retained covariate final model.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Model: 2-compartment IV linear elimination. TMDD. Key covariates: Albumin (strongest, power -1.54 CL), ADA status (+47.1% CL), sex (-23.6% CL -13.7% Vc females). Body weight significant Vc (exponent 0.538), CL (unlike mAb models). Concomitant immunomodulators (AZA, 6-MP) affect PK final model.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Fasanmade AA, Adedokun OJ, Blank M, Zhou H, Davis HM. Population pharmacokinetic analysis infliximab patients ulcerative colitis. Eur J Clin Pharmacol. 2009;65(12):1211-1228. doi:10.1007/s00228-009-0718-4

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Simulate fremanezumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Fiedler-Kelly et al. (2019). Fremanezumab fully humanized IgG2 delta-/kappa monoclonal antibody binds CGRP approved preventive treatment migraine. model fitted 13,745 concentrations 2,546 subjects (74 healthy adults 2,474 patients episodic chronic migraine) pooled across seven phase 1 / 2b / 3 studies IV SC administration. structural model 2-compartment disposition first-order SC absorption, absorption lag time, first-order elimination. Weight covariate retained final model, entering allometric power clearance (exponent 1.05) central volume distribution (exponent 1.53) 71 kg reference. intrinsic extrinsic factors examined (age, sex, race, albumin, renal hepatic function, ADA status, injection site, acute / analgesic / preventive medication use, patient vs. healthy subject status) statistically significant absent packaged model. Article: Br J Clin Pharmacol 85(12):2721-2733

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Fiedler-Kelly 2019 (Section 3.1 Supporting Table S1): 2,546 subjects contributed 13,745 concentrations. analysis population primarily Caucasian (79.9%) female (86.1%), median (range) age 43 (18-71) years median (range) body weight 70.8 (43.5-131.8) kg. presence anti-drug antibodies confirmed 0.7% samples significant covariate. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab")$population (note: readModelDb() returns parsed model function, field accessed attribute function body rather live list).

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Simulate 500-subject virtual cohort whose weight distribution matches Section 3.1 Fiedler-Kelly 2019 (median 70.8 kg, range 43.5-131.8 kg). paper publish individual-level weight distribution, use log-normal approximation bounded reported range.
code: set.seed(2019) n_subj <- 500  pop <- tibble(   ID = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, meanlog = log(70.8), sdlog = 0.22), 43.5), 131.8) )

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Replicate two phase 3 therapeutic regimens reported Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Section 3.4: 225 mg SC monthly 12 doses, 675 mg SC quarterly 4 doses. Simulate 12 months daily sampling first cycle weekly samples thereafter.
code: obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 28, by = 1),   seq(28, 364, by = 7) )))  build_events <- function(pop, dose_mg, dose_times, regimen_label) {   dose_rows <- pop %>%     crossing(time = dose_times) %>%     mutate(amt = dose_mg, evid = 1, cmt = "depot", dv = NA_real_)   obs_rows <- pop %>%     crossing(time = obs_times) %>%     mutate(amt = NA_real_, evid = 0, cmt = NA_character_, dv = NA_real_)   bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) %>%     mutate(treatment = regimen_label) %>%     arrange(ID, time, desc(evid)) }  events_monthly <- build_events(pop,   dose_mg       = 225,   dose_times    = seq(0, by = 28, length.out = 12),   regimen_label = "225 mg SC Q4W")  events_quarterly <- build_events(pop,   dose_mg       = 675,   dose_times    = seq(0, by = 84, length.out = 4),   regimen_label = "675 mg SC Q12W")  events_all <- bind_rows(events_monthly, events_quarterly)

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab")  sim <- events_all %>%   group_by(treatment) %>%   group_modify(~ {     ev <- .x %>% rename(id = ID)     as_tibble(rxSolve(mod, ev, returnType = "data.frame"))   }) %>%   ungroup() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Replicate Figure 7: concentration-time profiles over 12 months
title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Figure 7 Fiedler-Kelly 2019 shows median 90% prediction intervals fremanezumab concentration-time profiles two phase 3 regimens.
code: fig7 <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(treatment, time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fig7, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi, fill = treatment), alpha = 0.2) +   geom_line(aes(y = median, colour = treatment), linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~treatment, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x       = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y       = "Fremanezumab concentration (ug/mL)",     title   = "Figure 7 replication - simulated fremanezumab exposure",     caption = "Replicates Figure 7 of Fiedler-Kelly et al. 2019. Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500)."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

what: Replicate Figure 8 (qualitative): exposure vs. body-weight quartiles
title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Figure 8 Fiedler-Kelly 2019 shows monotonic decrease steady-state exposure across quartiles body weight regimens.
code: cav_monthly <- sim %>%   filter(treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W",          time >= 11 * 28, time <= 12 * 28) %>%   group_by(id, WT) %>%   summarise(Cav = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%   mutate(interval = "Cav,ss(0-28d)")  cav_quarterly <- sim %>%   filter(treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W",          time >= 3 * 84, time <= 4 * 84) %>%   group_by(id, WT) %>%   summarise(Cav = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%   mutate(interval = "Cav,ss(0-84d)")  cav_bind <- bind_rows(cav_monthly, cav_quarterly) %>%   group_by(interval) %>%   mutate(wt_quartile = cut(WT,                            breaks = quantile(WT, c(0, .25, .5, .75, 1)),                            include.lowest = TRUE,                            labels = paste0("Q", 1:4))) %>%   ungroup()  ggplot(cav_bind, aes(x = wt_quartile, y = Cav)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = wt_quartile), alpha = 0.7) +   facet_wrap(~interval, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x       = "Body-weight quartile",     y       = "Average steady-state concentration (ug/mL)",     title   = "Figure 8 replication - steady-state exposure by body-weight quartile",     caption = "Replicates qualitative trend shown in Figure 8 of Fiedler-Kelly et al. 2019."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Paper-reported NCA metrics simulation can compared (Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Section 3.4 Table 3): median terminal half-life approximately 30 days, independent dose regimen; steady state reached approximately 168 days (6 months); accumulation ratio AUC across 225 mg SC Q4W regimen (12th vs. 1st dose, 0-28 days) median = 2.43; 675 mg SC Q12W regimen (4th vs. 1st dose, 0-84 days) median = 1.21. Compute simulated single-dose AUC Cmax first dosing interval last dosing interval, compare ratio published accumulation ratios. PKNCA grouped treatment + id. PKNCA summary treatment / interval (auclast ug*day/mL, Cmax ug/mL). Accumulation ratios: simulated medians vs. Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Table 3. simulated accumulation ratios within approximately 20% published medians; larger deviations motivate re-examination dose schedule sampling grid rather parameter tuning.
code: nca_window <- function(sim, treat_label, dose_amt, start, end) {   sim %>%     filter(treatment == treat_label, time >= start, time <= end) %>%     transmute(       id,       time_rel  = time - start,       Cc,       treatment = paste(treat_label, ifelse(start == 0, "dose 1", "last dose")),       amt       = dose_amt,       WT     ) }  conc_monthly_d1 <- nca_window(sim, "225 mg SC Q4W", 225, 0, 28) conc_monthly_ss <- nca_window(sim, "225 mg SC Q4W", 225, 11 * 28, 12 * 28)  conc_quarterly_d1 <- nca_window(sim, "675 mg SC Q12W", 675, 0, 84) conc_quarterly_ss <- nca_window(sim, "675 mg SC Q12W", 675, 3 * 84, 4 * 84)  nca_conc <- bind_rows(conc_monthly_d1, conc_monthly_ss,                       conc_quarterly_d1, conc_quarterly_ss) %>%   filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0)  nca_dose <- nca_conc %>%   group_by(id, treatment, amt) %>%   summarise(time_rel = 0, .groups = "drop") %>%   select(id, time_rel, amt, treatment) conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = max(nca_conc$time_rel),   cmax    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- pk.nca(nca_data)  nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(nca_summary,              digits  = 2,              caption = "PKNCA summary by treatment / interval (auclast in ug*day/mL, Cmax in ug/mL).") nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) %>%   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("auclast", "cmax")) %>%   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) %>%   summarise(median = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = median)  acc_table <- tibble(   regimen = c("225 mg SC Q4W", "675 mg SC Q12W"),   `AR_AUC simulated` = c(     nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W dose 1"],     nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W dose 1"]   ),   `AR_AUC published (median)`  = c(2.43, 1.21),   `AR_Cmax simulated` = c(     nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W dose 1"],     nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W dose 1"]   ),   `AR_Cmax published (median)` = c(2.38, 1.22) )  knitr::kable(acc_table,              digits  = 2,              caption = "Accumulation ratios: simulated medians vs. Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Table 3.")

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Weight distribution. Fiedler-Kelly 2019 publish per-subject weight distribution. sample log-normal around 70.8 kg median SD 0.22 log scale, clipped reported 43.5-131.8 kg range. SC residual error . source paper reports different residual error models IV (proportional, variance 0.0467) SC (combined proportional variance 0.0531 + additive variance 0.204) observations. approved therapeutic route SC vast majority dataset SC, packaged model uses SC combined error model. Users simulating IV administration aware published IV-specific proportional-error reproduced . Vc,SC . source paper estimated separate central volumes IV (Vc,iv = 2.98 L, FIXED) SC (Vc,SC = 1.88 L) administration reconcile biphasic IV profile monophasic SC profile pooled dataset. packaged model uses Vc,SC = 1.88 L primary therapeutic use SC dosing. Simulating IV administration model slightly -represent initial central volume relative source paper. IV dosing exercised vignette. phase 1 IV arm (225 mg 900 mg, 1-hour infusion) supported structurally (omit depot dose, dose directly central) simulated . Race ADA status included simulated covariates retained significant predictors final model.

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Structural model: 2-compartment first-order SC absorption, absorption lag time, first-order elimination, allometric weight scaling CL (exponent 1.05) Vc (exponent 1.53) relative 71 kg reference. Fixed parameters: Q, Vp, F, tlag held fixed source (Section 3.2 rationale: Q Vp estimated IV phase 1 data, F tlag combined IV + SC phase 1 data, fixed pooling phase 2b / 3 trough samples). packaged model preserves wrapping fixed(). IIV: diagonal matrix CL, Vc, ka; source paper reports -diagonal terms estimated. Terminal half-life predicted structural parameters approximately 30 days, matching paper’s reported median.

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Fiedler-Kelly JB, Cohen-Barak O, Morris DN, et al. Population pharmacokinetic modelling simulation fremanezumab healthy subjects patients migraine. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2019;85(12):2721-2733. doi:10.1111/bcp.14096

title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Citation: Grimm HP, Schumacher V, Schafer M, et al. Delivery Brainshuttle amyloid-beta antibody fusion trontinemab non-human primate brain projected efficacious dose regimens humans. mAbs. 2023;15(1):2261509. doi:10.1080/19420862.2023.2261509 Description (trontinemab): Trontinemab PK model non-human primates (Grimm 2023): two-compartment plasma PK Michaelis-Menten elimination brain-region effect-compartment distribution (cerebellum, hippocampus, striatum, cortex, choroid plexus, CSF). Description (gantenerumab): Gantenerumab PK model cynomolgus monkeys (Grimm 2023): two-compartment plasma PK brain extracellular distribution across six brain regions (cerebellum, hippocampus, striatum, cortex, choroid plexus, CSF). Article: https://doi.org/10.1080/19420862.2023.2261509

what: Simulate trontinemab PK in plasma and brain
title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figures 2 3 publication single 10 mg/kg dose cynomolgus monkey.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 10, # dose in mg/kg # nolint: commented_code_linter.     WT = 5, # cynomologus monkey body weight according to the paper     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = c(5 / 60, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, seq(24, 336, by = 24)),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <- dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) Grimm2023Tront <- readModelDb("Grimm_2023_trontinemab") # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimTront <- rxode2::rxSolve(Grimm2023Tront |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = dSimPrep) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfpla_cerebellum', 'etalfpla_hippocampus', 'etalfpla_striatum', 'etalfpla_cortex', 'etalfpla_choroid_plexus' dSimTront$Analyte <- "Trontinemab"

what: Simulate gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain
title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figures 2 3 publication single 20 mg/kg dose cynomolgus monkey.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 20, # dose in mg/kg # nolint: commented_code_linter.     WT = 5, # cynomologus monkey body weight according to the paper     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = c(5 / 60, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, seq(24, 336, by = 24)),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <- dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) Grimm2023Gant <- readModelDb("Grimm_2023_gantenerumab") # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimGant <- rxode2::rxSolve(Grimm2023Gant |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = dSimPrep) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfpla_cerebellum', 'etalfpla_hippocampus', 'etalfpla_striatum', 'etalfpla_cortex', 'etalfpla_choroid_plexus' dSimGant$Analyte <- "Gantenerumab"

title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figure 2 paper.
code: dSim <- bind_rows(dSimTront, dSimGant) dSim$Analyte <- factor(dSim$Analyte, levels = c("Trontinemab", "Gantenerumab"))  ggplot(dSim, aes(x = time, y = sim)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Concentration (ng/mL)"   ) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 336, by = 48)) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1e3, NA)) +   facet_grid(~Analyte)

title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figure 3 paper.
code: d_plot_brain <-   dSim |>   select(time, Analyte, starts_with("C", ignore.case = FALSE)) |>   select(-starts_with("Cbrain"), -Cc) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -c("time", "Analyte"), names_to = "ASPEC", values_to = "AVAL") |>   mutate(     ASPEC =       factor(         gsub(x = ASPEC, pattern = "^C", replacement = ""),         levels = c("hippocampus", "cerebellum", "choroid_plexus", "cortex", "striatum", "csf"),         labels = c("Hippocampus", "Cerebellum", "Choroid Plexus", "Cortex", "Striatum", "Cerebrospinal Fluid")       )   )  ggplot(d_plot_brain, aes(x = time, y = AVAL, colour = Analyte)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Concentration (ng/g)"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 72, 168, 240, 336)) +   facet_wrap(~ASPEC)

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Gupta et al. (2016) characterised amatuximab (MORAb-009) pharmacokinetics 199 patients across four clinical studies (two Phase dose-escalation studies advanced mesothelin-expressing tumours, Phase II pancreatic cancer study, Phase II malignant pleural mesothelioma [MPM] study). final PK model two-compartment structure parallel linear Michaelis-Menten (saturable) elimination central compartment. Covariate analysis retained body weight central volume (power effect, reference 70 kg) antidrug-antibody (ADA) positivity titer threshold > 64 linear clearance (multiplicative effect 1.49). vignette documents parameter provenance source-trace table, builds virtual cohort matching Gupta 2016 Table 1, simulates 5 mg/kg weekly (without interruption) regimen evaluated paper’s dose selection analysis, validates simulated NCA observed Study 003 median Cmin published PK characteristics. Citation: Gupta , Hussein Z, Hassan R, Wustner J, Maltzman JD, Wallin BA. Population pharmacokinetics exposure-response relationship amatuximab, anti-mesothelin monoclonal antibody, patients malignant pleural mesothelioma application dose selection. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 2016;77(4):733-743. Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s00280-016-2984-z

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Gupta 2016 Table 1 (pharmacokinetic-analysis database, N = 199): information available programmatically via readModelDb("Gupta_2016_amatuximab")$population.

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Every numeric value model file inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Gupta_2016_amatuximab.R comes Gupta et al. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol 2016;77(4):733-743 (doi:10.1007/s00280-016-2984-z). CV% values converted log-normal variances via ω2=log(CV2+1)\omega^2 = \log(CV^2 + 1), CL-VcV_c covariance cov=r⋅ωCL2⋅ωVc2=0.411⋅0.056443=0.023198\mathrm{cov} = r \cdot \sqrt{\omega^2_{CL}\cdot\omega^2_{V_c}} = 0.411 \cdot 0.056443 = 0.023198.

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: N = 200 virtual adult cancer patients. Body weight drawn truncated normal distribution matching published mean (75.1 kg) SD (16.4 kg), clipped observed range (35-134 kg). ADA positivity (>64 titer threshold) drawn Bernoulli(0.10) plausible approximation; Gupta 2016 reports ADA reactions hypersensitivity events main driver dose discontinuation publish exact ADA-prevalence distribution PK dataset.
code: set.seed(2016) n_subj <- 200  # Body weight — truncated normal matching Gupta 2016 Table 1 (mean 75.1 kg, # SD 16.4, range 35-134). wt <- rnorm(n_subj, mean = 75.1, sd = 16.4) wt <- pmin(pmax(wt, 35), 134)  # ADA titer > 64 indicator, approximate 10% positivity for illustration. ada_pos <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.10)  pop <- tibble(   ID      = seq_len(n_subj),   WT      = wt,   ADA_POS = ada_pos )  summary(pop$WT) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   35.00   63.82   74.91   75.45   86.55  123.03 mean(pop$ADA_POS) #> [1] 0.115

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: paper’s dose-selection simulation (Results “Simulations dose evaluation”, p. 739) administered amatuximab 5 mg/kg weekly 1-h constant-rate IV infusion six 21-day cycles (interruption), giving 18 doses ~126 days. reproduce schedule, sampling pre-dose trough, end--infusion, 24 h, 72 h, mid-interval NCA.
code: week_h <- 7 * 24 n_cycles <- 6 cycle_h <- 21 * 24 dose_times <- sort(unique(c(   outer((seq_len(n_cycles) - 1) * cycle_h,         c(0, 7 * 24, 14 * 24),         `+`) ))) tmax_h <- max(dose_times) + 14 * 24  # follow-up through 2 wk post last dose  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 24, by = 1),   dose_times + 1, dose_times + 24, dose_times + 72,   dose_times + 168,   seq(0, tmax_h, by = 24) )))  # Build dosing records: 5 mg/kg as 1-h infusion (mg dose = 5 * WT) d_dose <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 5 * WT,     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "central",     RATE = AMT / 1,   # 1-h infusion     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     RATE = 0,     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, RATE, DV, WT, ADA_POS)  stopifnot(sum(d_sim$EVID == 1) == n_subj * length(dose_times))

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Stochastic simulation full IIV (VPC-style plots NCA) typical-value reference pass via rxode2::zeroRe().
code: mod <- readModelDb("Gupta_2016_amatuximab")  set.seed(20160222) sim_full <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvmax' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Gupta 2016 Figure 2 shows observed-vs-predicted concentration-time profile pooled cohort. panel shows first-dose profile (0-168 h) virtual cohort overlaid typical-value trajectory.
code: first_dose <- sim_full |> filter(time <= 168, time > 0) first_typ  <- sim_typ  |> filter(time <= 168, time > 0, id == 1)  ggplot() +   geom_line(     data = first_dose, aes(time, Cc, group = id),     colour = "grey70", alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.25   ) +   geom_line(     data = first_typ, aes(time, Cc), colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 1   ) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x       = "Time after first dose (h)",     y       = expression(Amatuximab~concentration~(mu*g/mL)),     title   = "Single 5 mg/kg IV 1-h infusion - virtual cohort vs typical patient",     caption = "Gupta 2016 Figure 2-equivalent (pre-infusion pcVPC, Phase I panel)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Gupta 2016 (Results “Simulations dose evaluation”, p. 739) simulated weekly 5 mg/kg regimen without interruption. paper reports without ADA, simulated median steady-state CminC_\min 83.1 µg/mL, approximately 80% patients achieved CminC_\min exposure-response threshold 38.2 µg/mL.
code: trough <- sim_full |>   filter(time %in% dose_times) |>   mutate(ADA_label = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA titer > 64", "ADA-negative"))  trough_summary <- trough |>   group_by(time_day, ADA_label) |>   summarise(     Q05 = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(trough_summary, aes(time_day, Q50, colour = ADA_label, fill = ADA_label)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 38.2, linetype = "dashed", colour = "firebrick") +   annotate("text", x = 10, y = 42,            label = "Exposure-response threshold 38.2 ug/mL",            colour = "firebrick", hjust = 0, size = 3) +   labs(     x       = "Time (days)",     y       = expression(Amatuximab~pre-dose~trough~(mu*g/mL)),     colour  = NULL, fill = NULL,     title   = "Simulated amatuximab trough profile, 5 mg/kg weekly without interruption",     caption = "Gupta 2016 Figure 3-equivalent (dose-selection simulation)"   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

what: PKNCA validation at the final-week steady state
title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Run NCA final 168-h (weekly) dosing interval 18-dose course compare simulated CmaxC_\max, CminC_\min, AUC0−τAUC_{0-\tau}, terminal half-life published values. PKNCA formula includes treatment grouping variable (ADA status) results can rolled separately ADA-negative ADA titer > 64 subjects, per library’s PKNCA-recipe convention.
code: tau_h   <- week_h start_ss <- max(dose_times) end_ss   <- start_ss + tau_h  sim_nca <- sim_full |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= start_ss - 1, time <= end_ss + 1) |>   transmute(     id        = id,     time      = time,     Cc        = Cc,     treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA titer > 64", "ADA-negative")   )  dose_nca <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 1) |>   transmute(     id        = ID,     time      = TIME,     amt       = AMT,     treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA titer > 64", "ADA-negative")   )  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_nca, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start       = start_ss,   end         = end_ss,   cmax        = TRUE,   tmax        = TRUE,   cmin        = TRUE,   auclast     = TRUE,   cav         = TRUE,   half.life   = TRUE )  res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) nca_summary <- as.data.frame(res$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "cmin", "auclast", "cav", "half.life")) |>   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) |>   summarise(     median = stats::median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     p05    = stats::quantile(PPORRES, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     p95    = stats::quantile(PPORRES, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) nca_summary #> # A tibble: 12 × 5 #>    treatment      PPTESTCD   median     p05     p95 #>    <chr>          <chr>       <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #>  1 ADA titer > 64 auclast    6732.  3575.    9789.  #>  2 ADA titer > 64 cav          40.1   21.3     58.3 #>  3 ADA titer > 64 cmax        102.    67.2    151.  #>  4 ADA titer > 64 cmin         12.9    2.35    24.0 #>  5 ADA titer > 64 half.life    68.6   24.1     91.1 #>  6 ADA titer > 64 tmax          1      1        1   #>  7 ADA-negative   auclast   10346.  5658.   20712.  #>  8 ADA-negative   cav          61.6   33.7    123.  #>  9 ADA-negative   cmax        117.    79.6    208.  #> 10 ADA-negative   cmin         32.1    7.95    81.8 #> 11 ADA-negative   half.life   108.    54.4    168.  #> 12 ADA-negative   tmax          1      1        1

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Interpretation: model reproduces directionality ADA effect magnitude (simulated CminC_\min ratio ADA-positive : ADA-negative closely matches paper’s reported ratio ~26.9 / 83.1 = 0.32, consistent 1/1.49 fold clearance increase). absolute simulated CminC_\min values systematically lower paper’s published stochastic simulation values — see “Assumptions deviations” likely cause (published Manly eta transformation VmaxV_\max re-implemented , paper’s stochastic median may influenced heavy upper tail log-normal VmaxV_\max distribution ω=1.24\omega = 1.24).
code: sim_cmin_ss <- sim_full |>   filter(time == start_ss) |>   group_by(ADA_POS) |>   summarise(median_cmin = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  sim_cmin_negative <- sim_cmin_ss |> filter(ADA_POS == 0) |> pull(median_cmin) sim_cmin_positive <- sim_cmin_ss |> filter(ADA_POS == 1) |> pull(median_cmin)  comparison <- tibble::tribble(   ~metric,                                                ~published, ~simulated,       ~units,   "SS Cmin, 5 mg/kg QW, ADA-negative (paper simulation)", 83.1,       sim_cmin_negative, "ug/mL",   "SS Cmin, 5 mg/kg QW, ADA titer > 64 (paper simulation)", 26.9,    sim_cmin_positive, "ug/mL",   "Observed median Cmin, Study 003 MPM (5 mg/kg D1+D8 of 21-day cycle)", 38.2, NA_real_, "ug/mL",   "Exposure-response threshold for OS",                    38.2,      NA_real_,           "ug/mL" ) comparison #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   metric                                               published simulated units #>   <chr>                                                    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr> #> 1 SS Cmin, 5 mg/kg QW, ADA-negative (paper simulation)      83.1      32.1 ug/mL #> 2 SS Cmin, 5 mg/kg QW, ADA titer > 64 (paper simulati…      26.9      12.9 ug/mL #> 3 Observed median Cmin, Study 003 MPM (5 mg/kg D1+D8 …      38.2      NA   ug/mL #> 4 Exposure-response threshold for OS                        38.2      NA   ug/mL

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Manly transformation ηVmax\eta_{V_\max}. Gupta 2016 applied Manly transformation shape factor λ=−0.181\lambda = -0.181 (95% CI -0.450 0.0875, .e., statistically distinguishable zero / log-normal) eta VmaxV_\max. vignette uses standard log-normal form nlmixr2 without Manly reshaping. Since λ\lambda’s CI includes zero, first-order-equivalent approximation; simulated marginal VmaxV_\max distribution differs upper tail. paper’s Table 2 CV 124% treated log-normal CV converted via ω2=log(CV2+1)\omega^2 = \log(CV^2 + 1). Absolute CminC_\min offset vs paper’s dose-evaluation simulation. paper’s dose-evaluation sim reports median weekly-regimen CminC_\min 83.1 µg/mL (ADA) 26.9 µg/mL (ADA >64). vignette’s typical-value simulation lower (around 30 µg/mL -ADA), stochastic medians virtual cohort track typical value closely. Possible contributors: paper’s Manly transformation (produces heavier-tailed VmaxV_\max distribution pure log-normal) can raise median predicted CminC_\min due right-tail mass low-VmaxV_\max subjects; paper report residual baseline demographic distribution (weight, ADA prevalence) used inside 199-patient dose-evaluation stochastic cohort, vignette supplies plausible defaults. observed median CminC_\min MPM Study 003 (38.2 µg/mL, intermittent D1+D8 21-day cycle dosing) closer magnitude simulation . ADA prevalence. exact ADA-positivity prevalence inside 199-patient PK cohort reported; vignette assumes 10% prevalence illustration. Replace Bernoulli probability virtual-pop exact prevalence applying specific population. Intermittent vs continuous weekly dosing. paper distinguishes observed data (D1 + D8 21-day cycle MPM patients) simulated weekly-without-interruption regimen used dose-selection analysis. vignette implements continuous weekly regimen (match paper’s Figure 3 dose-evaluation simulation). Race, age, ECOG, albumin, baseline mesothelin. Gupta 2016 tested covariates retain final PK model (Results, “Effect covariates”). simulated . pcVPC replication (Gupta 2016 Figure 1). reproduced exactly published figure uses observed (irregular) sampling design across four studies prediction-correction individual-covariate-predicted concentration. Figure 1-equivalent panel shows analogous single-dose PK shape axes.

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Gupta , Hussein Z, Hassan R, Wustner J, Maltzman JD, Wallin BA. Population pharmacokinetics exposure-response relationship amatuximab, anti-mesothelin monoclonal antibody, patients malignant pleural mesothelioma application dose selection. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 2016;77(4):733-743. doi:10.1007/s00280-016-2984-z

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Replicate Figure 2 Hu et al. (2026): prediction-corrected visual predictive check (VPC) showing clesrovimab serum concentrations 150 days single 105 mg intramuscular dose, presented overall population panel panel stratified baseline body weight category. original study data (5,850 PK samples 2,942 infants across three trials) publicly available, vignette simulates virtual infant population using covariate distributions approximate study demographics (median age 3.02 months, median weight 5.4 kg, preterm full-term infants). Body weight trajectories follow-generated using weight--age growth standards (combined sex, 0–10 months).

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: weight--age LMS parameters combined sex, 0–10 months (Multicentre Growth Reference Study Group, 2006). LMS formula : weight = M × (1 + L × S × z)^(1/L)
code: who_lms <- data.frame(   age_mo = 0:10,   L = c(0.3487, 0.2297, 0.1970, 0.1738, 0.1553, 0.1395,     0.1257, 0.1125, 0.0998, 0.0875, 0.0756),   M = c(3.3464, 4.4709, 5.5675, 6.3762, 7.0023, 7.5105,     7.9340, 8.2970, 8.6151, 8.9014, 9.1649),   S = c(0.14602, 0.13395, 0.12385, 0.11876, 0.11535, 0.11254,     0.11056, 0.10947, 0.10868, 0.10814, 0.10765) )  # Returns weight (kg) for a given age (months) and individual z-score # using linear interpolation of LMS parameters between whole-month values who_weight <- function(age_mo, z) {   age_mo <- pmax(0, pmin(age_mo, 10))   L <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$L, xout = age_mo)$y   M <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$M, xout = age_mo)$y   S <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$S, xout = age_mo)$y   M * (1 + L * S * z)^(1 / L) }

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Sample N = 500 virtual infants covariate distributions approximating study population.
code: set.seed(1654) # clesrovimab MK-1654 n_subj <- 500  # Gestational age: 85% full-term (37-42 wk), 15% preterm (32-37 wk) is_preterm <- runif(n_subj) < 0.15 GA <- ifelse(is_preterm, runif(n_subj, 32, 37), runif(n_subj, 37, 42))  # Postnatal age at dosing (months); study enrolled infants <= 3 months PNA_0 <- runif(n_subj, 0, 3)  # Individual weight z-score (fixed percentile across follow-up) # Truncated to ±2 SD to avoid extreme weights wt_z <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n_subj, 0, 1)))  # Race: approximate study demographics race_draw <- runif(n_subj) RACE_ASIAN <- as.integer(race_draw < 0.15) RACE_BLACK <- as.integer(race_draw >= 0.15 & race_draw < 0.35) RACE_MULTI <- as.integer(race_draw >= 0.35 & race_draw < 0.40) # White/Other: race_draw >= 0.40 (reference; all indicators = 0)  pop <- data.frame(   ID         = seq_len(n_subj),   GA         = GA,   PNA_0      = PNA_0,   wt_z       = wt_z,   RACE_ASIAN = RACE_ASIAN,   RACE_BLACK = RACE_BLACK,   RACE_MULTI = RACE_MULTI )  # Baseline weight for stratification pop$WT_base <- who_weight(pop$PNA_0, pop$wt_z) pop$wt_group <- cut(   pop$WT_base,   breaks = c(0, 4, 6, Inf),   labels = c("< 4 kg", "4\u20136 kg", "\u2265 6 kg"),   right  = FALSE )

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Observation times every 7 days 0 150 days, plus dose event time 0.
code: obs_times_day <- seq(0, 150, by = 7)  # Dose records (one per subject at time 0) d_dose <- pop |>   mutate(     TIME = 0,     AMT  = 105, # mg, single IM dose     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA   )  # Observation records with time-varying WT and PNA d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_subj), each = length(obs_times_day)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(obs_times_day, times = n_subj),     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA,     PNA  = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44, # postnatal age in months     WT   = who_weight(PNA, wt_z) # time-varying weight from WHO curves   )  # Dose records also need WT and PNA at time 0 d_dose <- d_dose |>   mutate(     PNA = PNA_0,     WT  = who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PNA, GA, RACE_ASIAN, RACE_BLACK, RACE_MULTI,     wt_group, wt_z)

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Hu_2026_clesrovimab")  # Simulate: rxSolve uses the N=500 subjects' IIV from the model ini() block. # Setting seed for reproducibility. set.seed(2026) sim_out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: # Attach baseline weight group to simulation output wt_grp_map <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 0, TIME == 0) |>   select(ID, wt_group) |>   distinct()  sim_plot <- sim_out |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0) |> # exclude pre-dose time point   left_join(wt_grp_map, by = c("id" = "ID"))  # Overall population quantiles d_overall <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(panel = "Overall")  # Weight-stratified quantiles d_strat <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time, wt_group) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   rename(panel = wt_group)

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: ggplot(d_overall, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10(     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 30)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = "Clesrovimab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Overall population\nMedian and 90% prediction interval"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 2 — Stratified by baseline body weight
title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: ggplot(d_strat, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~panel, nrow = 1) +   scale_y_log10(     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 60)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = "Clesrovimab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "By baseline body weight category\nMedian and 90% prediction interval"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Virtual population: 500 infants GA, postnatal age, weight percentile, race sampled approximate three-study population (MK-1654-002, CLEVER, SMART). Time-varying weight: Individual weight trajectories generated using weight--age growth standards (LMS method), subject assigned fixed z-score (growth percentile) enrollment. IIV: Simulated using published omega^2 values Ka (23.5% CV), CL/F (14.4% CV), Vc/F (8.12% CV; note high shrinkage 71.9% original analysis). Q/F Vp/F IIV final model. Residual error: Combined proportional (14.3%) additive (0.231 µg/mL). prediction intervals reflect inter-individual variability residual error final population PK model.

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Hu Z, Hellmann F, Zang X, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Clesrovimab Preterm Full-Term Infants. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2026;119(4):1036-1046. doi:10.1002/cpt.70199

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Citation: Hua F, Ribbing J, Reinisch W, Cataldi F, Martin S. pharmacokinetic comparison anrukinzumab, anti-IL-13 monoclonal antibody, among healthy volunteers, asthma ulcerative colitis patients. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2015;80(1):101-109. Article: doi:10.1111/bcp.12589 Publicly available full text: https://bpspubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/bcp.12589 Anrukinzumab (IMA-638) humanized IgG1 anti-IL-13 monoclonal antibody. Hua et al. pooled PK data five clinical studies (N = 255) across four populations — healthy volunteers, mild--moderate asthma, moderate--severe asthma, ulcerative colitis (UC) — identify disease demographic covariates mAb PK parameters. final model two-compartment structure first-order SC absorption linear elimination, body weight baseline albumin demographic covariates UC disease status (CL) moderate--severe asthma status (SC bioavailability F) disease-state covariates.

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Hua 2015 Table 2 summarises pooled cohort 255 subjects across 5 studies: Sex: 65% male, 35% female. Race: 73% White, 12% Black, 12% Asian, 3% . Age: median 37 years (mean 38, SD 13). Body weight: median 81.3 kg (mean 82.6, SD 18.7). Disease state: healthy volunteers 17%, mild--moderate asthma 20%, moderate--severe asthma 38%, ulcerative colitis 25%. Dosing route: SC 72%, IV 28%. Per-study design (Hua 2015 Table 1): information available programmatically via readModelDb("Hua_2015_anrukinzumab")$population.

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Hua_2015_anrukinzumab.R. table collects one place review.

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: DIS_UC DIS_SASTHMA newly ratified inst/references/covariate-columns.md (2026-04-21) scope-specific binary indicators decomposed paper’s four-level disease-state categorical (healthy volunteer, mild--moderate asthma, moderate--severe asthma, UC).

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Original per-subject data publicly available. virtual population approximates pooled Table 2 demographics Table 1 disease-state mixture.
code: set.seed(2015) n_subj <- 400  pop <- tibble(   ID  = seq_len(n_subj),   WT  = rlnorm(n_subj, log(81.3), 0.22),               # median 81.3, SD ~20   ALB = pmax(2.8, pmin(rnorm(n_subj, 4.3, 0.4), 5.5))  # mean 4.3, SD 0.4 g/dL )  # Disease-state assignment matching Hua 2015 Table 2 proportions. pop$disease <- sample(   c("HV", "mild_moderate_asthma", "moderate_severe_asthma", "UC"),   size    = n_subj,   replace = TRUE,   prob    = c(0.17, 0.20, 0.38, 0.25) ) pop$DIS_UC      <- as.integer(pop$disease == "UC") pop$DIS_SASTHMA <- as.integer(pop$disease == "moderate_severe_asthma")

what: Simulation setup: single SC dose (healthy + asthma cohort)
title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Replicates profile shape Hua 2015 Figure 4E-F (single-dose SC healthy volunteers mild--moderate asthma) Figure 4D (single 0.6 mg/kg SC moderate--severe asthma).
code: pop_sc <- pop %>% filter(disease != "UC")  dose_sc <- pop_sc %>%   mutate(     TIME = 0,     AMT  = round(2 * WT, 1),   # 2 mg/kg SC, consistent with study 1 and 3     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, DIS_UC, DIS_SASTHMA, disease)  obs_times_sc <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 24, by = 3),   seq(24, 24 * 7, by = 12),   seq(24 * 7, 24 * 56, by = 24) )))  obs_sc <- pop_sc %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times_sc) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = NA_real_,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, DIS_UC, DIS_SASTHMA, disease)  d_sc <- bind_rows(dose_sc, obs_sc) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) mod <- readModelDb("Hua_2015_anrukinzumab") sim_sc <- rxSolve(mod, d_sc, keep = "disease", returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

previous_headings: Simulation setup: single SC dose (healthy + asthma cohort)
title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
code: sim_sc %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   mutate(week = time / (24 * 7)) %>%   group_by(week, disease) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = week, color = disease, fill = disease)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, linetype = 0) +   geom_line(aes(y = median), linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since dose (weeks)",     y = "Anrukinzumab concentration (ng/mL)",     title = "Simulated 2 mg/kg SC single dose, stratified by disease state",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval; replicates the profile shape of Hua 2015 Fig. 4E,F,D",     caption = "Reduced F in moderate-to-severe asthma depresses exposure at matched dose."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Simulation setup: 200 mg IV repeat dosing (UC vs matched non-UC)
title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Replicates Figure 5 Hua 2015: five 200 mg IV doses day 0, weeks 2, 4, 8, 12, comparing UC patients covariate-matched non-UC subjects. paper holds WT ALB distributions matched difference UC disease status; .
code: n_iv <- 100  pop_uc <- tibble(   ID  = seq_len(n_iv),   WT  = rlnorm(n_iv, log(75), 0.18),   ALB = pmax(2.8, pmin(rnorm(n_iv, 4.3, 0.3), 5.5)) ) %>%   crossing(group = c("non-UC", "UC")) %>%   mutate(     ID          = (as.integer(factor(group)) - 1L) * n_iv + ID,     DIS_UC      = as.integer(group == "UC"),     DIS_SASTHMA = 0L   )  dose_weeks_iv <- c(0, 2, 4, 8, 12) dose_times_iv <- dose_weeks_iv * 7 * 24  # hours  dose_iv <- pop_uc %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times_iv) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = 200,        # 200 mg IV     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, DIS_UC, DIS_SASTHMA, group)  obs_times_iv <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 24, by = 4),   seq(24, 24 * 7 * 16, by = 24) )))  obs_iv <- pop_uc %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times_iv) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = NA_real_,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, DIS_UC, DIS_SASTHMA, group)  d_iv <- bind_rows(dose_iv, obs_iv) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) sim_iv <- rxSolve(mod, d_iv, keep = "group", returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'
