what: Replicates Figure 5 (200 mg IV, UC vs non-UC)
title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
code: sim_iv %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   mutate(week = time / (24 * 7)) %>%   group_by(week, group) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = week, color = group, fill = group)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, linetype = 0) +   geom_line(aes(y = median), linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("non-UC" = "#2f7f4f", "UC" = "#c25252")) +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c("non-UC" = "#2f7f4f", "UC" = "#c25252")) +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y = "Anrukinzumab concentration (ng/mL)",     title = "Simulated 200 mg IV (day 0, wk 2, 4, 8, 12): UC vs non-UC",     subtitle = "Median and 95% prediction interval; replicates Hua 2015 Fig. 5.",     caption = "UC patients show +72.8% CL and lower exposure at matched WT/ALB."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Run PKNCA first 28-day single-dose interval 200 mg IV cohort, stratified group (UC vs non-UC). PKNCA formula includes group treatment variable Cmax, AUClast, half-life computed per-group. PKNCA summary single 200 mg IV dose (first 28 days), stratified UC vs non-UC.
code: single_dose_obs <- sim_iv %>%   filter(time >= 0, time <= 28 * 24, Cc > 0) %>%   transmute(     ID  = id,     time,     Cc,     treatment = group   )  single_dose_df <- pop_uc %>%   mutate(TIME = 0, AMT = 200) %>%   transmute(ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT, treatment = group)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(single_dose_obs, Cc ~ time | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(single_dose_df,  amt ~ time | treatment + ID)  data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 28 * 24,          # 28 days in hours     cmax      = TRUE,     tmax      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE,     half.life = TRUE   ) ) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #> Warning: Too few points for half-life calculation (min.hl.points=3 with only 0 points) #> Too few points for title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Hua 2015 reports median half-life non-UC subjects 567 h (95% CI 548-586) UC subjects 328 h (95% CI 301-352) (page 105 / Figure 5 caption). computed model’s terminal-phase parameters CL Vss = Vc + Vp, matched WT ALB: Analytic terminal-phase half-life model’s typical-value parameters vs Hua 2015 reported medians. analytic values differ sparse-sampling NCA output NCA estimates half-life whatever time window simulated concentrations span; paper’s 567 h / 328 h values come population model model-file parameters supposed reproduce.
code: vss_nonuc <- 3.81 + 2.17 t_half_nonuc <- log(2) * vss_nonuc / 0.00732 t_half_uc    <- log(2) * vss_nonuc / (0.00732 * (1 + 0.728))  tibble(   group     = c("non-UC", "UC"),   t_half_h  = c(t_half_nonuc, t_half_uc),   paper_h   = c(567, 328) ) %>%   knitr::kable(     digits  = 0,     caption = "Analytic terminal-phase half-life from the model's typical-value parameters vs Hua 2015 reported medians."   )

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Subject-level covariates: Hua 2015 publish per-subject WT, ALB, disease-state values. WT sampled log-normal around reported Table 2 mean 82.6 kg / SD 18.7 kg; ALB sampled normal around mean 4.3 g/dL (Table 3 reference value), clipped clinical range [2.8, 5.5] g/dL. Albumin units: paper state units explicitly Table 3; value 4.3 Figure 1 x-axis range (3.0-5.0) consistent g/dL (US convention). recorded covariateData[[ALB]]$units per-parameter comment. Full-dose regimen UC: simulate 200 mg arm (day 0, weeks 2, 4, 8, 12) Hua 2015 Figure 5 plots. paper’s study-5 protocol also tested 400 mg 600 mg doses; concentration-time shape dose-proportional linear model, single dose level demonstrates UC-vs-non-UC contrast. Time-varying covariates: WT ALB treated time-invariant baseline values, consistent typical IgG1 PK modelling Hua 2015’s “baseline” covariate terminology. NCA half-life vs typical-value half-life: PKNCA sparse-sampling output differs analytic CL/V-based half-life; “Comparison published values” table reports analytic value, model-file parameters meant reproduce.

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Hua F, Ribbing J, Reinisch W, Cataldi F, Martin S. pharmacokinetic comparison anrukinzumab, anti-IL-13 monoclonal antibody, among healthy volunteers, asthma ulcerative colitis patients. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2015;80(1):101-109. doi:10.1111/bcp.12589

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Simulate ixekizumab serum concentration–time profiles using final paediatric population PK model Jackson et al. (2022) patients aged 6 <18 years moderate--severe plaque psoriasis enrolled IXORA-PEDS (NCT03073200). model two-compartment linear PK model first-order subcutaneous absorption, allometric body-weight effect CL, Q, V2, V3, log-linear ADA-titre effect CL. Citation: Jackson K, Chua L, Velez de Mendizabal N, et al. Population pharmacokinetic exposure-efficacy analysis ixekizumab paediatric patients moderate--severe plaque psoriasis (IXORA-PEDS). Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2022;88(3):1074-1086. doi:10.1111/bcp.15034 Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.15034

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: IXORA-PEDS Phase 3, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial conducted paediatric patients aged 6 <18 years moderate--severe plaque psoriasis (PASI ≥ 12, sPGA ≥ 3, BSA ≥ 10% screening baseline). population PK analysis dataset comprised 558 measurable ixekizumab serum concentrations 184 patients: 4 patients <25 kg (2.2%), 45 patients 25–50 kg (24.5%), 135 patients >50 kg (73.4%). Overall baseline age ranged 6–17 years (median 15 years >50 kg group, 10 years 25–50 kg group, 7 years <25 kg group), baseline weight 21.5–136 kg, 56.5% female. majority white (81.5%), 3.26% Black/African American, 3.26% Asian, 1.63% American Indian Alaska Native, 7.61% , 2.72% missing race. Regions: US 37.5%, Europe 41.3%, Rest World 21.2%. Baseline demographics reproduced Jackson 2022 Table 1. approved weight-based Q4W regimens 20 mg (<25 kg), 40 mg (25–50 kg), 80 mg (>50 kg), initial 40/80/160 mg loading dose respectively. Programmatic access: readModelDb("Jackson_2022_ixekizumab")$population.

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: IXORA-PEDS patient-level data publicly available, figures use virtual paediatric cohort whose weight, weight-group, ADA distributions approximate Jackson 2022 Table 1 ADA incidence reported Results section 3.1. Simulated cohort summary (compare Jackson 2022 Table 1 weight-group means Results 3.1 ADA incidence).
code: set.seed(20220301) # IXORA-PEDS paper month n_subj <- 184 # match dataset size  # Weight-group proportions from Jackson 2022 Table 1 (overall column) # <25 kg: 4/184 = 2.2%; 25-50 kg: 45/184 = 24.5%; >50 kg: 135/184 = 73.4% u <- runif(n_subj) weight_group <- cut(   u,   breaks = c(-Inf, 4 / 184, 49 / 184, Inf),   labels = c("<25 kg", "25-50 kg", ">50 kg"),   right  = TRUE )  # Sample body weights inside each group to match the reported mean +/- SD # from Jackson 2022 Table 1. Clip to the reported per-group ranges. simulate_weight <- function(group) {   switch(as.character(group),     "<25 kg"   = pmin(22.6, pmax(21.5, rnorm(1, 21.9, 0.520))),     "25-50 kg" = pmin(50.0, pmax(25.0, rnorm(1, 37.7, 8.10))),     ">50 kg"   = pmin(136.0, pmax(50.1, rnorm(1, 71.3, 19.5)))   ) } WT <- vapply(weight_group, simulate_weight, numeric(1))  # ADA titre: 85.8% ADA-negative (ADA_TITER = 1); 14.2% TE-ADA-positive with # titres in three bands per Results section 3.1: #   - Low    (<1:160)        : 52.6% of ADA+ samples #   - Moderate (>=1:160, <1:1280): 38.5% #   - High     (>=1:1280)    :  9.0% ada_pos <- runif(n_subj) < 0.142 ada_titre <- rep(1, n_subj) n_pos <- sum(ada_pos) if (n_pos > 0) {   band <- sample(c("low", "mod", "high"),     size    = n_pos,     replace = TRUE,     prob    = c(0.526, 0.385, 0.090)   )   titre_sample <- function(b) {     switch(b,       low  = sample(c(10, 20, 40, 80), 1),          # 1:10 to <1:160       mod  = sample(c(160, 320, 640), 1),           # 1:160 to <1:1280       high = sample(c(1280, 2560), 1)               # >=1:1280     )   }   ada_titre[ada_pos] <- vapply(band, titre_sample, numeric(1)) }  cohort <- tibble(   ID           = seq_len(n_subj),   weight_group = weight_group,   WT           = WT,   ADA_TITER    = ada_titre )  summary_tbl <- cohort |>   group_by(weight_group) |>   summarise(     n           = dplyr::n(),     mean_WT     = mean(WT),     median_WT   = median(WT),     pct_ada_pos = 100 * mean(ADA_TITER > 1),     .groups     = "drop"   ) knitr::kable(summary_tbl, digits = 2,              caption = "Simulated cohort summary (compare with Jackson 2022 Table 1 weight-group means and Results 3.1 ADA incidence).")

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Weight-based Q4W regimen per IXORA-PEDS: loading dose Week 0 (40/80/160 mg <25/25–50/>50 kg), maintenance Q4W (20/40/80 mg). Simulate Week 24 (6 maintenance doses) trough Week 12 (co-primary endpoint timepoint) reflects steady state. Times hours (model unit).
code: # Weeks -> hours conversion hours_per_week <- 7 * 24  maint_doses <- function(group) {   switch(as.character(group), "<25 kg" = 20, "25-50 kg" = 40, ">50 kg" = 80) } load_doses <- function(group) {   switch(as.character(group), "<25 kg" = 40, "25-50 kg" = 80, ">50 kg" = 160) }  dose_weeks    <- c(0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20) dose_times_hr <- dose_weeks * hours_per_week obs_weeks     <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 24, by = 0.5), 1, 9))) # dense early + the Week 1 and Week 9 PK addendum timepoints obs_times_hr  <- obs_weeks * hours_per_week  d_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(dose_idx = seq_along(dose_times_hr)) |>   mutate(     TIME = dose_times_hr[dose_idx],     AMT  = ifelse(dose_idx == 1,                   vapply(weight_group, load_doses, numeric(1)),                   vapply(weight_group, maint_doses, numeric(1))),     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA_real_   ) |>   select(-dose_idx)  d_obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times_hr) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   as.data.frame()

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Jackson_2022_ixekizumab") set.seed(42) sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Figure 1 — Mean ixekizumab concentration vs time by weight group (Weeks 0–12)
title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Jackson 2022 Figure 1 plots mean (±SD) trough-observed concentrations 25–50 kg >50 kg groups Week 12. simulated curves show mean (5th/95th percentile ribbon) simulated ixekizumab concentrations window, stratified baseline weight group.
code: sim_plot <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   left_join(cohort |> select(ID, weight_group), by = c("id" = "ID")) |>   filter(time > 0, time <= 12 * hours_per_week, !is.na(Cc)) |>   mutate(week = time / hours_per_week)  fig1 <- sim_plot |>   group_by(weight_group, week) |>   summarise(     mean_Cc = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     Q05     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fig1, aes(x = week, y = mean_Cc, colour = weight_group, fill = weight_group)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_continuous() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 4)) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Ixekizumab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Figure 1 replication: mean concentration by weight group",     subtitle = "Simulated Weeks 0\u201312 (loading + Q4W maintenance); mean and 5\u201395th percentile",     caption = "Replicates Figure 1 of Jackson 2022."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 3A — Week 12 trough concentration vs body weight
title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
code: wk12_time <- 12 * hours_per_week sim_wk12 <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(abs(time - wk12_time) < 1e-6) |>   left_join(cohort |> select(ID, weight_group, WT), by = c("id" = "ID"))  ggplot(sim_wk12, aes(x = WT, y = Cc, colour = weight_group)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Baseline body weight (kg)",     y = "Week 12 trough concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Figure 3A replication: Week 12 model-predicted trough vs body weight",     caption = "Replicates Figure 3A of Jackson 2022."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Steady-state trough by weight group (Weeks 8 and 12)
title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Jackson 2022 Results section 3.1 reports mean trough concentrations predicted steady state (Weeks 8 12) 25–50 kg >50 kg groups range 3.20–3.33 µg/mL. Insufficient PK data available summarise <25 kg group. Simulated mean trough concentrations Weeks 8 12. Compare Jackson 2022 Results 3.1: reported means 3.20–3.33 μg/mL 25–50 kg >50 kg groups; insufficient data <25 kg group.
code: trough_weeks <- c(8, 12) trough_times <- trough_weeks * hours_per_week  trough_tbl <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time %in% trough_times) |>   left_join(cohort |> select(ID, weight_group), by = c("id" = "ID")) |>   group_by(weight_group, week = time / hours_per_week) |>   summarise(     mean_trough  = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     sd_trough    = sd(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     median_trough = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups      = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(trough_tbl, digits = 2,              caption = paste(                "Simulated mean trough concentrations at Weeks 8 and 12.",                "Compare with Jackson 2022 Results 3.1: reported means 3.20\u20133.33 \u03bcg/mL",                "in the 25\u201350 kg and >50 kg groups; insufficient data in the <25 kg group."              ))

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Run PKNCA steady-state dosing interval Week 8 Week 12 doses (.e., Week 8 dose Week 12 dose, 4-week window). interval Jackson 2022 reports steady-state trough concentrations. Simulated steady-state (Week 8–Week 12) NCA weight group. Cmin trough Week 12; compare Jackson 2022 Results 3.1: reported mean trough 3.20–3.33 μg/mL 25–50 >50 kg groups.
code: ss_start <- 8  * hours_per_week ss_end   <- 12 * hours_per_week  nca_conc <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, Cc > 0) |>   left_join(cohort |> select(ID, weight_group), by = c("id" = "ID")) |>   mutate(time_rel = time - ss_start,          treatment = paste0("IXE_Q4W_", weight_group)) |>   rename(ID = id) |>   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- cohort |>   mutate(time_rel = 0,          AMT      = vapply(weight_group, maint_doses, numeric(1)),          treatment = paste0("IXE_Q4W_", weight_group)) |>   select(ID, time_rel, AMT, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID)  data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start   = 0,     end     = 4 * hours_per_week,     cmax    = TRUE,     tmax    = TRUE,     cmin    = TRUE,     auclast = TRUE,     cav     = TRUE   ) )  nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = paste(     "Simulated steady-state (Week 8\u2013Week 12) NCA by weight group.",     "Cmin is the trough at Week 12; compare with Jackson 2022 Results 3.1:",     "reported mean trough 3.20\u20133.33 \u03bcg/mL in the 25\u201350 and >50 kg groups."   ) )

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Week 12 mean trough: simulated vs Jackson 2022 Results 3.1. Simulated Week 12 mean troughs track published 3.20–3.33 µg/mL range within reasonable margin; residual difference driven differences virtual cohort’s weight distribution actual IXORA-PEDS cohort.
code: pub <- tibble(   weight_group = c("<25 kg", "25-50 kg", ">50 kg"),   published_mean_trough = c(     NA_real_,  # insufficient data in <25 kg group     mean(c(3.20, 3.33)),     mean(c(3.20, 3.33))   ),   published_range = c(     "Insufficient data",     "3.20-3.33",     "3.20-3.33"   ) ) sim_trough <- trough_tbl |>   filter(week == 12) |>   select(weight_group, simulated_mean_trough = mean_trough)  comparison <- pub |>   left_join(sim_trough, by = "weight_group") |>   mutate(pct_diff = 100 * (simulated_mean_trough - published_mean_trough) / published_mean_trough)  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 2,              caption = "Week 12 mean trough: simulated vs Jackson 2022 Results 3.1.")

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Patient-level data unavailable. Weight distributions within group drawn Jackson 2022 Table 1 mean ± SD clipped reported range; actual IXORA-PEDS per-subject weights published. ADA-titre sampling. 14.2% patients flagged TE-ADA positive per Results section 3.1; per-band split (52.6% low / 38.5% moderate / 9.0% high titre) applies paper’s band-level percentages uniformly patient level, whereas paper reports sample level. ADA-negative patients (85.8%) carry ADA_TITER = 1 log-linear effect zero. Time-varying ADA modelled. paper uses time-matched ADA titre publish per-subject longitudinal trajectories; virtual subject carries single titre full follow-. Baseline weight . Jackson 2022 used baseline weight (mean percent change 1.6% first 12 weeks; 23% ≥±10% change 108 weeks). vignette uses single baseline weight per subject, matching paper. Residual error central compartment. paper reports one proportional residual error (27.7%); additive residual error reported added . Site injection modelled. paper evaluated injection site covariate retain final paediatric model. Dosing regimen. IXORA-PEDS used approved Q4W regimens (loading dose followed Q4W maintenance). Ongoing open-label periods beyond Week 12 simulated beyond Week 24.

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Jackson K, Chua L, Velez de Mendizabal N, et al. Population pharmacokinetic exposure-efficacy analysis ixekizumab paediatric patients moderate--severe plaque psoriasis (IXORA-PEDS). Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2022;88(3):1074-1086. doi:10.1111/bcp.15034

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Astegolimab anti-ST2 IgG2 monoclonal antibody developed severe asthma. Kotani et al. (2022) fit two-compartment population PK model first-order subcutaneous (SC) absorption first-order elimination Zenyatta (NCT02918019) Phase 2b data 368 adult patients. Structurally, model estimates apparent clearance (CL/F), central volume (Vc/F), peripheral volume (Vp/F), intercompartmental clearance (Q/F), absorption rate (ka). Shared allometric exponents body weight fit {CL, Q} {Vc, Vp}. Baseline CRCL (MDRD eGFR) baseline blood eosinophil count enter CL power covariates, binary indicator 70 mg dose arm scales relative bioavailability (Frel) −15.3% Box-Cox transformed IIV per Petersson et al. (2009). Citation: Kotani N, Dolton M, Svensson RJ, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Exposure-Response Relationships Astegolimab Patients Severe Asthma. J Clin Pharmacol. 2022;62(7):905-917. doi:10.1002/jcph.2021 Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/jcph.2021

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: PK analysis data set comprises 368 adults severe asthma medium- high-dose inhaled corticosteroids plus least one additional controller least one exacerbation prior 12 months. Median baseline characteristics (Table 1): age 53 years (18–75), body weight 79 kg (43–130), CRCL 87.9 mL/min/1.73 m² (MDRD eGFR), blood eosinophil count 180 cells/µL. 66.8% female; 84.0% White, 5.4% Black, 4.6% Asian, 4.6% Native American, 1.4% multiple races. Treatment-emergent anti-drug antibodies observed 7.3% patients. Patients randomized 70, 210, 490 mg SC every 4 weeks (Q4W) 52 weeks. population metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Kotani_2022_astegolimab")$population.

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: parameter values functional forms come Kotani et al. (2022) J Clin Pharmacol 62(7):905-917. -file comments next ini() entry record row-level source; table collects .

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Individual-level Zenyatta data public. Simulate 450 virtual patients (150 per dose arm) whose covariate distributions approximate Table 1.
code: set.seed(2022) n_per_dose <- 150 doses <- c(70, 210, 490) n_subj <- n_per_dose * length(doses)  pop <- tibble(   ID        = seq_len(n_subj),   dose_mg   = rep(doses, each = n_per_dose),   # Body weight ~ lognormal matching median 79 kg and range 43-130 kg   WT        = pmin(130, pmax(43, rlnorm(n_subj, log(79), 0.22))),   # CRCL (MDRD eGFR) ~ normal matching median 87.9; clip to observed physiological range   CRCL      = pmin(160, pmax(30, rnorm(n_subj, 87.9, 20))),   # Blood eosinophils: right-skewed, median 180 cells/uL   EOS      = pmin(2000, pmax(5, rlnorm(n_subj, log(180), 0.9))),   DOSE_70MG = as.integer(dose_mg == 70) ) pop$treatment <- factor(   paste0(pop$dose_mg, " mg Q4W"),   levels = c("70 mg Q4W", "210 mg Q4W", "490 mg Q4W") )

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Q4W dosing 52 weeks (14 doses). Weekly sampling week 60.
code: dose_times <- seq(0, by = 28, length.out = 14)      # days obs_times  <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 28, by = 3.5),   seq(28, 420, by = 7) )))  d_dose <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>   mutate(AMT = dose_mg, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot", DV = NA_real_)  d_obs <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>   mutate(AMT = 0, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_)  events <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV,          WT, CRCL, EOS, DOSE_70MG, treatment, dose_mg)

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Kotani_2022_astegolimab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Replicate published figure — concentration–time by dose group
title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Kotani et al. (2022) Figure 2 shows observed vs. model-predicted astegolimab concentrations time 70, 210, 490 mg SC Q4W. plot analogous VPC packaged model.
code: treatment_map <- pop |> select(ID, treatment)  sim_plot <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0) |>   left_join(treatment_map, by = c("id" = "ID"))  vpc <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time, treatment) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time / 7, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~treatment) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Astegolimab serum concentration (\u00b5g/mL)",     title = "Astegolimab VPC by dose group (SC Q4W x 14 doses)",     caption = "Replicates the dose-stratified VPC panels in Kotani 2022 Figure 2."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Compute NCA first dosing interval (days 0–28) using PKNCA dose group treatment grouping variable. Simulated NCA (0–28 day dosing interval) Zenyatta dose arm.
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= 0, time <= 28) |>   left_join(treatment_map, by = c("id" = "ID")) |>   transmute(id = id, time = time, Cc = Cc, treatment = treatment)  dose_df <- events |>   filter(EVID == 1, TIME == 0) |>   transmute(id = ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT, treatment = treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = 28,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)) nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   caption = "Simulated NCA (0–28 day dosing interval) by Zenyatta dose arm." )

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Kotani et al. report terminal half-life 19.6 days (Abstract / Results). model’s analytical λz computed disposition micro-constants (kel, k12, k21) smaller root characteristic equation.
code: CL <- 0.244; Vc <- 0.614; Q <- 0.171; Vp <- 2.74 kel <- CL / Vc; k12 <- Q / Vc; k21 <- Q / Vp s   <- kel + k12 + k21 lambda_z <- 0.5 * (s - sqrt(s^2 - 4 * kel * k21)) cat(sprintf("Analytical terminal t1/2 = %.2f days (published 19.6).\n",             log(2) / lambda_z)) #> Analytical terminal t1/2 = 19.65 days (published 19.6).

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Kotani et al. tabulate NCA metrics per dose group, report terminal half-life (Abstract) state Discussion exposure dose-proportional 70–490 mg range adjusting 70 mg Frel offset. simulated median Cmax AUClast first dose scale dose × Frel (Frel = 0.847 70 mg arm, 1.00 210 490 mg arms). simulated absorption-phase half-life (≈16 days ka = 0.0437/day) dominates observed terminal slope single SC dose absorption slower disposition (flip-flop). Steady-state troughs therefore reached several months repeat Q4W dosing.

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Covariate distributions. Approximated Table 1 aggregate statistics: weight ~ lognormal centered 79 kg median; CRCL ~ normal centered 87.9 mL/min/1.73 m²; blood eosinophils ~ lognormal centered 180 cells/µL. original subject-level joint distribution public. Dose allocation. 150 subjects per dose arm (70, 210, 490 mg) — balanced. Zenyatta randomized 2:1:1:1 placebo:70:210:490, active per-arm N ≈ 100 ; balanced synthetic cohort chosen VPC readability . Baseline-covariates. Kotani 2022 uses baseline WT / CRCL / blood eosinophils (time-fixed per subject). convention applied event table. ADA status. ADA reported 7.3% patients significant covariate astegolimab PK final model. simulation introduce ADA effect, matching paper. Residual error. Combined additive + proportional natural scale (Cc ~ add(0.603 ug/mL) + prop(0.198)), matching Table 2. IIV Frel. Box-Cox transformed η (shape −2.81) per Eq. 2; η variance square pre-Box-Cox SD reported Table 2 (0.243² = 0.05905).

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Citation: Kovalenko P, Davis JD, Li M, et al. Base Covariate Population Pharmacokinetic Analyses Dupilumab Using Phase 3 Data. Clinical Pharmacology Drug Development. 2020;9(6):756-767. doi:10.1002/cpdd.780 Description: Dupilumab PK model (Kovalenko 2020) Article: Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev. 2020;9(6):756-767 (open access via PMC7496533)

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 pooled 16 clinical studies (N = 2115 participants; 202 healthy volunteers 1913 patients moderate--severe atopic dermatitis (AD)). , 2041 participants active treatment contributed 18,243 20,809 samples population PK analysis. Source studies include Phase 3 AD trials R668-AD-1334 (LIBERTY AD SOLO 1), R668-AD-1416 (LIBERTY AD SOLO 2), R668-AD-1224 (LIBERTY AD CHRONOS). Phase 1 studies R668--0907, TDU12265, PKM14161, R668-AD-1117 used fit “Model 1” - rich-data model file implements. paper’s main text tabulate detailed baseline demographics (age, sex, weight median/range, race breakdown) pooled cohort; appear Supplementary Table S1. approved adult AD maintenance regimen 600 mg SC loading dose day 0 followed 300 mg SC every 2 weeks (Q2W); Kovalenko 2016 precursor publication used 75 kg reference body weight allometric effect central volume, 2020 paper reuses equation without re-stating reference weight. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab")$population.

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Kovalenko 2020 Table 1 (Model 1 column) Supplementary Table S2 (IIV SDs residual errors). paper--implementation cross-walk: paper’s Methods section explicitly defines omega “omega (omega, standard deviation [SD] -subject variability)” sigma “sigma (sigma, SD measurement error)”. nlmixr2’s etalxxx ~ value syntax stores variance (omega^2), Supp. Table S2 SDs squared ini(). Model 1 shrinkage SD reported paper (10.4% / 25.2% / 22.9% / 23.2% / 54.9% Vc / ke / Vm / ka / MTT) consistent IIV--SD accounting.

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 parameterizes linear elimination first-order rate ke * central (CL * Cc) distribution direct rate constants kcp kpc = kcp / Mpc (Q Vp). model file preserves parameterization, derived quantities : Typical clearance: CL = ke * Vc = 0.0534 * 2.48 = 0.132 L/day (matches Table 1 “CL (L/d)”). Typical peripheral rate kpc = 0.213 / 0.686 = 0.310 1/day. etalmtt eta applied MTT = exp(lmtt + etalmtt) (.e. log-normal MTT). Supplementary Table S2 paper reports random effect additively MTT; log-normal implementation chosen avoids negative MTT draws simulation deviation published random-effect structure.

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Detailed observed demographics pooled cohort publicly reproduced paper’s main text. virtual cohort targets labelled adult AD population drawing body weight truncated normal centered Kovalenko 2016 reference weight 75 kg (SD ~18 kg, limits 40-165 kg), matches typical Phase 3 AD trial demographics reported Simpson 2016 SOLO / Blauvelt 2017 CHRONOS publications.
code: set.seed(20260418) n_subj <- 400  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 75, sd = 18), 40), 165) )  # Labelled AD regimen: 600 mg SC loading dose on day 0, 300 mg SC Q2W # for 12 additional doses -> study window 0-182 days. By dose 10+ the # profile is close to steady state (typical half-life ~2-3 weeks). load_dose <- 600 maint_dose <- 300 tau <- 14 n_maint <- 12 dose_days <- c(0, seq(tau, tau * n_maint, by = tau)) amt_vec <- c(load_dose, rep(maint_dose, n_maint))  ev_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time) |>   dplyr::group_by(id) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_vec, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L) |>   dplyr::ungroup()  obs_days <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, tau * (n_maint + 1), by = 1),   dose_days + 0.25,   dose_days + 1,   dose_days + 3 )))  ev_obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT)

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = "WT")

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 Figure 5 illustrates simulated median concentration-time profiles functional dupilumab several SC maintenance regimens (qw, q2w, q4w, q8w). figure reproduces labelled adult AD regimen (600 mg SC loading + 300 mg SC Q2W) 5th/50th/95th percentile bands across virtual cohort, spanning ~13 dosing cycles.
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#4682b4") +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = dose_days, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey70") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Dupilumab Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentile profile; 600 mg SC load then 300 mg SC Q2W",     caption = "Virtual AD cohort (N = 400); WT ~N(75, 18) kg, truncated 40-165 kg."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Zoomed-view final Q2W cycle (days 168-182) isolate steady-state peak, trough, AUC_tau used NCA .
code: ss_start <- tau * n_maint # day 168 (time of dose 13) ss_end <- ss_start + tau # day 182  ss_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(ss_summary, aes(time - ss_start, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#b4464b") +   geom_line(colour = "#b4464b", linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time after final dose (days)",     y = "Dupilumab Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Steady-state Q2W cycle (dose 13 of 13)",     caption = "Median and 90% prediction interval over one 14-day dosing interval."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Non-compartmental analysis steady-state Q2W interval (days 168-182). Compute Cmax, Cmin (Ctrough end tau), AUC_tau, average concentration per simulated subject.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start,     treatment = "300mg_Q2W_SS") |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = maint_dose, treatment = "300mg_Q2W_SS") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start = 0,   end = tau,   cmax = TRUE,   cmin = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) summary(nca_res) #>  start end    treatment   N     auclast        cmax        cmin         cav #>      0  14 300mg_Q2W_SS 400 1190 [43.9] 95.0 [41.9] 70.4 [49.2] 85.1 [43.9] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; N: number of subjects

what: Comparison against published typical steady-state exposure
title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 tabulate point estimates steady-state Cmax, Ctrough, AUC_tau main text; Figure 5 shows simulated medians qualitatively. Dupilumab FDA labelling follow-PK publications report typical steady-state trough concentrations ~70-80 mg/L 75-kg adult 600-mg-load + 300-mg-Q2W SC regimen. typical-value (“population typical”) prediction IIV zeroed provides direct self-consistency check: Typical-subject steady-state exposure (WT = 75 kg; IIV zeroed).
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  ev_typical <- events |>   dplyr::filter(id == 1L) |>   dplyr::mutate(WT = 75)  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical, keep = "WT" ) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvc', 'etalke', 'etalka', 'etalvm', 'etalmtt'  ss_typical <- sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc))  typical_summary <- tibble::tibble(   metric = c("Cmax (mg/L)", "Cmin/Ctrough (mg/L)",     "Cavg (mg/L)", "AUC_tau (day*mg/L)"),   typical_value = c(     max(ss_typical$Cc),     min(ss_typical$Cc),     mean(ss_typical$Cc),     sum(diff(ss_typical$time) *       (ss_typical$Cc[-length(ss_typical$Cc)] +         ss_typical$Cc[-1]) / 2)   ) ) knitr::kable(typical_summary, digits = 2,   caption = "Typical-subject steady-state exposure (WT = 75 kg; IIV zeroed).")

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 Model 1 uses ke/kcp/kpc parameterization rather CL/Q/Vp. model file preserves original parameterization verbatim. Typical values can cross-checked Table 1’s derived CL = ke * Vc = 0.132 L/day. Km F reported fixed Table 1 Model 1, values carried earlier Kovalenko 2016 model; CcaddSd likewise fixed 0.03 mg/L. etalmtt eta applied log-normal MTT (MTT = exp(lmtt + etalmtt)), whereas Supplementary Table S2 implements random effect additively MTT. log-normal form prevents non-physical negative MTT draws simulation. etas match paper’s random-effect structure directly. IIV (omega) reported Kovalenko 2020 standard deviation, per Methods definition. Values ini() squared yield variance nlmixr2 stores ~ operator. earlier version file stored SDs directly RHS (.e. variances), understate IIV magnitude mAb-relevant scale; current file corrected form. reference body weight (75 kg) inherited Kovalenko 2016 (doi:10.1002/psp4.12136); Kovalenko 2020 restate reference weight. See file header comment details. Demographic details (age, sex, race) used Model 1 implementation (WT enters covariate model) re-simulated virtual cohort. Kovalenko 2020 publish numerical Cmax / Cmin / AUC_tau steady state, PKNCA summary self-consistency check implemented model visual appearance Figure 5 rather back--paper numerical comparison. unit conversion required dosing amount (mg) concentration unit (mg/L) mg/L natural unit central/vc .

title: Kyhl_2016_nalmefene
text: Citation: Kyhl LE, Li S, Faerch KU, Soegaard B, Larsen F, Areberg J. Population pharmacokinetics nalmefene healthy subjects relation μ-opioid receptor occupancy. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2016 Feb;81(2):290-300. doi: 10.1111/bcp.12805. Epub 2016 Jan 27. PMID: 26483076; PMCID: PMC4833148. Description: Population PK model nalmefene healthy volunteers (Kyhl 2016): two-compartment model first-order absorption oral dosing, separate absorption rates tablet solution formulations, link mu-opioid receptor occupancy. Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.12805

title: Kyhl_2016_nalmefene
text: Replicate figures 5 publication single 20 mg dose fed state. Assumed mean age, lean body mass, tablet, LC-MS/MS assay used. paper indicates simulations used uniform age distribution 18-80 years LBM ~N(56,72). Since limits provided LBM simulation settings, simulation settings used.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 20, # dose in mg/kg # nolint: commented_code_linter.     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "depot"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = seq(0, 24, by = 0.1),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <-   dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) |>   dplyr::mutate(     LBM = 56.28,     AGE = 28,     RIA_ASSAY = 0,     FED = 1,     TABLET = 1   ) Kyhl2016Nalmefene <- readModelDb("Kyhl_2016_nalmefene") # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimNalmefene <- rxode2::rxSolve(Kyhl2016Nalmefene, events = dSimPrep, nStud = 500) dSimNalmefene$Analyte <- "Nalmefene"

title: Kyhl_2016_nalmefene
text: Replicate figure 5 paper. Assuming “confidence bounds” actually 95% prediction intervals.
code: dSimNalmefenePlot <-   dSimNalmefene |>   group_by(time) |>   summarize(     Q025_pk = quantile(sim, probs = 0.025),     Q50_pk = quantile(sim, probs = 0.5),     Q975_pk = quantile(sim, probs = 0.975),     Q025_occ = quantile(e_mu_opioid, probs = 0.025),     Q50_occ = quantile(e_mu_opioid, probs = 0.5),     Q975_occ = quantile(e_mu_opioid, probs = 0.975)   )  ggplot(dSimNalmefenePlot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_pk, ymin = Q025_pk, ymax = Q975_pk)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Estimated plasma concentration (ng mL^-1)"   ) +   geom_ribbon(fill = "gray") +   geom_line() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 5)) ggplot(dSimNalmefenePlot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_occ, ymin = Q025_occ, ymax = Q975_occ)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Occupancy (%)"   ) +   geom_ribbon(fill = "gray") +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = c(60, 90)) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 5))

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Citation: Li X, Roy , Murthy B. Population Pharmacokinetics Exposure-Response Relationship Intravenous Subcutaneous Abatacept Patients Rheumatoid Arthritis. J Clin Pharmacol. 2019 Feb;59(2):245-257. doi:10.1002/jcph.1308 Description: Two-compartment population PK model abatacept (CTLA4-Ig Fc-fusion) adults rheumatoid arthritis (Li 2019), first-order SC absorption, zero-order IV infusion support, first-order linear elimination, logit-scale SC bioavailability, full-block IIV CL/VC/Q/VP, KA parameterisation enforces KA > k_el. Article: J Clin Pharmacol. 2019;59(2):245-257 (open access via PMC6587803)

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Li 2019 pooled 11 clinical studies (4 phase 2 7 phase 3) final population PK dataset 2244 adults rheumatoid arthritis (RA). Six studies administered IV abatacept, four studies administered SC abatacept, one study (ACQUIRE) investigated routes. Doses ranged 0.5 10 mg/kg Q4W IV administration 75 200 mg QW SC administration. exclusion samples missing dose/sample information -LLOQ concentrations, analysis dataset contained 10,382 measurements (validated ELISA, LLOQ 1.0 ng/mL). approved regimens weight-tiered ~10 mg/kg IV Q4W (500 mg <60 kg, 750 mg 60-100 kg, 1000 mg >100 kg) fixed 125 mg SC QW. Li 2019 concluded regimens comparable, delivering steady-state trough concentration (Cminss) ≥10 μg/mL — exposure target associated near-maximal DAS28 response — ~90% patients across body weight strata. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Li_2019_abatacept")$population.

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Li 2019 Table 1 (: final-model structural parameters, B: covariate effects; see also Errata section Table 1B’s caption issue). Reference covariate values : 50-year-old male, baseline body weight 70 kg, baseline albumin 4.0 g/dL (paper typo: “mg/dL”), calculated GFR 90 mL/min/1.73 m², swollen joint count 16, concomitant NSAIDs, treated phase-3 (commercial) SC formulation.

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Time-unit conversion. Li 2019 reports CL Q L/h KA 1/h. nlmixr2lib convention time days, values multiplied 24 inside log(...) ini(). Logit-F parameterisation. Li 2019 constrains absolute bioavailability (0, 1) via inverse-logit link: F_abs = 1 / (1 + exp(-F_TV)) F_TV = logitfdepot + etalogitfdepot + FORM_ABA_PHASE2 * e_form_f. reference value logitfdepot = 1.42 gives F_abs ~= 0.805 phase-3 commercial formulation; phase-2 formulation shifts F_TV -1.16, giving F_abs ~= 0.565. KA > kel constraint. Li 2019 reports ~4-day SC Tmax ~14-day terminal half-life — .e. absorption faster elimination. prevent flip-flop parameter estimates fitting, paper reparameterises absorption KA_i = KA_TV * exp(etaKA) + k_el,k_el,= CL_i / VC_i. model file implements verbatim; simulation using typical parameters reproduces Tmax approximately 4.5 days (see ). Full-block IIV CL, VC, Q, VP. Li 2019 fits 4×4 block covariance matrix CL, VC, Q, VP etas; 10 lower-triangle values taken directly Table 1A. IIV KA logit-F independent blocks. Combined proportional + additive residual error. Li 2019 reports θPROP = 0.215 (fraction) θADD = 0.341 μg/mL; nlmixr2lib uses mg/L concentration, addSd = 0.341 carries without conversion (1 μg/mL = 1 mg/L). SWOL shifted-power form. swollen-joint-count effect ((SWOL+1)/(16+1))^0.0965; adding 1 avoids zero-count edge case origin power form. Canonical CRCL usage. Li 2019 calls renal covariate cGFR units mL/min/1.73 m²; canonical CRCL column carries units accepts either MDRD-estimated eGFR BSA-normalised measured CrCl. Reference 90. MTX dropped backward elimination. Table 1B lists CLMTX row (-0.0405, 95% CI -0.0889 0.00791, significant) paper’s final-model covariate equation p. 249 include MTX term. model implements published equation therefore excludes MTX; see Errata.

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Li 2019 Methods baseline-demographic descriptors (Table S3 accessible PMC full text). Subject-level observed data released paper. Three regimens simulated: labelled 125 mg SC QW (reference); weight-tiered IV Q4W (500 mg <60 kg, 750 mg 60-100 kg, 1000 mg >100 kg); single-dose 125 mg SC Tmax / Cmax check.
code: set.seed(20260424) n_subj <- 400  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id              = seq_len(n_subj),   WT              = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 70, sd = 18),   40, 160)),   AGE             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 50, sd = 13),   18,  90)),   ALB             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 4.0, sd = 0.35), 2.5, 5.0)),   CRCL            = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 90, sd = 25),   30, 180)),   SWOL_28JOINT    = pmin(pmax(round(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 16, sd = 6)),   0,  28)),   SEXF            = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.78),         # RA populations are ~75-80% female   CONMED_NSAID    = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.55),         # Li 2019 Methods: NSAID use common in RA cohort   FORM_ABA_PHASE2 = 0L                               # Phase-3 commercial SC formulation for labelled regimen ) tau_sc <- 7         # SC QW tau_iv <- 28        # IV Q4W n_sc   <- 26        # 26 weekly doses -> ~182 days, deeply into SS n_iv   <- 7         # 7 q4w doses -> ~196 days  dose_days_sc <- seq(0, tau_sc * (n_sc - 1), by = tau_sc) dose_days_iv <- seq(0, tau_iv * (n_iv - 1), by = tau_iv)  # Weight-tiered IV amount per subject cohort_iv <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(     amt_iv = dplyr::case_when(       WT <  60             ~ 500,       WT <= 100            ~ 750,       TRUE                 ~ 1000     )   )  build_sc_events <- function(cohort, dose_amt, dose_days, treatment) {   ev_dose <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_amt, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L, treatment = treatment)   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(     seq(0, max(dose_days) + tau_sc, by = 1),     dose_days + 1, dose_days + 3   )))   ev_obs <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L, treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  build_iv_events <- function(cohort_iv, dose_days, treatment) {   ev_dose <- cohort_iv |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_iv, cmt = "central", evid = 1L, treatment = treatment)   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(     seq(0, max(dose_days) + tau_iv, by = 1),     dose_days + 1, dose_days + 7   )))   ev_obs <- cohort_iv |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L, treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  events_sc_125 <- build_sc_events(cohort, 125, dose_days_sc, "SC_125_QW") events_iv     <- build_iv_events(cohort_iv, dose_days_iv, "IV_weight_tiered_Q4W")

title: Li_2019_abatacept
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Li_2019_abatacept")) keep_cols <- c("WT", "AGE", "ALB", "CRCL", "SWOL_28JOINT",                "SEXF", "CONMED_NSAID", "FORM_ABA_PHASE2", "treatment")  sim_sc <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_sc_125, keep = keep_cols)) sim_iv <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_iv,     keep = keep_cols)) sim    <- dplyr::bind_rows(sim_sc, sim_iv)

what: SC absorption: Tmax and early-dose kinetics
title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Li 2019 narrates “time maximum concentration (Tmax) SC abatacept ~4 days terminal half-life ~14 days IV SC abatacept.” block confirms model reproduces Tmax simulating single 125 mg SC dose typical covariate values plotting concentration-time profile 28 days.
code: mod_typ <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() typ_cov <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1L, WT = 70, AGE = 50, ALB = 4.0, CRCL = 90,   SWOL_28JOINT = 16, SEXF = 0L, CONMED_NSAID = 0L, FORM_ABA_PHASE2 = 0L ) ev_single_sc <- typ_cov |>   tidyr::crossing(time = c(0, seq(0.25, 28, by = 0.25))) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = ifelse(time == 0, 125, 0),                 cmt = ifelse(time == 0, "depot", NA_character_),                 evid = ifelse(time == 0, 1L, 0L)) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) sim_single_sc <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = ev_single_sc)) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalka', 'etalogitfdepot'  tmax_day  <- sim_single_sc$time[which.max(sim_single_sc$Cc)] cmax_mgL  <- max(sim_single_sc$Cc)  ggplot(sim_single_sc, aes(time, Cc)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = tmax_day, linetype = "dashed", colour = "steelblue") +   labs(     x = "Time after single SC dose (days)",     y = "Abatacept Cc (mg/L = µg/mL)",     title = sprintf("Typical-patient single 125 mg SC: Tmax = %.1f days, Cmax = %.2f mg/L",                     tmax_day, cmax_mgL),     caption = "Li 2019 narrates 'Tmax for SC abatacept was ~4 days'."   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Concentration-time profiles: SC QW vs IV Q4W
title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: two approved regimens overlaid axes first 12 weeks. Percentile bands reflect cohort-level IIV.
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0, time <= 84) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment, time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Abatacept Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentile profiles: 125 mg SC QW vs weight-tiered ~10 mg/kg IV Q4W",     caption = "Virtual RA cohort (N = 400); first 12 weeks."   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Steady-state exposures by body-weight group (Figure S2 replication)
title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Li 2019 Figure S2 plots simulated steady-state trough (Cminss) body weight group (<60 kg, 60-100 kg, >100 kg) IV SC treatments shows () SC Cminss decreases increasing body weight (b) IV Cminss approximately constant across weight groups virtue weight-tiered dosing. block replicates body-weight-group summary using steady-state cycle virtual cohort.
code: ss_start_sc <- tau_sc * (n_sc - 1) ss_end_sc   <- ss_start_sc + tau_sc ss_start_iv <- tau_iv * (n_iv - 1) ss_end_iv   <- ss_start_iv + tau_iv  trough_by_wtgrp <- dplyr::bind_rows(   sim_sc |>     dplyr::filter(time == ss_start_sc + tau_sc) |>     dplyr::mutate(regimen = "SC 125 mg QW"),   sim_iv |>     dplyr::filter(time == ss_start_iv + tau_iv) |>     dplyr::mutate(regimen = "IV weight-tiered Q4W") ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     wt_group = cut(WT, breaks = c(-Inf, 60, 100, Inf),                    labels = c("<60 kg", "60-100 kg", ">100 kg"))   )  ggplot(trough_by_wtgrp, aes(wt_group, Cc, fill = regimen)) +   geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.size = 0.6) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 10, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Baseline body-weight group",     y = "Steady-state trough Cc (mg/L, log scale)",     title = "Replicates Li 2019 Figure S2: C_minss by body-weight group, IV vs SC",     caption = "Red dashed line = 10 μg/mL near-maximal-efficacy target per Li 2019."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Non-compartmental analysis final (steady-state) SC IV dosing intervals. Computes Cmax, Cmin, Cavg, AUC per simulated subject regimen.
code: nca_conc_sc <- sim_sc |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start_sc, time <= ss_end_sc, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start_sc) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose_sc <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 125, treatment = "SC_125_QW") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj_sc <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc_sc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj_sc <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose_sc, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_sc <- data.frame(start = 0, end = tau_sc,                            cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                            auclast = TRUE, cav = TRUE) nca_sc <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_sc, dose_obj_sc, intervals = intervals_sc)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■         81% |  ETA:  1s summary(nca_sc) #>  start end treatment   N    auclast        cmax        cmin              tmax #>      0   7 SC_125_QW 400 123 [38.1] 19.4 [35.7] 14.9 [44.0] 2.00 [1.00, 3.00] #>          cav #>  17.6 [38.1] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects nca_conc_iv <- sim_iv |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start_iv, time <= ss_end_iv, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start_iv) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose_iv <- cohort_iv |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = amt_iv, treatment = "IV_weight_tiered_Q4W") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj_iv <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc_iv, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj_iv <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose_iv, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_iv <- data.frame(start = 0, end = tau_iv,                            cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                            auclast = TRUE, cav = TRUE) nca_iv <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_iv, dose_obj_iv, intervals = intervals_iv)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■          76% |  ETA:  1s summary(nca_iv) #>  start end            treatment   N     auclast       cmax        cmin #>      0  28 IV_weight_tiered_Q4W 400 1340 [32.3] 224 [25.7] 14.2 [56.8] #>                  tmax         cav #>  0.000 [0.000, 0.000] 47.8 [32.3] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects

what: Comparison against the Li 2019 Cminss ≥10 μg/mL benchmark
title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Li 2019 reports ~90% patients achieved steady-state trough concentration least 10 μg/mL approved regimens. cohort-fraction check direct numerical replicate claim. Fraction virtual subjects steady-state trough Cc >= 10 mg/L regimen weight group (Li 2019: ~90% across groups).
code: pct_above <- trough_by_wtgrp |>   dplyr::group_by(regimen, wt_group) |>   dplyr::summarise(     N              = dplyr::n(),     pct_ge_10_mgL  = 100 * mean(Cc >= 10, na.rm = TRUE),     median_Cmin    = median(Cc),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(pct_above, digits = 1,   caption = "Fraction of virtual subjects with steady-state trough Cc >= 10 mg/L by regimen and weight group (Li 2019: ~90% across all groups).")

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Time-unit rescaling. Li 2019 reports CL, Q, KA per-hour units; model file converts per-day factor 24 match nlmixr2lib units$time = "day" convention. VC, VP, F, IIV variances, residual-error magnitudes carry unchanged. MTX excluded final-model equation. Li 2019 Table 1B lists CLMTX = -0.0405 (95% CI crosses zero, significant 0.1% backward-elimination threshold) paper’s final-model covariate equation p. 249 include MTX term. model implements published final-model equation therefore excludes MTX; remaining 10 covariate effects Table 1B retained. FORM_ABA_PHASE2 model-specific covariate. abatacept SC phase-2-vs-phase-3 formulation indicator implemented inline covariateData rather registered canonical entry, per nlmixr2lib policy FORM_* covariates stay model-specific unless clearly generalize across multiple drugs. covariate used phase-2 trial SC data; set 0 simulations approved commercial 125 mg SC QW regimen. checkModelConventions() flags warning, accepted exception. Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. Li 2019 Table S3 (baseline demographics) referenced paper embedded PMC full text, virtual-cohort distributions approximate: WT ~ N(70, 18) truncated [40, 160]; AGE ~ N(50, 13) truncated [18, 90]; ALB ~ N(4.0, 0.35) truncated [2.5, 5.0] g/dL; CRCL ~ N(90, 25) truncated [30, 180]; SWOL_28JOINT ~ rounded N(16, 6) truncated [0, 28]; SEXF = 78% female; CONMED_NSAID = 55%. reference values match Li 2019 Methods narrative; dispersions approximate typical RA phase-3 cohort. IV instantaneous bolus. Li 2019 uses zero-order infusion (approved IV regimen 30-minute infusion). simulation treats IV doses instantaneous bolus central, slightly overstates early-post-dose Cmax effect AUC, trough, half-life estimates. Users wanting exact infusion kinetics set rate (dur) rxode2 event table. subject-level observed data. Li 2019 release individual subject data; figures reported pure forward simulation published final-model parameters virtual cohort.

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: published Li 2019 paper contains two items reviewer re-reading source alongside model aware : Albumin unit typo (Methods p. 248). Li 2019 Methods states “baseline albumin 4.0 mg/dL” reference-subject covariate value. Normal adult serum albumin ~4 g/dL; value 4 mg/dL factor 1000 low physiologically impossible. treated publication unit typo: model codes ALB reference 4.0 g/dL, clinically sensible matches abatacept PK reports (e.g. Orencia label). unit recorded covariateData[[ALB]]$units g/dL explicit note. Table 1B caption vs final-model equation (Table 1 caption Results p. 249). Table 1 captioned “Parameter Estimates () Structural Part Final PPK Model Abatacept (B) Covariates Full PPK Model” — .e. Table 1A final Table 1B explicitly full model. Table 1B’s 11th row (CLMTX = -0.0405, 95% CI crossing zero) present published final-model covariate equation p. 249. operator decision extraction treat Table 1B’s 10 non-MTX estimates published final-model covariate values (matching final-model equation exactly) drop CLMTX; paper separately tabulate post-backward-elimination covariate estimates. Bristol-Myers Squibb ever publishes separate table final-model covariate estimates differ Table 1B, values model refit source.

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Citation: Lon HK, Liu D, DuBois DC, Almon RR, Jusko WJ. Modeling pharmacokinetics/pharmacodynamics abatacept disease progression collagen-induced arthritic rats: population approach. J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn. 2013;40(6):701-712. doi:10.1007/s10928-013-9341-1 Description: Two-compartment population PK model linear elimination short-term zero-order SC absorption abatacept (CTLA-4Ig Fc-fusion) male Lewis rats collagen-induced arthritis (Lon 2013). Article: J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn 40(6):701-712

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Simulate abatacept plasma concentration-time profiles collagen-induced arthritic (CIA) Lewis rats using final population PK model Lon et al. 2013. Abatacept (CTLA-4Ig Fc-fusion protein; ~92 kDa) blocks CD28 co-stimulatory signal required T-cell activation. Lon 2013 characterized abatacept PK rat CIA model using IV SC single-dose SC multiple-dose regimens, fitting three arms simultaneously two-compartment linear-elimination model short-term zero-order SC absorption pattern (Eq. 3 paper).

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Lon 2013 Methods (Animals Experimental Design): fifty male Lewis rats (150-175 g arrival, 6-9 weeks old) purchased. CIA induced intradermal injection bovine type II collagen day 0 boosted day 7. day 20, twenty-three rats >= 50% paw-swelling response one two hind paws randomized four treatment arms: Vehicle control (single n = 3, multiple n = 3) IV 10 mg/kg single dose day 21 (n = 5) SC 20 mg/kg single dose day 21 (n = 6) SC 20 mg/kg day 21 followed SC 10 mg/kg days 23, 25, 27, 29 (n = 6) population PK fit used 17 abatacept-treated rats. Plasma abatacept quantified soluble CTLA-4 ELISA (LLOQ 0.16 ng/mL, inter-day CV ~15%). information available programmatically readModelDb("Lon_2013_abatacept") (returned function’s body holds population list literal).

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Lon_2013_abatacept.R. table collects one place review.

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Individual body weights reported. build 17-rat virtual cohort whose arm sizes match Lon 2013 exactly (IV n = 5, SC single n = 6, SC multiple n = 6) whose weights sampled uniform distribution across published 150-175 g range arrival (weight gain modeled short post-dosing observation window).
code: set.seed(2013)  cohorts <- tribble(   ~treatment,             ~n,   "IV 10 mg/kg",           5,   "SC 20 mg/kg single",    6,   "SC multiple",           6 )  pop <- cohorts |>   group_by(treatment) |>   summarise(ID = list(seq_len(n[1])), .groups = "drop") |>   tidyr::unnest(ID) |>   mutate(     ID = dplyr::row_number(),     WT = runif(dplyr::n(), min = 0.150, max = 0.175)   )  pop #> # A tibble: 17 × 3 #>    treatment             ID    WT #>    <chr>              <int> <dbl> #>  1 IV 10 mg/kg            1 0.162 #>  2 IV 10 mg/kg            2 0.174 #>  3 IV 10 mg/kg            3 0.170 #>  4 IV 10 mg/kg            4 0.169 #>  5 IV 10 mg/kg            5 0.156 #>  6 SC 20 mg/kg single     6 0.168 #>  7 SC 20 mg/kg single     7 0.173 #>  8 SC 20 mg/kg single     8 0.170 #>  9 SC 20 mg/kg single     9 0.173 #> 10 SC 20 mg/kg single    10 0.170 #> 11 SC 20 mg/kg single    11 0.150 #> 12 SC multiple           12 0.156 #> 13 SC multiple           13 0.169 #> 14 SC multiple           14 0.171 #> 15 SC multiple           15 0.166 #> 16 SC multiple           16 0.168 #> 17 SC multiple           17 0.174

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Doses delivered depot SC arms central IV. packaged model uses podo(depot) tad(depot) drive zero-order release F * Dose / tau central first tau = 2.67 days SC dose; IV doses bypass mechanism.
code: obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0,   1,   by = 1/12),           # every 2 h on day 1   seq(1,   3,   by = 1/4),            # every 6 h through day 3   seq(3,  10,   by = 0.5),            # twice daily through day 10   seq(10, 30,   by = 1)               # daily through day 30 )))  sc_multiple_times <- c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8) sc_multiple_amts  <- c(20,  10, 10, 10, 10)  # mg/kg  dose_rows_iv <- pop |>   filter(treatment == "IV 10 mg/kg") |>   transmute(     ID, treatment, WT,     time = 0,     amt  = 10 * WT,           # mg/kg * kg = mg     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "central",     dv   = NA_real_   )  dose_rows_sc_single <- pop |>   filter(treatment == "SC 20 mg/kg single") |>   transmute(     ID, treatment, WT,     time = 0,     amt  = 20 * WT,     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "depot",     dv   = NA_real_   )  dose_rows_sc_multi <- pop |>   filter(treatment == "SC multiple") |>   tidyr::crossing(     dose_idx = seq_along(sc_multiple_times)   ) |>   transmute(     ID, treatment, WT,     time = sc_multiple_times[dose_idx],     amt  = sc_multiple_amts[dose_idx] * WT,     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "depot",     dv   = NA_real_   )  dose_rows <- bind_rows(dose_rows_iv, dose_rows_sc_single, dose_rows_sc_multi)  obs_rows <- pop |>   select(ID, treatment, WT) |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>   mutate(     amt  = NA_real_,     evid = 0L,     cmt  = NA_character_,     dv   = NA_real_   )  events <- bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) |>   arrange(ID, time, desc(evid))

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Simulate -subject variability CL V1 spread across virtual cohort matches paper’s individual variability.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Lon_2013_abatacept")  events_sim <- events |> rename(id = ID) sim <- rxSolve(object = mod, events = events_sim, returnType = "data.frame") |>   as_tibble() |>   left_join(pop |> select(ID, treatment), by = c("id" = "ID")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Replicate Figure 2: plasma concentration-time profiles by treatment
title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Figure 2 Lon 2013 shows abatacept plasma concentration (umol/L) vs. time following IV 10 mg/kg, SC 20 mg/kg single, SC multiple-dose regimens, 90% prediction intervals population model.
code: fig2 <- sim |>   filter(time > 0, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   group_by(treatment, time) |>   summarise(     med = median(Cc),     q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fig2, aes(x = time, y = med, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = q05, ymax = q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~treatment, nrow = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x       = "Time since first dose (days)",     y       = "Abatacept plasma concentration (umol/L, log scale)",     colour  = "Treatment",     fill    = "Treatment",     title   = "Figure 2 replication - simulated abatacept PK by treatment arm",     caption = "Replicates Figure 2 of Lon et al. 2013 (median and 90% interval across virtual cohort)."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Compute non-compartmental CL (IV arm), AUC, Cmax, Tmax, terminal half-life per subject per treatment arm. PKNCA formula groups concentrations treatment + id summaries per-arm. SC single-dose arm also derive bioavailability F comparing dose- normalized AUC(0,inf) IV arm. Simulated NCA summaries treatment arm.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   filter(time >= 0, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- dose_rows |>   transmute(id = ID, time, amt, treatment) conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = Inf,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE,   cl.obs     = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- pk.nca(nca_data)  sim_nca <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "aucinf.obs", "half.life", "cl.obs")) |>   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) |>   summarise(     mean = mean(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     sd   = sd(PPORRES,   na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(sim_nca, digits = 3, caption = "Simulated NCA summaries by treatment arm.")

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Lon 2013 Results (Pharmacokinetics) reports following NCA-derived parameters across abatacept-treated arms: CL = 20.8 mL/day/kg Vss = 146 mL/kg F = 57.7% (SC vs. IV) model-estimated values (Table 1) CL = 21.8 mL/day/kg, V1 + V2 = 69.5 + 61.9 = 131.4 mL/kg, F = 59.2%. simulation converts PKNCA output per-kg clearance (using rat’s WT) derives F dose-normalized AUC(0,inf). Simulated NCA vs. Lon 2013 NCA / model values. simulated IV CL SC F track published NCA values within ~20% tolerance described verification checklist (differences driven two effects - structural-model CL (21.8) NCA CL (20.8) already differed ~5% source paper, finite-sample mean across 5 simulated rats variability 9.67% CV CL).
code: # Per-kg CL for the IV arm iv_cl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   filter(treatment == "IV 10 mg/kg", PPTESTCD == "cl.obs") |>   inner_join(pop |> select(id = ID, WT), by = "id") |>   mutate(cl_mL_per_day_per_kg = PPORRES / WT * 1000)  # L/day -> mL/day, then / kg  # Dose-normalized AUC for F calculation per_subject_auc <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD == "aucinf.obs") |>   inner_join(pop |> select(id = ID, WT), by = "id") |>   mutate(     dose_mgkg = case_when(       treatment == "IV 10 mg/kg"        ~ 10,       treatment == "SC 20 mg/kg single" ~ 20,       TRUE                              ~ NA_real_     ),     auc_per_dose = PPORRES / dose_mgkg   ) |>   filter(!is.na(dose_mgkg))  mean_iv_auc_per_dose <- per_subject_auc |>   filter(treatment == "IV 10 mg/kg") |>   summarise(mean_auc_per_dose = mean(auc_per_dose)) |>   pull(mean_auc_per_dose)  mean_sc_auc_per_dose <- per_subject_auc |>   filter(treatment == "SC 20 mg/kg single") |>   summarise(mean_auc_per_dose = mean(auc_per_dose)) |>   pull(mean_auc_per_dose)  F_sim <- mean_sc_auc_per_dose / mean_iv_auc_per_dose  comparison <- tribble(   ~parameter,                            ~source,                  ~value,   "CL (mL/day/kg), NCA IV arm",          "Lon 2013",                20.8,   "CL (mL/day/kg), NCA IV arm",          "Simulation",              round(mean(iv_cl$cl_mL_per_day_per_kg), 2),   "F (SC vs IV, fraction), NCA",         "Lon 2013",                0.577,   "F (SC vs IV, fraction), NCA",         "Simulation",              round(F_sim, 3),   "CL (mL/day/kg), model theta",         "Lon 2013 Table 1",        21.8,   "Vss (mL/kg), model V1+V2",            "Lon 2013 Table 1",        131.4,   "F (fraction), model theta",           "Lon 2013 Table 1",        0.592,   "Vss (mL/kg), NCA",                    "Lon 2013",                146.0 )  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 3,              caption = "Simulated NCA vs. Lon 2013 NCA / model values.")

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Body weight distribution. Lon 2013 reports rats weighed 150-175 g arrival. sample WT uniformly across range; weight gain modeled 8-day (SC multiple) 20-30 day observation windows. Sampling schedule. Individual sampling times published full detail. use dense early schedule (every 2-6 h 3 days, twice daily day 10, daily thereafter) supports stable NCA early-distribution late-terminal phases. Assay. Observations simulated lower limit quantification (0.16 ng/mL) construction; filtering step !.na(Cc) & Cc > 0 removes post-elimination near-zero concentrations destabilize log-linear terminal regression PKNCA. Residual error. combined prop + add error model yields additive floor 0.0365 umol/L (~ 3.4 ng/mL) plus 16.1% proportional component. Simulated concentrations include residual variability; Figure 2 replication plots median 90% interval across virtual cohort match paper’s prediction band. PD disease progression. Lon 2013 also reports PD model paw edema (Table 2) built top PK parameters. PD arm packaged model; Lon_2013_abatacept() PK-.

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Structural model: 2-compartment linear elimination central compartment. SC doses go depot; zero-order rate F * Dose / tau driven depot central t < tau zero thereafter, leaving (1 - F) * Dose unabsorbed residual depot. Units. Dose mg volumes mL give central / vc mg/mL; observation equation divides MW (92,000 g/mol) multiplies 1e6 report concentrations umol/L, matching units Lon 2013 Table 1. IIV: diagonal CL V1 , per Lon 2013 Table 1; IIV reported CLD, V2, F, tau.

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Lon HK, Liu D, DuBois DC, Almon RR, Jusko WJ. Modeling pharmacokinetics/pharmacodynamics abatacept disease progression collagen-induced arthritic rats: population approach. J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn. 2013;40(6):701-712. doi:10.1007/s10928-013-9341-1

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Long et al. (2017) characterised population pharmacokinetics necitumumab (second-generation fully human IgG1 anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody) 807 cancer patients pooled across five clinical studies (three phase /II rich sampling two phase III trough-sampling squamous non-squamous non-small-cell lung cancer). final model two-compartment parallel linear Michaelis–Menten (target-mediated) elimination central compartment. Body weight covariate retained, entering power effect clearances (CL Q, shared exponent 0.768) volumes (V1 V2, shared exponent 0.498). vignette reproduces typical concentration–time profile phase III SQUIRE regimen (800 mg IV ~1 h days 1 8 21-day cycle), documents parameter provenance source-trace table, validates simulated NCA published Vss, CLtot steady state, preclinical 40 mg/L trough threshold.

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Long 2017 Tables 2 3 (final pooled Pop-PK dataset): Age, sex, race, ethnicity, ECOG, liver function (AST, ALT, bilirubin), renal function (Cockcroft–Gault CrCl), concomitant gemcitabine / cisplatin tested covariates; body weight retained. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Long_2017_necitumumab")$population.

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Every numeric value model file inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Long_2017_necitumumab.R comes following locations Long , Chigutsa E, Wallin J, Clinical Pharmacokinetics 2017;56(5):505–514 (doi:10.1007/s40262-016-0452-x). errata located PubMed, Google Scholar, Springer Nature landing page (search date 2026-04-21).

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: N = 200 virtual cancer patients. Body weight drawn log-normal distribution anchored published median (71 kg, geometric CV 22%) clipped Long 2017 min/max (35–181 kg) match Table 2. Sex age simulated completeness; model sex, age, race, ethnicity effect (none retained final covariate search), WT drives -subject variability typical-value predictions.
code: set.seed(2017) n_subj <- 200  wt <- pmax(35, pmin(181, exp(log(71) + rnorm(n_subj, 0, 0.22)))) pop <- tibble(   ID = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = wt ) summary(pop$WT) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   40.13   60.47   71.31   72.94   81.41  141.58

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Four 21-day cycles (12 weeks) SQUIRE regimen: 800 mg IV 1 h days 1 8 cycle. Observation grid: dense first 72 h first dose (capture distribution), weekly troughs mid-cycle samples remaining cycles.
code: cycle_h <- 21 * 24 cycle_dose_offsets <- c(0, 7 * 24) cycles <- 4 dose_times <- as.numeric(outer(cycle_dose_offsets, (seq_len(cycles) - 1) * cycle_h, `+`)) dose_times <- sort(dose_times) tmax_h <- max(dose_times) + 21 * 24  # one full trough cycle past last dose  obs_early <- seq(0, 72, by = 1) obs_post <- sort(unique(c(   dose_times + 1,  # end of 1-h infusion   dose_times + 6,   dose_times + 24,   seq(0, tmax_h, by = 12) ))) obs_times <- sort(unique(setdiff(c(obs_early, obs_post), dose_times)))  d_dose <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 800,     EVID = 1,     CMT  = 1L,     RATE = 800,  # 1-h infusion (mg/h)     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = 1L,     RATE = 0,     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, RATE, DV, WT)  stopifnot(sum(d_sim$EVID == 1) == n_subj * length(dose_times))

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Two passes: stochastic simulation full IIV residual error (VPC-style plots NCA), typical-value simulation rxode2::zeroRe() reference trajectory.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Long_2017_necitumumab")  set.seed(20170930) sim_full <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure-equivalent 1 — Single-cycle profile (virtual cohort + typical patient)
title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Long 2017 Figure 2 shows observed concentrations vs. time first infusion across five studies. panel shows first-cycle profile (0–21 days) virtual cohort overlaid typical-value trajectory.
code: first_cycle <- sim_full |> filter(time <= cycle_h) first_cycle_typ  <- sim_typ  |> filter(time <= cycle_h, id == 1)  ggplot() +   geom_line(     data = first_cycle, aes(time_day, Cc, group = id),     colour = "grey70", alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.25   ) +   geom_line(     data = first_cycle_typ, aes(time_day, Cc),     colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 1   ) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x     = "Time since first dose (days)",     y     = "Necitumumab concentration (mg/L)",     title = "First SQUIRE cycle — virtual cohort vs typical 70 kg patient",     caption = "Long 2017 Figure 2-equivalent; 800 mg IV 1-h infusion, days 1 and 8"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure-equivalent 2 — Steady-state trough across four cycles
title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Long 2017 reports steady state reached within three four cycles phase III 800 mg regimen produces trough concentrations exceed preclinical 40 mg/L threshold. plot shows simulated pre-dose end--cycle trough concentrations across four cycles 40 mg/L threshold marked.
code: trough_times <- sort(unique(c(dose_times, dose_times + 21 * 24 - 1))) trough_times <- trough_times[trough_times <= tmax_h]  trough <- sim_full |>   filter(time %in% trough_times) |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24)  trough_summary <- trough |>   group_by(time_day) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(trough_summary, aes(time_day, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dashed", colour = "firebrick") +   annotate(     "text", x = 5, y = 45,     label = "Preclinical target 40 mg/L",     colour = "firebrick", hjust = 0, size = 3   ) +   labs(     x     = "Time since first dose (days)",     y     = "Necitumumab concentration (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated necitumumab trough/peak profile over 4 SQUIRE cycles",     caption = "Long 2017 Figure 4-equivalent; 800 mg IV days 1 and 8 of 21-day cycle"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: PKNCA validation over the final (cycle 4) dosing interval
title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Run NCA final 21-day cycle compare simulated Vss CLtot published typical-patient steady-state values (6.97 L 0.0141 L/h, Long 2017 Results narrative). PKNCA formula includes treatment grouping variable summary rolls per regimen, per library’s PKNCA-recipe convention.
code: ss_start <- dose_times[length(dose_times) - 1]  # start of cycle 4 = day 1 of cycle 4 ss_end   <- ss_start + 21 * 24                  # end of 21-day cycle  sim_nca <- sim_full |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end) |>   transmute(     id        = id,     time      = time,     Cc        = Cc,     treatment = "800 mg IV days 1 and 8 q3w"   )  dose_nca <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 1, TIME >= ss_start, TIME <= ss_end) |>   transmute(     id        = ID,     time      = TIME,     amt       = AMT,     treatment = "800 mg IV days 1 and 8 q3w"   )  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_nca, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start     = ss_start,   end       = ss_end,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   cmin      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   cav       = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #>
