what: Per dosing-interval exposures vs Thakre 2022 Table 2
title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: Compute Cmax, Ctrough, AUC first three full dosing intervals (weeks 0-4, 4-16, 16-28) compare Thakre 2022 Table 2. Simulated per-interval exposures (compare Thakre 2022 Table 2: interval-1 Cmax median 9.40, interval-2 Cmax median 14.1, interval-3 Cmax median 11.0 ug/mL; interval-1 Ctrough median 5.68, interval-2 Ctrough median 1.93, interval-3 Ctrough median 1.52 ug/mL; interval AUC medians 207, 565, 439 ug*day/mL).
code: intervals <- tribble(   ~label,    ~start_wk, ~end_wk,   "1 (0-4)",   0,   4,   "2 (4-16)",  4,  16,   "3 (16-28)", 16, 28 )  ivl_summary <- intervals %>%   rowwise() %>%   do({     row <- .     sub <- sim %>%       filter(time >= row$start_wk * 7, time <= row$end_wk * 7, Cc > 0)     per_id <- sub %>%       group_by(id) %>%       summarise(         Cmax    = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),         Ctrough = Cc[which.max(time)],         AUCtau  = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),         .groups = "drop"       )     tibble(       interval = row$label,       Cmax_median    = median(per_id$Cmax),       Cmax_mean      = mean(per_id$Cmax),       Ctrough_median = median(per_id$Ctrough),       Ctrough_mean   = mean(per_id$Ctrough),       AUCtau_median  = median(per_id$AUCtau),       AUCtau_mean    = mean(per_id$AUCtau)     )   }) %>%   bind_rows()  knitr::kable(   ivl_summary,   digits = 2,   caption = paste(     "Simulated per-interval exposures (compare with Thakre 2022 Table 2:",     "interval-1 Cmax median 9.40, interval-2 Cmax median 14.1,",     "interval-3 Cmax median 11.0 ug/mL;",     "interval-1 Ctrough median 5.68, interval-2 Ctrough median 1.93,",     "interval-3 Ctrough median 1.52 ug/mL;",     "interval AUC medians 207, 565, 439 ug*day/mL)."   ) )

title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: Run PKNCA third dosing interval (weeks 16-28, steady-state maintenance), one reported Third dosing interval Thakre 2022 Table 2. PKNCA summary third dosing interval (weeks 16-28). Compare Cmax / AUClast Thakre 2022 Table 2 interval 3.
code: nca_conc <- sim %>%   filter(time >= 16 * 7, time <= 28 * 7, Cc > 0) %>%   mutate(     time_rel  = time - 16 * 7,     treatment = "risankizumab_150mg_q12w"   ) %>%   rename(ID = id) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- pop %>%   mutate(     time_rel  = 0,     AMT       = 150,     treatment = "risankizumab_150mg_q12w"   ) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, AMT, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 12 * 7,     cmax      = TRUE,     tmax      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE,     half.life = TRUE   ) ) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■                             13% |  ETA: 15s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■                       32% |  ETA: 11s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                  52% |  ETA:  8s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■            71% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      91% |  ETA:  1s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = paste(     "PKNCA summary for the third dosing interval (weeks 16-28).",     "Compare Cmax / AUClast against Thakre 2022 Table 2 interval 3."   ) )

previous_headings: Risankizumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: Thakre 2022 publish individual PK per-subject covariate values, virtual population approximates typical PsA phase 2/3 populations rather reproducing : Weight: sampled log-normal around 85 kg median, SD 0.22 log scale. paper references 70 kg; median PsA phase-3 trials typically ~85 kg. Age: normal around 51 years, SD 12, clipped 18-80. paper references 52 years. Serum albumin: normal around 43 g/L, SD 3.5, clipped 30-52 g/L (reference 45 g/L). Serum creatinine: normal around 72 umol/L, SD 16, clipped 40-140 umol/L (reference 70.73 umol/L). CRP (hs-CRP assay): log-normal around median 6 mg/L (reference 5.21 mg/L). hs-CRP highly skewed PsA. correlation continuous covariates imposed; paper report joint distributions. Dosing regimen uses approved PsA label (150 mg SC weeks 0 4, every 12 weeks). Thakre 2022 included earlier dose-ranging phase 1 / 2 data; regimens simulated . ADA titers included evaluated meet retention criteria final model.

title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: Model: two-compartment first-order SC absorption linear elimination. TMDD. Typical-subject CL reference covariates: 0.248 L/day. Typical-subject V1: 4.71 L. Typical-subject Vp: 4.26 L. Steady-state Vss = V1 + V2 ~ 9.0 L (reference subject; paper cites approximately 0.31 L/day 6.8 L Vc median-PsA covariates). Strongest covariates: body weight (power 0.869 CL, 1.46 V1) serum albumin (power -0.703 CL).

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: Tiraboschi et al. (2025) developed population PK model amlitelimab (anti-OX40L monoclonal antibody) 439 adults (78 healthy volunteers 361 moderate--severe atopic dermatitis). structural model two-compartment disposition first-order subcutaneous absorption, absorption lag, parallel linear Michaelis-Menten (target-mediated) elimination central compartment. Allometric body-weight scaling acts linear CL, V1, V2; baseline EASI adds linear CL additively, baseline serum albumin modifies subcutaneous bioavailability (additive linear scale logit transformation). vignette simulates labelled STREAM-AD regimen (250 mg SC Q4W 500 mg SC loading dose time 0) virtual AD population verifies three qualitative claims paper: Terminal half-life amlitelimab linear PK range approximately 28 days (range 24-43 days). Body weight dominant PK covariate, driving steady-state exposure roughly 70% 40 kg subject roughly 25% 100 kg subject, relative 75 kg reference participant (Table S3). Clearance lower limit quantification (0.0469 µg/mL) dominated (~66%) target-mediated elimination, whereas ~2 µg/mL linear pathway dominates (Figure 3).

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
code: set.seed(2025) n_subj <- 300  # Body weight: log-normal around the population median of 74.5 kg, # truncated to the study range [40.5, 148] kg. wt_draw <- exp(rnorm(n_subj, log(74.5), 0.22)) WT <- pmin(pmax(wt_draw, 40.5), 148)  # Baseline EASI: mean 29.7, SD 11.3 in AD subjects (Table S1); # truncated at EASI >= 6 (moderate threshold for STREAM-AD eligibility) # and <= 72 (the maximum possible score). EASI <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 29.7, 11.3), 6), 72)  # Baseline serum albumin: mean 46.4 g/L, SD 3.5 g/L, truncated to [37, 57]. ALB <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 46.4, 3.5), 37), 57)  pop <- data.frame(   ID   = seq_len(n_subj),   WT   = WT,   EASI = EASI,   ALB  = ALB )  # Weight strata for the covariate sensitivity figure pop$wt_group <- cut(   pop$WT,   breaks = c(0, 50, 75, 100, Inf),   labels = c("< 50 kg", "50\u201375 kg", "75\u2013100 kg", "\u2265 100 kg"),   right  = FALSE )

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: STREAM-AD labelled regimen: 500 mg SC loading dose day 0, followed 250 mg SC Q4W week 24, weekly observations week 30.
code: dose_days <- c(0, seq(28, 168, by = 28))           # loading + Q4W for 24 weeks dose_amt  <- c(500, rep(250, length(dose_days) - 1))  obs_times_day <- seq(0, 210, by = 7)               # through week 30  d_dose <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_subj), each = length(dose_days)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(dose_days, times = n_subj),     AMT  = rep(dose_amt,  times = n_subj),     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA   )  d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_subj), each = length(obs_times_day)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(obs_times_day, times = n_subj),     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, EASI, ALB, wt_group)

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab")  set.seed(2025) sim_out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Figure — population concentration-time profile (median and 90% PI)
title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
code: sim_plot <- sim_out |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0)  d_overall <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(d_overall, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 210, by = 28)) +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = "Amlitelimab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "STREAM-AD 250 mg Q4W with 500 mg loading dose\nMedian and 90% prediction interval"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure — stratification by baseline body weight
title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
code: wt_map <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 0, TIME == 0) |>   select(ID, wt_group) |>   distinct()  d_wt <- sim_plot |>   left_join(wt_map, by = c("id" = "ID")) |>   group_by(time, wt_group) |>   summarise(     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(d_wt, aes(x = time, y = Q50, colour = wt_group, fill = wt_group)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 210, by = 28)) +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = "Amlitelimab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     colour = "Body weight",     fill   = "Body weight",     title  = "STREAM-AD Q4W regimen stratified by baseline body weight"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: Compare steady-state week-24 exposure (AUC 4-week dosing interval, AUC4W) reference AD patient (75 kg, EASI 27.5, albumin 47 g/L) receiving 62.5 mg Q4W. Table S3 reports AUC4W changes +72%, +44%, -24%, -51% 40, 50, 100, 150 kg participants respectively. Simulated AUC4W steady state (62.5 mg Q4W) vs body weight
code: ref <- data.frame(ID = 1, WT = 75, EASI = 27.5, ALB = 47)  typ_pop <- rbind(   transform(ref, ID = 1, WT =  40),   transform(ref, ID = 2, WT =  50),   transform(ref, ID = 3, WT =  75),   # reference   transform(ref, ID = 4, WT = 100),   transform(ref, ID = 5, WT = 150) )  typ_dose_days <- seq(0, 20 * 28, by = 28)  # 20 doses for steady state typ_dose <- typ_pop[rep(seq_len(nrow(typ_pop)), each = length(typ_dose_days)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(typ_dose_days, times = nrow(typ_pop)),     AMT  = 62.5,     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA   )  typ_obs_times <- seq(20 * 28, 24 * 28, by = 0.5)  # dense sampling across one SS interval typ_obs <- typ_pop[rep(seq_len(nrow(typ_pop)), each = length(typ_obs_times)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(typ_obs_times, times = nrow(typ_pop)),     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA   )  typ_ev <- bind_rows(typ_dose, typ_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, EASI, ALB)  typ_sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = typ_ev, omega = NA, sigma = NA) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  trapz_auc <- function(time, conc) {   sum(diff(time) * (head(conc, -1) + tail(conc, -1)) / 2) }  typ_auc <- as.data.frame(typ_sim) |>   filter(time >= 20 * 28) |>   group_by(id) |>   summarise(     WT  = WT[1],     AUC = trapz_auc(time, Cc),     .groups = "drop"   )  ref_auc <- typ_auc$AUC[typ_auc$WT == 75] typ_auc$pct_change <- 100 * (typ_auc$AUC - ref_auc) / ref_auc knitr::kable(   typ_auc,   digits  = c(0, 0, 2, 1),   caption = "Simulated AUC4W at steady state (62.5 mg Q4W) vs body weight" )

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: Total clearance typical AD patient grid free concentrations, decomposed linear TMDD (Michaelis-Menten) components, reproducing Figure 3.
code:
cc_grid    <- 10^seq(log10(0.01), log10(100), length.out = 200) tvcll      <- 0.115            # unit: L/day (TVCLL at 75 kg ref) e_easi_cl  <- 0.00111          # unit: L/day per EASI unit vmax_mgday <- 0.0362           # unit: mg/day (TVVM; label typo "ug/day" in Table S2) km_ugmL    <- 0.0783           # unit: ug/mL (equivalently mg/L)  cl_linear <- tvcll + e_easi_cl * 27.5                 # CL for 75 kg, EASI 27.5 cl_tmdd   <- vmax_mgday / (km_ugmL + cc_grid)         # unit: L/day (since VM is mg/day, KM+C in mg/L) cl_total  <- cl_linear + cl_tmdd  df_cl <- data.frame(   Cc_ugmL    = rep(cc_grid, 3),   clearance  = c(cl_linear + 0 * cc_grid, cl_tmdd, cl_total),   component  = factor(rep(c("Linear", "TMDD", "Total"), each = length(cc_grid)),                       levels = c("TMDD", "Linear", "Total")) )  ggplot(df_cl, aes(x = Cc_ugmL, y = clearance, colour = component, linetype = component)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 0.0469, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   annotate("text", x = 0.0469, y = 0.5, label = "LLOQ", hjust = -0.2, colour = "grey40") +   scale_x_log10() +   labs(     x        = "Free amlitelimab concentration (μg/mL)",     y        = "Clearance (L/day)",     colour   = "Component",     linetype = "Component",     title    = "Typical AD patient: linear vs TMDD clearance
(75 kg, EASI 27.5)"   ) +   theme_bw() cl_at_lloq <- tvcll + 0.00111 * 27.5 + vmax_mgday / (km_ugmL + 0.0469) tmdd_frac_at_lloq <- (vmax_mgday / (km_ugmL + 0.0469)) / cl_at_lloq cat(sprintf("TMDD fraction of total CL at LLOQ (0.0469 ug/mL): %.1f%%
",             100 * tmdd_frac_at_lloq)) #> TMDD fraction of total CL at LLOQ (0.0469 ug/mL): 66.5% cat(sprintf("TMDD fraction of total CL at 1 ug/mL: %.1f%%
",             100 * (vmax_mgday / (km_ugmL + 1)) / (tvcll + 0.00111 * 27.5 + vmax_mgday / (km_ugmL + 1)))) #> TMDD fraction of total CL at 1 ug/mL: 18.7%

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: paper reports mean terminal half-life 28 days linear PK range, individual range 24-43 days. Compute typical-value terminal half-life model’s eigenvalues population mean.
code: tvv1   <- 3.46 tvv2   <- 2.48 tvq    <- 0.569 tvcl   <- tvcll + e_easi_cl * 27.5   # linear CL at reference AD patient  k10 <- tvcl / tvv1 k12 <- tvq  / tvv1 k21 <- tvq  / tvv2  A <- k10 + k12 + k21 B <- k10 * k21 beta <- (A - sqrt(A * A - 4 * B)) / 2 t_half_day <- log(2) / beta cat(sprintf("Typical terminal half-life (linear range) = %.1f days\n", t_half_day)) #> Typical terminal half-life (linear range) = 29.6 days

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: Virtual population: 300 AD patients body weight, EASI, albumin drawn Tiraboschi 2025 Table S1 distributions, truncated study ranges. Regimen: STREAM-AD labelled adult dosing - 500 mg SC loading dose, 250 mg SC every 4 weeks week 24. IIV: Simulated using published omega^2 values - V1 CL correlated block (omega^2 V1 = 0.0491, cov = 0.024, omega^2 CL = 0.0482), plus diagonal V2 (0.0693), Fsc (1.18 logit scale), ALAG (0.151), Ka (0.135). Residual error: Proportional, CV 15.8%. Unit note: Table S2 labels TVVM “ug/day”; fact mg/day, verified paper’s claim TMDD represents approximately 66% total clearance LLOQ 0.0469 ug/mL approximately 20% 1 ug/mL. Figure 3 replication terminal half-life check reproduce fractions.

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: Tiraboschi JM, Zohar S, Quartino AL, Monnier R, Coulette V, Bizot JL, Jamois C. Population Pharmacokinetic Pharmacodynamic Modeling Prediction Extended Amlitelimab Phase 3 Dosing Regimen Atopic Dermatitis. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2025;14(12):2161-2173. doi:10.1002/psp4.70121

title: Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab
text: Citation: Valenzuela B, Neyens M, Zhu Y, Ramchandren S, Dosne AG, Leu JH, Faelens R, Ling LE, Perez-Ruixo JJ. Nipocalimab Dose Selection Generalized Myasthenia Gravis. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2025;14(12):2074-2085. doi:10.1002/psp4.70109 Description: Integrated PK/RO/IgG/MG-ADL QSS TMDD model nipocalimab healthy adults generalized myasthenia gravis (Valenzuela 2025) Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.70109 Supplement (PK/RO/IgG/MG-ADL model equations S4): https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/psp4.70109 Nipocalimab fully human IgG1 monoclonal antibody binds neonatal Fc receptor (FcRn) high affinity, blocks IgG recycling, consequently reduces circulating IgG (including pathogenic autoantibodies generalized myasthenia gravis, gMG). Valenzuela 2025 analysis pools data five phase 1 studies healthy adults phase 2 Vivacity-MG study (MOM-M281-004, NCT03772587) adults gMG build integrated PK (quasi-steady-state TMDD) / FcRn receptor occupancy / total serum IgG / MG-ADL model used select 15 mg/kg Q2W maintenance dose (30 mg/kg loading dose) pivotal phase 3 Vivacity-MG3 study.

title: Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab
text: Pooled analysis (N = 228): 160 healthy adult participants 68 adult participants gMG (Vivacity-MG / MOM-M281-004). Age 18–83 years (mean 41.3), body weight 45–188 kg (mean 75.9), 52.6 % female. Race distribution: 68 % White, 25 % Asian, 3.5 % Black, 3.5 % . Healthy participants received single IV doses 0.3–60 mg/kg weekly 15–30 mg/kg (studies MOM-M281-001, MOM-M281-007, MOM-M281-010, EDI1001, EDI1002). Vivacity-MG participants received 5–60 mg/kg IV Q2W Q4W 8 weeks, baseline MG-ADL ranging 6–24 points (mean 9.8). Full demographics appear Table 2 Valenzuela 2025. information available programmatically via model’s population metadata:
code: str(rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab"))$meta$population) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> List of 15 #>  $ n_subjects        : int 228 #>  $ n_studies         : int 6 #>  $ age_range         : chr "18-83 years" #>  $ age_median        : chr "41.3 years (mean; SD 16.2)" #>  $ weight_range      : chr "45-188 kg" #>  $ weight_median     : chr "75.9 kg (mean; SD 19.2)" #>  $ sex_female_pct    : num 52.6 #>  $ race_ethnicity    : Named num [1:4] 68 3.51 25 3.51 #>   ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:4] "White" "Black" "Asian" "Other" #>  $ disease_state     : chr "Pooled healthy adult participants (160) and adult participants with generalized myasthenia gravis (68, from Viv"| __truncated__ #>  $ dose_range        : chr "0.3-60 mg/kg single IV dose; 5-60 mg/kg IV Q4W or Q2W (gMG cohort)" #>  $ regions           : chr "Pooled phase 1 (healthy) and phase 2 (gMG) studies; populations include Japanese (study MOM-M281-010) and Chine"| __truncated__ #>  $ n_subjects_healthy: int 160 #>  $ n_subjects_gmg    : int 68 #>  $ mgadl_range_gmg   : chr "6-24 points (mean 9.8; Vivacity-MG baseline)" #>  $ notes             : chr "Demographics from Valenzuela 2025 Table 2. title: Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab
text: Original observed data publicly available. figures simulate representative gMG subject (Vivacity-MG / MOM-M281-004 population used paper Figure 4 Figure 3) plus representative healthy participant single-dose NCA check. paper Figures 3 4 depict typical-value predictions, zero -subject variability via rxode2::zeroRe().
code: MW_nipocalimab <- 142000  # g/mol, as used inside the model mg_per_kg      <- 15      # maintenance dose level wt_kg          <- 75      # reference body weight mgadl_bl       <- 9.8     # Vivacity-MG cohort mean baseline MG-ADL  dose_mg <- mg_per_kg * wt_kg load_mg <- 30 * wt_kg     # 30 mg/kg loading dose

title: Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab") mod_typical <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  # ---- Scenario 1: 15 mg/kg Q2W maintenance, no loading dose, gMG participant ---- q2w_times <- seq(0, 112, by = 14)  # dose every 14 days for 16 weeks obs_times <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 112, by = 0.5), q2w_times + 0.001)))  ev_q2w <- et(id = 1) for (td in q2w_times) {   ev_q2w <- et(ev_q2w, amt = dose_mg, cmt = "central", time = td, id = 1) } ev_q2w <- et(ev_q2w, time = obs_times, cmt = "Cc",                id = 1) ev_q2w <- et(ev_q2w, time = obs_times, cmt = "pctUnoccupiedFcRn", id = 1) ev_q2w <- et(ev_q2w, time = obs_times, cmt = "IgG_obs",           id = 1) ev_q2w <- et(ev_q2w, time = obs_times, cmt = "dMGADL",            id = 1)  icov_gmg <- data.frame(   id = 1, WT = wt_kg, ELISA = 0, PHASE1 = 0,   STUDY_M281_004 = 1, MGADL = mgadl_bl )  sim_q2w <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, ev_q2w, iCov = icov_gmg,                             returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalFcRn0', 'etalIgG0', 'etalkdeg_IgG', 'etalIgK', 'etaIDecplacebo', 'etaSIgG', 'etaSplacebo' sim_q2w <- sim_q2w[!duplicated(sim_q2w$time), ]  # ---- Scenario 2: 30 mg/kg loading + 15 mg/kg Q2W maintenance ---- load_times <- c(0, seq(14, 112, by = 14))  # loading at day 0 (30 mg/kg), then maintenance ev_load <- et(id = 1) ev_load <- et(ev_load, amt = load_mg, cmt = "central", time = 0, id = 1) for (td in load_times[-1]) {   ev_load <- et(ev_load, amt = dose_mg, cmt = "central", time = td, id = 1) } ev_load <- et(ev_load, time = obs_times, cmt = "Cc",                id = 1) ev_load <- et(ev_load, time = obs_times, cmt = "pctUnoccupiedFcRn", id = 1) ev_load <- et(ev_load, time = obs_times, cmt = "IgG_obs",           id = 1) ev_load <- et(ev_load, time = obs_times, cmt = "dMGADL",            id = 1)  sim_load <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, ev_load, iCov = icov_gmg,                              returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalFcRn0', 'etalIgG0', 'etalkdeg_IgG', 'etalIgK', 'etaIDecplacebo', 'etaSIgG', 'etaSplacebo' sim_load <- sim_load[!duplicated(sim_load$time), ]

what: Figure 3 / 4 — IgG change from baseline under 15 mg/kg Q2W, ± loading dose
title: Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab
text: Replicates IgG-reduction panels Figure 3 (maintenance-) Figure 3C (30 mg/kg loading) Valenzuela 2025. paper reports steady-state mean IgG CFB approximately −70 % 15 mg/kg Q2W.
code: baseline_igg_gmg <- 11.4 * 0.777  # IgG0 * FRIgG0_M281_004  igg_df <- bind_rows(   sim_q2w  |> transmute(time, IgG = total_IgG, regimen = "15 mg/kg Q2W"),   sim_load |> transmute(time, IgG = total_IgG, regimen = "30 mg/kg load -> 15 mg/kg Q2W") ) |>   mutate(IgG_CFB_pct = 100 * (IgG - baseline_igg_gmg) / baseline_igg_gmg)  ggplot(igg_df, aes(time, IgG_CFB_pct, colour = regimen)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   geom_hline(yintercept = -70, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (day)",     y = "Total serum IgG change from baseline (%)",     colour = NULL,     title = "Typical-value IgG reduction vs. time (Vivacity-MG subject, 75 kg)",     caption = paste("Dashed line = −70% target from Valenzuela 2025. Replicates",                     "Figure 3 (maintenance) and Figure 3C (with loading dose).")   ) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

what: Figure 4 — Integrated PK, FcRn occupancy, IgG, and MG-ADL over the Q2W dose interval
title: Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab
text: Replicates Figure 4 panels –D Valenzuela 2025 typical 75 kg gMG participant 15 mg/kg Q2W (without loading dose).
code: # Cc is already in ug/mL (model concentration unit). sim_plot <- sim_q2w |>   mutate(pctRO        = 100 - pctUnoccupiedFcRn,          IgG_CFB_pct  = 100 * (total_IgG - baseline_igg_gmg) / baseline_igg_gmg)  p_pk <- ggplot(sim_plot, aes(time, Cc)) +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Nipocalimab serum conc. (µg/mL)",        title = "Figure 4A — PK")  p_ro <- ggplot(sim_plot, aes(time, 100 - pctRO)) +   geom_line(colour = "seagreen", linewidth = 0.7) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Free FcRn (% of baseline)",        title = "Figure 4B — FcRn inhibition")  p_igg <- ggplot(sim_plot, aes(time, IgG_CFB_pct)) +   geom_line(colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.7) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "IgG change from baseline (%)",        title = "Figure 4C — IgG")  p_mgadl <- ggplot(sim_plot, aes(time, dMGADL)) +   geom_line(colour = "purple", linewidth = 0.7) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "MG-ADL change from baseline (points)",        title = "Figure 4D — MG-ADL")  # Stack with patchwork if available; otherwise print separately if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {   patchwork::wrap_plots(p_pk, p_ro, p_igg, p_mgadl, ncol = 2) } else {   print(p_pk); print(p_ro); print(p_igg); print(p_mgadl) }

title: Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab
text: NCA simulate single 15 mg/kg IV dose (healthy participant, ELISA assay, Phase 1 protocol) compute standard PK parameters. paper publish formal NCA table, perform self-consistency check: Cmax, Tmax, AUC, half-life track two-compartment linear elimination behavior nipocalimab within linear-CL range, target-mediated component fully engages. PKNCA summary single 15 mg/kg IV dose (healthy; typical-value simulation).
code: # Single-dose healthy adult scenario for NCA nca_times <- sort(unique(c(0, 0.04, 0.083, 0.25, 1, 2, 4, 8,                            seq(0.5, 28, by = 0.5)))) ev_sd <- et(id = 1) |>   et(amt = dose_mg, cmt = "central", time = 0, id = 1) |>   et(time = nca_times, cmt = "Cc", id = 1)  icov_hv <- data.frame(   id = 1, WT = wt_kg, ELISA = 1, PHASE1 = 1,   STUDY_M281_004 = 0, MGADL = 0 )  sim_sd <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, ev_sd, iCov = icov_hv,                            returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalFcRn0', 'etalIgG0', 'etalkdeg_IgG', 'etalIgK', 'etaIDecplacebo', 'etaSIgG', 'etaSplacebo'  # Cc is already in ug/mL (matches the paper's display unit). sim_nca <- sim_sd |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(     id        = 1L,     treatment = "15 mg/kg IV SD, healthy"   ) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_df <- data.frame(   id = 1, time = 0, amt = dose_mg,   treatment = "15 mg/kg IV SD, healthy" )  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start       = 0,   end         = Inf,   cmax        = TRUE,   tmax        = TRUE,   aucinf.obs  = TRUE,   half.life   = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) knitr::kable(as.data.frame(nca_res), digits = 2,              caption = "PKNCA summary for a single 15 mg/kg IV dose (healthy; typical-value simulation).")

title: Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab
text: Peak concentration Initial Cc 1125 mg bolus dose_mg / Vc × 1e6 / MW = 1125 / 3.23 × 1000 / 142 ≈ 348 µg/mL, matches simulated Cmax within rounding. Linear half-life linear-CL range, t½ = ln(2) · Vc / CL = 0.693 · 3.23 / 0.655 ≈ 3.4 days. PKNCA terminal half-life single-dose profile longer TMDD contributes elimination profile; high doses FcRn saturated, t½ approaches linear-CL value. IgG reduction SS (15 mg/kg Q2W) paper reports average CFB approximately −70 % steady state. typical-value simulation gives:
code: ss_mean_igg_cfb <- sim_q2w |>   filter(time >= 84, time <= 112) |>   summarise(mean_cfb_pct = mean(100 * (total_IgG - baseline_igg_gmg) / baseline_igg_gmg)) ss_mean_igg_cfb #>   mean_cfb_pct #> 1    -72.48007

title: Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab
text: Internal concentration units. model works µg/mL throughout, dimensionally consistent mg dosing L volumes (Cc = central / vc natively mg/L = µg/mL). FcRn0 (published nmol/L) PK additive RUVs (published nmol/L) converted µg/mL using nipocalimab MW = 142 kDa, derived Valenzuela 2025’s unit check (15 mg/kg × 75 kg = 1125 mg ≡ 7.92 µmol, page 2082). Kss stored paper’s reported unit (µg/mL) used directly. Assay selection. ELISA = 1 Phase 1 healthy-participant default (studies MOM-M281-001, MOM-M281-007, MOM-M281-010). ELISA = 0 PHASE1 = 0 STUDY_M281_004 = 1 Vivacity-MG gMG default. MG-ADL additive IIV encoding. Table 3 reports three values “IIV, CV %” column (1.89, 3.76, 0.125) MG-ADL additive etas. “CV %” header inherited log-normal rows; additive etas NONMEM OMEGA values reported directly squared parameter units. interpret variances encode −0.733 IIV correlation IDec_placebo S_IgG via -diagonal cov = −0.733 · √1.89 · √3.76 = −1.954. Flag authors confirm different convention. S_IgG unit conversion. Table 3 labels S_IgG “points per 10 % IgG reduction”, Supplement Equation 6 writes MG-ADL IgG term S_IgG × IgG_EC × (BL/7)^E_IgG effect-compartment output IgG_EC dimensionless fraction (0 baseline, 1 full blockade). reconcile, model multiplies S_IgG × effect 10 inside model() product dimensions points. Tested paper’s quoted −1.94 point reduction 70 % IgG CFB MG-ADL₀ = 9.8 (page 2083). Placebo effect gating. placebo component ΔMG-ADL activated t > 0; subject baseline observation negative time gets 0 placebo effect prediction, consistent Supplement Equation 6 (TSFD ≤ 0 branch). Healthy participants MG-ADL. MGADL = 0 healthy participants collapses MG-ADL response placebo slope × time term; healthy cohorts contribute MG-ADL observations paper’s analysis.

title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: Citation: Wade JR, Parker G, Kosutic G, Feagen BG, Sandborn WJ, Laveille C, Oliver R. Population pharmacokinetic analysis certolizumab pegol patients Crohn’s disease. J Clin Pharmacol. 2015;55(8):866-874. doi:10.1002/jcph.491 Description: One-compartment population PK model first-order SC absorption additive baseline concentration certolizumab pegol adults Crohn’s disease (Wade 2015) Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/jcph.491
code: #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: Wade 2015 pooled certolizumab pegol (CZP) PK data 2157 adults moderately--severely active Crohn’s disease across nine clinical studies (C87005, C87031, C87032, C87037, C87042, C87043, C87047, C87048, C87085). total dataset contained 13,561 CZP concentrations (median 6 samples per subject; range 1 17). Baseline demographics Wade 2015 Table 2: age range 16-80 years (median 35), body weight 31-151 kg (median 65), BMI 13-56 kg/m^2 (median 23), BSA 1.2-2.7 m^2 (median 1.8), 55.5% female. Race composition: 1964 White (91.1%), 89 Japanese (4.1%), 42 (1.9%), 29 Black (1.3%), 17 Indian (0.8%), 11 Asian (0.5%), 5 Hispanic (0.2%). Baseline laboratory medians: albumin 41 g/L, CRP 8 mg/L, lymphocyte count 1.5 x 10^9/L, CDAI 290. Immunosuppressant use baseline 889/2157 subjects (41.2%). Anti-CZP antibodies (ADA) detected 139 subjects (6.4%), contributing 270 ADA-positive concentrations (2.0% total observations). Dosing regimens pooled analysis included 100, 200, 400 mg SC Q2W Q4W. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Wade_2015_certolizumab")$population.

title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Wade_2015_certolizumab.R. table collects one place review. Reference covariate values typical-subject predictions: BSA = 1.76 m^2, ALB = 41 g/L, CRP = 8 mg/L, ADA-negative, race = reference (White / Indian Asian / Hispanic / ). paper reports derived typical half-lives use external validation targets: Absorption half-life: ~3.5 days (Results, “Final Population Pharmacokinetic Model” section). Elimination half-life ADA-negative patients: ~8 days (section). Elimination half-life ADA-positive patients: ~2 days (section).

title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: Individual observed data public. simulations build virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Wade 2015 Table 2.
code: make_cohort <- function(n,                         n_doses = 6,                         dosing_interval_days = 28,                         obs_days_per_dose = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 14, 21, 28),                         amt_mg = 400,                         seed = 20152015) {   set.seed(seed)    # Baseline covariates approximating Wade 2015 Table 2.   BSA <- pmax(1.2, pmin(2.7, rnorm(n, 1.8, 0.2)))   ALB <- pmax(17,  pmin(52,  rnorm(n, 41, 5)))   CRP <- pmax(0.1, pmin(286, exp(rnorm(n, log(8), 1.4))))    # Race distribution matches Table 2 percentages. RACE_JAPANESE, RACE_BLACK,   # and RACE_ASIAN are mutually exclusive; all others (White, Indian, Hispanic,   # Other) fall into the reference group (all three indicators = 0).   race <- sample(     c("reference", "japanese", "black", "asian"),     n, replace = TRUE,     prob = c((1964 + 17 + 5 + 42) / 2157,  # White + Indian + Hispanic + Other              89 / 2157,                    # Japanese              29 / 2157,                    # Black              11 / 2157)                    # Asian (excluding Japanese)   )   RACE_JAPANESE <- as.integer(race == "japanese")   RACE_BLACK    <- as.integer(race == "black")   RACE_ASIAN    <- as.integer(race == "asian")    # Primary cohort is ADA-negative throughout (reference population). An   # ADA-positive sensitivity arm is built separately below.   ADA_POS <- 0    dose_times <- seq(0, (n_doses - 1) * dosing_interval_days,                     by = dosing_interval_days)    pop <- data.frame(     ID = seq_len(n),     BSA, ALB, CRP,     RACE_JAPANESE, RACE_BLACK, RACE_ASIAN,     ADA_POS = ADA_POS   )    # Dose records (SC injections to depot)   d_dose <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(dose_times)), ] |>     dplyr::mutate(       TIME = rep(dose_times, times = n),       AMT  = amt_mg,       EVID = 1,       CMT  = "depot",       DV   = NA_real_     )    # Observation records (concentration sampling in central)   obs_grid <- sort(unique(as.vector(outer(obs_days_per_dose, dose_times, "+"))))   d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(obs_grid)), ] |>     dplyr::mutate(       TIME = rep(obs_grid, times = n),       AMT  = 0,       EVID = 0,       CMT  = "central",       DV   = NA_real_     )    dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>     dplyr::select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV,                   BSA, ALB, CRP,                   RACE_JAPANESE, RACE_BLACK, RACE_ASIAN, ADA_POS) } mod <- rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Wade_2015_certolizumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: primary simulation uses approved Crohn’s maintenance regimen CZP: 400 mg SC Q4W 6 doses (~24 weeks), dense sampling dosing interval. ADA-negative patients reference population.
code: events_vpc <- make_cohort(n = 300) sim_vpc <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_vpc) |> as.data.frame()

title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: Wade 2015 Figure 1 shows CZP concentration versus time dose across studies (linear scale). reproduce shape 400 mg SC Q4W.
code: d_vpc <- sim_vpc |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(d_vpc, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "#4682b4", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q05), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q95), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 168, by = 28),                      labels = seq(0, 168, by = 28) / 7) +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y = expression("CZP concentration (" * mu * "g/mL)"),     title = "Figure 1 analogue - simulated CZP concentration VPC",     caption = paste0(       "Simulated 5th/50th/95th percentiles, N = 300 virtual subjects, ",       "400 mg Q4W SC x 6 doses.\n",       "Replicates the shape of Figure 1 of Wade 2015."     )   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 2 analogue: covariate effects on CL/F
title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: Wade 2015 Figure 2 shows nonlinear estimated relationships albumin (bottom) CRP (top) CL/F, derived final model’s theta estimates. reproduce relationships using exact covariate forms model file.  CL/F values 5th/95th percentiles covariate ranges match values reported Results section Wade 2015: Albumin: CL/F falls ~1.05 L/day (low albumin, ~32 g/L) ~0.61 L/day (high albumin, ~47 g/L); paper reports 1.05 0.613 L/day. CRP: CL/F rises ~0.57 L/day (low CRP, ~1 mg/L) ~0.71 L/day (high CRP, ~79 mg/L); paper reports 0.574 0.712 L/day.
code: # Apply the same piecewise-linear form the model uses, at the ADA-negative # reference subject (BSA = 1.76, so CL is the baseline CL/F = 0.685 L/day). cl_ref  <- 0.685 alb_grid <- seq(17, 52, length.out = 201) crp_grid <- seq(0.1, 100, length.out = 201)  cl_alb <- cl_ref * ifelse(   alb_grid <= 41,   1 + -0.0598 * (alb_grid - 41),   1 + -0.0177 * (alb_grid - 41) )  cl_crp <- cl_ref * ifelse(   crp_grid <= 8,   1 + 0.0205 * (crp_grid - 8),   1 + 0.000561 * (crp_grid - 8) )  p_alb <- ggplot(data.frame(alb = alb_grid, cl = cl_alb),                 aes(x = alb, y = cl)) +   geom_line(colour = "#b00000", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 41, linetype = "dotted") +   labs(x = "Albumin (g/L)", y = "CL/F (L/day)",        title = "Albumin effect on CL/F (ADA-negative reference)") +   theme_bw()  p_crp <- ggplot(data.frame(crp = crp_grid, cl = cl_crp),                 aes(x = crp, y = cl)) +   geom_line(colour = "#b00000", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 8, linetype = "dotted") +   labs(x = "CRP (mg/L)", y = "CL/F (L/day)",        title = "CRP effect on CL/F (ADA-negative reference)") +   theme_bw()  if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {   patchwork::wrap_plots(p_crp, p_alb, ncol = 1) } else {   print(p_crp)   print(p_alb) }

what: Figure 3 analogue: ADA-negative vs ADA-positive VPC
title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: Wade 2015 Figure 3 contrasts VPCs ADA-negative (left) ADA-positive (right) subjects. reproduce typical ADA-negative vs ADA-positive trajectories 400 mg SC Q4W regimen.
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  # Reference subject dosing 400 mg Q4W for 6 doses, sampled every day. ev_ref <- make_cohort(n = 1, obs_days_per_dose = seq(0, 28, by = 1)) |>   dplyr::mutate(BSA = 1.76, ALB = 41, CRP = 8,                 RACE_JAPANESE = 0, RACE_BLACK = 0, RACE_ASIAN = 0,                 ADA_POS = 0)  ev_adap <- ev_ref |> dplyr::mutate(ADA_POS = 1)  sim_ref  <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_ref)  |>   as.data.frame() |> dplyr::mutate(arm = "ADA-negative") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalbaseline' sim_adap <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_adap) |>   as.data.frame() |> dplyr::mutate(arm = "ADA-positive (all samples)") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalbaseline'  bind_rows(sim_ref, sim_adap) |>   ggplot(aes(x = time, y = Cc, colour = arm)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 168, by = 28),                      labels = seq(0, 168, by = 28) / 7) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(`ADA-negative` = "#2c7fb8",                                  `ADA-positive (all samples)` = "#d95f0e")) +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y = expression("Typical Cc (" * mu * "g/mL)"),     colour = NULL,     title = "Figure 3 analogue - ADA-negative vs ADA-positive typical subject",     caption = paste0(       "Typical subject (BSA = 1.76 m^2, ALB = 41 g/L, CRP = 8 mg/L, reference race).\n",       "ADA-positive arm sets ADA_POS = 1 at every observation (worst-case sustained positivity)."     )   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "top")

what: Figure S1 analogue: BSA effect at steady state
title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: Wade 2015 Figure S1 shows influence BSA steady-state CZP concentrations 400 mg Q4W, low (1.19 m^2) high (2.67 m^2) BSA values ADA-negative subjects. reproduce typical-subject profiles.
code: mk_bsa <- function(bsa_value) {   make_cohort(n = 1, obs_days_per_dose = seq(0, 28, by = 1)) |>     dplyr::mutate(BSA = bsa_value, ALB = 41, CRP = 8,                   RACE_JAPANESE = 0, RACE_BLACK = 0, RACE_ASIAN = 0,                   ADA_POS = 0) }  sims_bsa <- dplyr::bind_rows(   rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = mk_bsa(1.19)) |>     as.data.frame() |> dplyr::mutate(arm = "BSA = 1.19 m^2"),   rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = mk_bsa(1.76)) |>     as.data.frame() |> dplyr::mutate(arm = "BSA = 1.76 m^2 (typical)"),   rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = mk_bsa(2.67)) |>     as.data.frame() |> dplyr::mutate(arm = "BSA = 2.67 m^2") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalbaseline' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalbaseline' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalbaseline'  ggplot(sims_bsa, aes(x = time, y = Cc, colour = arm)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 168, by = 28),                      labels = seq(0, 168, by = 28) / 7) +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y = expression("Typical Cc (" * mu * "g/mL)"),     colour = NULL,     title = "Figure S1 analogue - BSA effect on steady-state CZP",     caption = "Typical ADA-negative subject, 400 mg Q4W SC."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "top")

title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: Compute NCA simulated typical-value profiles reference patient (BSA = 1.76 m^2, ALB = 41 g/L, CRP = 8 mg/L, reference race), ADA-negative arm ADA-positive arm. quantities interest absorption elimination half-lives, Wade 2015 reports Results text: Absorption half-life: ~3.5 days (independent ADA status). Elimination half-life, ADA-negative: ~8 days. Elimination half-life, ADA-positive: ~2 days. Single-dose NCA typical ADA-negative patient. Single-dose NCA typical ADA-positive patient.
code: # Single 400 mg SC dose, dense sampling out to ~56 days (7 ADA-negative # half-lives) for a clean terminal-phase characterization. ev_single_neg <- make_cohort(   n = 1, n_doses = 1,   obs_days_per_dose = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 14, 21,                         28, 35, 42, 49, 56) ) |>   dplyr::mutate(BSA = 1.76, ALB = 41, CRP = 8,                 RACE_JAPANESE = 0, RACE_BLACK = 0, RACE_ASIAN = 0,                 ADA_POS = 0)  sim_single_neg <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_single_neg) |>   as.data.frame() |>   dplyr::mutate(id = 1L, treatment = "single_400mg_ADAneg") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalbaseline'  sim_nca_neg <- sim_single_neg |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_neg <- ev_single_neg |>   dplyr::filter(EVID == 1) |>   dplyr::transmute(id = ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT,                    treatment = "single_400mg_ADAneg")  conc_neg <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca_neg, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_neg_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_neg, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_neg <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = Inf,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_neg <- PKNCA::pk.nca(   PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_neg, dose_neg_obj, intervals = intervals_neg) ) knitr::kable(as.data.frame(nca_neg$result),              caption = "Single-dose NCA on the typical ADA-negative patient.") # Same single 400 mg SC dose, but ADA-positive at every observation. Dense # early sampling because the ADA-positive half-life is ~2 days. ev_single_pos <- make_cohort(   n = 1, n_doses = 1,   obs_days_per_dose = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 14, 21, 28) ) |>   dplyr::mutate(BSA = 1.76, ALB = 41, CRP = 8,                 RACE_JAPANESE = 0, RACE_BLACK = 0, RACE_ASIAN = 0,                 ADA_POS = 1)  sim_single_pos <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_single_pos) |>   as.data.frame() |>   dplyr::mutate(id = 1L, treatment = "single_400mg_ADApos") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalbaseline'  sim_nca_pos <- sim_single_pos |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_pos <- ev_single_pos |>   dplyr::filter(EVID == 1) |>   dplyr::transmute(id = ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT,                    treatment = "single_400mg_ADApos")  conc_pos <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca_pos, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_pos_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_pos, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  nca_pos <- PKNCA::pk.nca(   PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_pos, dose_pos_obj, intervals = intervals_neg) ) knitr::kable(as.data.frame(nca_pos$result),              caption = "Single-dose NCA on the typical ADA-positive patient.")

title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: Half-lives packaged model vs. values reported Wade 2015 Results. analytical half-lives derived packaged theta estimates match values reported Results text Wade 2015 within one decimal place (absorption 3.47 vs ~3.5 days; ADA-negative elimination 7.70 vs ~8 days; ADA-positive elimination 1.93 vs ~2 days). PKNCA half.life estimates simulated profiles may differ slightly terminal-phase log-linear regression picks curvature absorption phase sampling window extend far enough past Cmax.
code: get_param <- function(res, ppname) {   tbl <- as.data.frame(res$result)   val <- tbl$PPORRES[tbl$PPTESTCD == ppname]   if (length(val) == 0) return(NA_real_)   val[1] }  hl_neg_sim <- get_param(nca_neg, "half.life") hl_pos_sim <- get_param(nca_pos, "half.life")  # Model-implied analytical values for comparison. hl_neg_theory <- log(2) / (0.685 / 7.61)     # ADA-negative KEL = CL/V hl_pos_theory <- log(2) / (2.74  / 7.61)     # ADA-positive KEL hl_abs_theory <- log(2) / 0.200              # Absorption  comparison <- data.frame(   Quantity = c(     "Absorption half-life (days)",     "Elimination half-life, ADA-negative (days)",     "Elimination half-life, ADA-positive (days)"   ),   Published = c("~3.5", "~8", "~2"),   Analytical = c(round(hl_abs_theory, 2),                  round(hl_neg_theory, 2),                  round(hl_pos_theory, 2)),   NCA_simulated = c(     "n/a (absorption phase)",     as.character(round(hl_neg_sim, 2)),     as.character(round(hl_pos_sim, 2))   ) ) knitr::kable(   comparison,   caption = paste0(     "Half-lives from the packaged model vs. the values reported in Wade 2015 ",     "Results."   ) )

title: Wade_2015_certolizumab
text: Stratified IIV simplified. Wade 2015 reports separate IIV estimates CL/F baseline conditional ADA status (CL/F: ADA-negative 27.5% CV, ADA-positive 83.6% CV; baseline: ADA-negative 95.1% CV, ADA-positive 72.3% CV). nlmixr2 uses single eta distribution per parameter. packaged model uses ADA-negative IIVs, represent reference population (93.6% subjects, 98.0% observations). ADA-positive IIVs documented etalcl / etalbaseline -file comments users want simulate worst-case ADA-positive arm. Time-varying ADA status. ADA_POS supplied per observation event dataset (Wade 2015 coded ATB concentration level, per subject). primary virtual cohort ADA-negative every time point; ADA-positive comparison arm sets ADA_POS = 1 every observation show maximum model-predicted effect. Real applications supply observed per-sample ATB status. Baseline parameter implemented additive offset. Wade 2015 states Baseline nuisance parameter captures predose concentrations arising either residual drug previous anti-TNF therapy (assay cross-reacts infliximab, adalimumab, golimumab, etanercept) endogenous anti-TNF antibodies. packaged model adds baseline directly Cc, matches paper’s text explicitly documented NM-TRAN expression Table 3. merged White (1) pooled reference group; Japanese (8), Black (2), Asian (3) V/F effect. packaged model exposes three binary indicators (RACE_JAPANESE, RACE_BLACK, RACE_ASIAN); pooled reference group three indicators = 0. RACE_ASIAN model excludes Japanese (Wade 2015’s coding two separate categories); documented covariate register covariateData[[RACE_ASIAN]]$notes. Virtual covariate distributions. Exact baseline distributions published. BSA simulated normal(1.8, 0.2) truncated [1.2, 2.7] m^2; ALB normal(41, 5) truncated [17, 52] g/L; CRP log-normal median 8 mg/L log-SD 1.4 truncated 286 mg/L match Table 2 ranges. Race fractions drawn Table 2 counts. SC-dosing. Wade 2015 data subcutaneous. packaged model uses depot SC dose treats structural parameters apparent (CL/F V/F), bioavailability implicit separately estimated.

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
text: Citation: Wang B, Lau YY, Liang M, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Pharmacodynamics Benralizumab Healthy Volunteers Patients Asthma. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2017;6(4):249-257. doi:10.1002/psp4.12160 Description: Two compartment PK model benralizumab (anti-IL-5Ralpha) healthy volunteers patients asthma (Wang 2017) Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12160

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
text: Simulate benralizumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Wang et al. (2017) healthy volunteers patients asthma (N = 200 across 6 Phase /II studies). Benralizumab humanized, afucosylated IgG1 monoclonal antibody targeting IL-5 receptor alpha. model 2-compartment model first-order SC absorption, allometric weight scaling (CL exponent fixed 0.75), covariate effects high-titer anti-drug antibodies (ADA, titer >= 400) CL Japanese healthy volunteer status Vc. Source: Table 3 Wang et al. (2017) CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 6(4):249-257. doi:10.1002/psp4.12160. Parameters verified PMC5397562.

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
code: set.seed(2017) n_subj <- 500  pop <- data.frame(   ID          = seq_len(n_subj),   WT          = rlnorm(n_subj, log(77), 0.24),  # Mean ~77 kg (Table 2)   ADA         = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.05),         # ~5% high-titer ADA   JAPANESE_HV = 0                                 # Non-Japanese patient population )

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
text: Approved asthma regimen: 30 mg SC every 4 weeks first 3 doses, every 8 weeks. Simulate 30 weeks.
code: dose_times <- c(0, 28, 56, 112, 168)  # days (weeks 0, 4, 8, 16, 24)  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 7, by = 0.5),   seq(7, 210, by = 3.5) )))  d_dose <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = 30, EVID = 1, CMT = 1, DV = NA_real_)  d_obs <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 2, DV = NA_real_)  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Wang_2017_benralizumab") sim <- rxSolve(mod, d_sim, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
code: sim_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "darkorange", linewidth = 1) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Benralizumab concentration (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated benralizumab PK (30 mg SC, q4w x3 then q8w)",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500 virtual asthma patients)",     caption = "Model: Wang et al. (2017) CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
text: NCA summary (2nd dosing interval, weeks 4-8)
code: # Use 2nd dosing interval (weeks 4-8) sim_df <- as.data.frame(sim) # Build a unique subject key: use sim.id (replicate) and id (original subject) # when both are available; otherwise fall back to whichever exists if (all(c("sim.id", "id") %in% names(sim_df))) {   sim_df$subject <- paste(sim_df$sim.id, sim_df$id, sep = "_") } else if ("id" %in% names(sim_df)) {   sim_df$subject <- sim_df$id } else {   sim_df$subject <- sim_df$sim.id } nca_data <- data.frame(   subject  = sim_df$subject,   time_rel = sim_df$time - 28,   Cc       = sim_df$Cc ) nca_data <- nca_data[nca_data$time_rel >= 0 & nca_data$time_rel <= 28 & nca_data$Cc > 0, ]  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_data, Cc ~ time_rel | subject) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(   data.frame(subject = unique(nca_data$subject), time_rel = 0, AMT = 30),   AMT ~ time_rel | subject ) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj,                        intervals = data.frame(start = 0, end = 28,                                               cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                                               auclast = TRUE, half.life = TRUE)) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■                      38% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■       85% |  ETA:  1s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(nca_summary, digits = 2,              caption = "NCA summary (2nd dosing interval, weeks 4-8)")

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
text: Model: 2-compartment SC linear elimination. TMDD. Bioavailability: 52.6% SC (estimated, fixed). Allometry: CL exponent fixed 0.75 (standard); Vc Vp exponents estimated 0.651 0.576 respectively. ADA effect: High-titer ADA (>= 400) causes exp(1.52) = 4.57-fold increase CL (Eq. 6 paper; Table 3 footnote c “natural exponent”). ADA time-varying (assessed sampling visit). Japanese race: 1.34-fold larger Vc Japanese healthy volunteers (multiplicative factor; may confound healthy volunteer status). IIV 6 PK parameters (CL, Vc, Q, Vp, ka, F). Reference weight: 77 kg (population mean; paper states “normalized population median” exact median explicitly stated).

title: Xie_2019_agomelatine
text: Citation: Xie F, Vermeulen , Colin P, Cheng Z. semiphysiological population pharmacokinetic model agomelatine metabolites Chinese healthy volunteers. what: Recreate figure 4 from the original paper
title: Xie_2019_agomelatine
text: dose single 25 mg dose based methods section reference.    figure BQL 3-hydroxy-agomelatine original paper. added completeness.
code:
Xie_2019_agomelatine <- nlmixr(readModelDb("Xie_2019_agomelatine")) #> Warning: some etas defaulted to non-mu referenced, possible parsing error: e.IOV1, eta17, eta18, eta19, e.IOV2, eta21, eta22, eta23, e.IOV3, eta25, eta26, eta27, e.IOV4, eta29, eta30, eta31, e.IOV5, eta33, eta34, eta35 #> as a work-around try putting the mu-referenced expression on a simple line  d_sim_dosing <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     EVID = 1,     AMT = 25, # mg     TIME = 0,     CMT = c("DEPOT1", "DEPOT2")   ) d_sim_obs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     EVID = 0,     AMT = 0,     TIME = seq(0, 8, by = 0.1),     CMT = "lcalmt"   ) d_sim_prep <- rbind(d_sim_dosing, d_sim_obs) d_sim_prep$WT <- 60 # kg, based on table 1 from the paper d_sim_prep$ooc1 <- 1 d_sim_prep$ooc2 <-   d_sim_prep$ooc3 <-   d_sim_prep$ooc4 <- 0 d_sim_pop <- nlmixr2(Xie_2019_agomelatine, data = d_sim_prep, est = "rxSolve", control = list(nStud = 1000)) #> unhandled error message: EE:[lsoda] 70000 steps taken before reaching tout #>  @(lsoda.c:748 #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): some ID(s) could not solve the ODEs correctly; #> These values are replaced with 'NA' d_plot <-   d_sim_pop |>   group_by(time) |>   summarize(     Q05_calmt = quantile(calmt, probs = 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50_calmt = quantile(calmt, probs = 0.5, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95_calmt = quantile(calmt, probs = 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     prob_blq_calmt = sum(calmt < 0.046, na.rm = TRUE) / n(),      Q05_c3oh = quantile(c3oh, probs = 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50_c3oh = quantile(c3oh, probs = 0.5, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95_c3oh = quantile(c3oh, probs = 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     prob_blq_c3oh = sum(c3oh < 0.460, na.rm = TRUE) / n(),      Q05_c7dm = quantile(c7dm, probs = 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50_c7dm = quantile(c7dm, probs = 0.5, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95_c7dm = quantile(c7dm, probs = 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     prob_blq_c7dm = sum(c7dm < 0.137, na.rm = TRUE) / n()   ) |>   ungroup() ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_calmt, ymin = Q05_calmt, ymax = Q95_calmt)) +   geom_ribbon(colour = NA, linetype = "63", fill = "gray", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.046, linetype = "63", colour = "gray") +   scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.046, labels = "LLOQ")) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = "Plasma agomelatine concentration (ng/mL)
Median and 90% prediction intervals"   ) #> Warning in scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.046, : log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = prob_blq_calmt)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = "Probability of BLQ plasma agomelatine concentration"   ) ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_c3oh, ymin = Q05_c3oh, ymax = Q95_c3oh)) +   geom_ribbon(colour = NA, linetype = "63", fill = "gray", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.460, linetype = "63", colour = "gray") +   scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.460, labels = "LLOQ")) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = "Plasma 3-hydroxy-agomelatine concentration (ng/mL)
Median and 90% prediction intervals"   ) #> Warning in scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.46, : log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = prob_blq_c3oh)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = "Probability of BLQ plasma 3-hydroxy-agomelatine concentration"   ) ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_c7dm, ymin = Q05_c7dm, ymax = Q95_c7dm)) +   geom_ribbon(colour = NA, linetype = "63", fill = "gray", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.137, linetype = "63", colour = "gray") +   scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.137, labels = "LLOQ")) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = "Plasma 7-desmethyl-agomelatine concentration (ng/mL)
Median and 90% prediction intervals"   ) #> Warning in scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.137, : log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = prob_blq_c7dm)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = "Probability of BLQ plasma 7-desmethyl-agomelatine concentration"   )
